items.name: Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only; to and from Akashic Record and the Universe only)

description.value:
Requirements You have a planar key for the destination plane, used as a locus

You and your allies traverse the barriers between planes of existence. The targets move to another plane, such as the Plane of Fire, the Netherworld, or the Outer Rifts. You must know the destination plane exists and use a magic planar key created from material from that plane as a locus for the spell. While the planar keys for most prominent planes are uncommon, just like the spell interplanar teleport, more obscure planes and demiplanes often have rare or possibly even unique planar keys.

The spell is highly imprecise, and you appear 1d20×25 miles from the last place one of the targets (of your choice) was located the last time that target traveled to the plane. If it's the first time traveling to a particular plane for all targets, you appear at a random location on the plane. Interplanar teleport doesn't provide a means of return travel, though casting interplanar teleport again allows you to return to your previous plane unless there are extenuating circumstances.

You create an @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} floating eye at a location within range (even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect). The eye can't move, but you can see in all directions from that point as if using your normal visual senses.

You befuddle your target with strange impulses, causing it to act randomly. The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target babbles incoherently and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.

Critical Failure The target is Confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You create an @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} floating ear at a location within range (even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect). It can't move, but you can hear through the ear as if using your normal auditory senses.

Magic empowers the target to act faster. It gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} condition and can use the extra action each round only for Strike and Stride actions.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 6 creatures.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.ElkXovNrHB0Doi6O]{Spell Effect: Haste}

You rapidly catalog and collate information relevant to your current situation. You can instantly use up to 6 Recall Knowledge actions as part of Casting this Spell. For these actions, you can't use any special abilities, reactions, or free actions that trigger when you Recall Knowledge.

You transform your appearance to that of a Small or Medium humanoid, such as a dwarf, elf, goblin, halfling, human, or orc. You gain the humanoid trait in addition to your other traits while in this form, as well as any trait related to the creature's kind (such as goblin or human). If this transformation reduces your size, it reduces your reach accordingly (typically to 5 feet). This transformation doesn't change your statistics in any way, and you don't gain any special abilities of the humanoid form you assume. You can still wear and use your gear, which changes size (if necessary) to match your new form. If items leave your person, they return to their usual size.

Humanoid form grants you a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to pass as a generic member of the chosen ancestry, and you add your level even if you're untrained, but you can't make yourself look like a specific person. If you want to Impersonate an individual, you still need to create a disguise, though the GM won't factor in the difference in ancestry when determining the DC of your Deception check. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.B9Hzt4ki8eqlXxyh]{Spell Effect: Humanoid Form}

Heightened (3rd) You gain darkvision or low-light vision if the form you assume has that ability.

Heightened (5th) You can take on the appearance of a Large humanoid. If this increases your size, you gain the effects of the enlarge spell.

You dull the target's mind, depending on its Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} for 1 minute.

The target can understand the meaning of a single language it is hearing or reading when you cast the spell. This doesn't let it understand codes, language couched in metaphor, and the like (subject to GM discretion). If the target can hear multiple languages and knows that, it can choose which language to understand; otherwise, choose one of the languages randomly.

Heightened (3rd) The target can also speak the language.

Heightened (4th) You can target up to 10 creatures, and targets can also speak the language.

You execute a psychic search of the Akashic Record for information about a specific topic and magically transfer this data to your comm unit or computer. When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you're trained that has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} action.

Any time during the duration, you can consult your comm unit while attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This check gains a +1 status bonus. Checking your comm unit is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must be holding the comm unit to do so. The comm unit instantly compiles and displays snippets and selections from various media relevant to the topic, which grants you the bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the check is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference the comm unit, the spell ends. When the spell ends, your access to the Akashic Record ends, and all the information you downloaded returns to the Akashic Record.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.h1i8c47U0IOrMrHJ]{Spell Effect: Akashic Download}

Heightened (3rd) You can reference your comm unit three times before the spell ends.

system.requirements: You have a comm unit or computer, used as a locus.

description.value:
You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

target.value: 1 dataset or tech item with the tracking trait

description.value:
You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You alter how an item's or spell's magical aura appears to effects like detect magic. You can hide the auras entirely, have an item register as a common item of lower level, or make a spell register as a common spell of the same or lower rank. You can Dismiss the spell. A caster using @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.gpzpAAAJ1Lza2JVl]{Detect Magic} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.OhD2Z6rIGGD5ocZA]{Read Aura} of a higher rank than disguise magic can attempt to disbelieve the illusion using the skill matching the tradition of the spell (Arcana for arcane, Religion for divine, Occultism for occult, or Nature for primal). Further attempts by the same caster get the same result as the initial check to disbelieve.

Heightened (2nd) You can Cast this Spell on a creature, disguising all items and spell effects on it.

You focus on the target object, opening your mind to perceive magical auras. When the casting is complete, you know whether that item is magical. You or anyone you advise about the aura gains a +2 circumstance bonus to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.eReSHVEPCsdkSL4G]{Identify Magic} on the item. If the object is illusory, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your read aura spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.J6OhRNSN23KzOPRL]{Spell Effect: Read Aura}

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to 10 objects.

Heightened (6th) You can target any number of objects.

You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
The next time you make an attack roll before the end of your turn, roll it twice and use the better result. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}. You are then temporarily immune to sure strike for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.fpGDAz2v5PG0zUSl]{Spell Effect: Sure Strike}

duration.value: until the end of your turn

description.value: Instead of using their own memories, an Akashic dragon can ascertain information from the Akashic Records. This gives it the Akashic Record Lore skill, which it can use to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} of any kind.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AllAroundVision]

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. occult

description.value:
90 feet. The swirl of constant uploads and downloads near the Akashic dragon make attempts to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} unpredictable. A creature other than an akashic dragon that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's Akashic interference for 1 minute.

Critical Success For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it gains a +3 status bonus to its check.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it takes a –3 status penalty to its check.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.

Trigger The dragon is critically hit by an attack

Effect The young Akashic dragon raises a web of deflecting lasers using the eyes embedded all over their body, gaining resistance 8 to all damage against the triggering attack and absorbing some of the blow's energy, recharging their Akashic Barrage. If the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check against the creature in the last minute, they gain resistance 11 instead.
The Akashic dragon can activate magic and tech items with the Cast a Spell activation of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells.

The dragon condenses cutting truths and fiery revelations from the Akashic Record into sizzling laser fire that they project as a cone from their primary eye to assail their foes. This deals @Damage[10d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). If a creature critically fails its Reflex save against Akashic Barrage and the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about the creature in the last minute, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round. The dragon can't use Akashic Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Akashic Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.

The first time each encounter the Akashic dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if they'd succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about that creature.

details.publicNotes: Though rarely encountered outside their crumbling, relic-filled lairs, Akashic dragons occasionally live in hubs of knowledge, such as bustling space stations or cities. These dragons, bestowed with a direct connection to the Akashic Record, are arrogant curators of knowledge, assuming (often correctly) that no other being could possibly have a deeper understanding of the universe's secrets. Akashic dragons spend extensive time in the Akashic Record, often emerging after what they perceive as many days while mere hours have passed for the rest of the galaxy.

description.value:
You shine a blazing ray of light tinged with holy energy. Make a ranged spell attack. The ray deals 5d6 fire damage. If the target has the unholy trait, you deal an extra 5d6 spirit damage.

Critical Success The target takes double damage.

Success The target takes full damage.

If the light passes through an area of magical darkness or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, holy light attempts to counteract the darkness. If you need to determine whether the light passes through an area of darkness, draw a line between yourself and the spell's target.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 2d6, and the spirit damage against unholy creatures increases by 2d6.

You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a counteract check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, the item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an artifact or similar item, you automatically fail.

target.value: 1 spell effect or unattended magic item

description.value:
A cacophonous noise blasts out, dealing 2d10 sonic damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is deafened for 1 minute, and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d10.

A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.con2Hzt47JjpuUej]{Spell Effect: Resist Energy}

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

Blessings from beyond help your companions strike true. You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls while within the emanation. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet.

Bless can counteract @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.7ZinJNzxq0XF0oMx]{Bane}.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.RfCEHpMoEAZvB9IZ]{Aura: Bless}

target.value: you and allies in the area

description.value:
You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

items.name: Diabolic Pact (DC 22, Doesn't Require Secondary Casters)

description.value:
You make an appeal to a powerful devil, asking them to bind some of their subordinates to your service. If you succeed, the devil sends you their choice of one devil of level 2 or lower, two devils of level 0 or lower, or three devils of level –1 or lower.

Critical Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Weeks]]{1d4 weeks}.

Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Days]]{1d4 days}.

Failure Your request is denied.

Critical Failure Not only is your request denied, but the powerful devil sends word of its displeasure to your master.

Heightened (+1) Increase the level of devil sent to you by 2 for each option.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be a devil)

description.value:
You sap the target's strength, depending on its Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} for 1 minute.

You channel void energy to harm the living or heal the undead. If the target is a living creature, you deal 1d8 void damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. If the target is a willing undead creature, you restore that amount of Hit Points. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing an undead creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse void energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the area.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

target.value: 1 living creature or 1 willing undead creature

description.value:
You channel vital energy to heal the living or damage the undead. If the target is a willing living creature, you restore 1d8 Hit Points. If the target is undead, you deal that amount of vitality damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing a living creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse vital energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the burst.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

You channel vital energy to heal the living or damage the undead. If the target is a willing living creature, you restore 1d8 Hit Points. If the target is undead, you deal that amount of vitality damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing a living creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse vital energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the burst.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

You snap your fingers and point at a target, which begins to smolder. Make a spell attack roll against the target's AC, dealing 2d4 fire damage on a hit. If the target is within your melee reach, you can choose to make a melee spell attack with the flame instead of a ranged spell attack, which increases all the spell's damage dice to d6s.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and 1d4 persistent fire damage.

Success The target takes full damage.

Heightened (+1) The initial damage increases by 1d4 and the persistent fire damage on a critical hit increases by 1d4.

@Damage[(@item.rank)d4[persistent,fire]]{Scaling Persistent Fire Damage}

@Damage[(@item.rank)d6[persistent,fire]]{Scaling Persistent Fire Damage (Melee)}

You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.cVVZXNbV0nElVOPZ]{Spell Effect: Light}

Heightened (4th) The orb sheds light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light for the next 60 feet).

A spinning disk or smooth orb of shimmering steel leaps from one target to another. You deal 2d4 bludgeoning or slashing damage (with a basic Reflex save).

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
You summon a Tiny bouncing wisp of light that easily distracts even the most determined of combatants. The wisp floats near your target. A wisp doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would. The wisp generates bright light in a 10-foot radius. When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the spell, your wisp attempts to distract your target, which must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. A creature who critically succeeds becomes immune to wisp ally for 1 hour.

Heightened (+1) You create one additional wisp, allowing you to choose a different target. Each wisp you create must have a different target, and all of them attempt to distract their targets at the same time when you cast or Sustain this spell.

Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Upgrades: 1

Tempweave suits are reinforced hooded garments woven with thermal fabric and venting wires, popularly worn for trips to humid climates like the jungles of Castrovel or Pulonis.

Boosters attached to your armor supercharge your leap.

Activate—Jump 1 (manipulate)

Frequency once per round

Effect You activate the jump jets to get a quick boost. You Fly up to 20 feet with a maximum height of 10 feet, or you Fly up to 20 feet straight up. You must land at the end of this movement or fall. Jump jets can't lift you if you're encumbered.

10 feet. Magical wards cause a signifer's surrounding foes to catch flame. An enemy creature that starts its turn in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage. A signifer can activate and deactivate this aura as a single action that has the concentrate trait. Regardless of the result of the save, the creature is immune to the aura for 1 minute.

Frequency once per round

Effect The signifer accuses a target within 60 feet of misdeeds, then attempts an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] them. As long as the target is frightened due to Proclamation of Guilt, it's also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed}.
If the next action the signifer uses is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet.

details.publicNotes:
A Hellknight who displays prowess, scholarly discipline, and magical acumen might attain the rank of signifer.

Hellknights are infernal knights, and while they once ruled entire nations on Lost Golarion, the Hellknights of the Pact Worlds enforce law and order more holistically, traveling in massive citadel starships to spread their influence rather than remaining in any one place. In battle, Hellknights are orderly, unrelenting, and deadly.

description.value: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Trigger A creature attempts a ranged Strike against the remote war machine

Requirements The war machine is currently outside the creature's reach

Effect The remote war machine makes a ranged Strike against the triggering creature. If the result of this Strike is equal to or greater than the result of the triggering Strike, the triggering Strike is treated as though it were a critical miss.

The remote war machine Steps or Strides, then makes two Strikes against the same target. Both Strikes count toward its multiple attack penalty, but its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes both Strikes.
The war machine Steps or Strides twice. The war machine gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed during these moves.

details.publicNotes:
Strong and ferocious, code name "thermatrod" is designed to maximize carnage and destruction on a battlefield. In contrast, the "vracinea" is among the weakest of the unidentified biotech war machines observed in the Pact Worlds. Sleek and stealthy, it darts in and out of enemy defenses while transmitting data to its pilot before departing. If discovered, the vracinea consumes itself to destroy records of the data it was carrying.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value: A ferrofluid magneto-cluster can feel magnetic fields, allowing it to detect large sources of magnetic metal (Bulk 1 or greater) and items with the tech trait within the listed range.

30 feet. A ferrofluid magneto-cluster is surrounded by a constant field that interferes with nearby equipment. Items with the tech trait inside the aura take a –1 circumstance penalty to all checks using them. Unattended metal objects in the aura of 1 Bulk or less are pulled adjacent to the ooze.

A ferrofluid magneto-cluster can attach itself to any creature its size or larger that has the tech trait or is wearing or wielding items with the tech trait. This is similar to Grab, but the ooze moves with that creature rather than holding it in place.

As long as the ooze is attached, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, and the creature it's attached to must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] to Interact with items with the tech trait. On a failure, the item becomes attached to the ooze (treat as if being held by the ooze).

A ferrofluid magneto-cluster that takes fire damage has its magnetic field deactivated until the end of the ooze's next turn. It releases itself from any creature it was attached to, drops any held items, and can't use Magnetic Leap or Attach until its magnetic field returns.

The ferrofluid magneto-cluster instantly Attaches to a creature within its magnetic field that meets the requirements of that ability. A ferrofluid ooze can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Magnetic Leap]]{1d4 rounds}.

The ferrofluid magneto-cluster contracts, then spews metal shards in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30], dealing @Damage[5d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). Any creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical failure).

The ferrofluid magneto-cluster shifts the polarity of its magnetic field, causing it to briefly repulse magnetic objects. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save or else they're forced to drop any items with the tech trait they're wielding in an adjacent square.

In addition, each creature with the tech trait or wearing armor with the tech trait must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

details.publicNotes:
Magneto-clusters are usually created when several smaller ferrofluid oozes combine. There have, however, been documented cases of these creatures spontaneously appearing after large industrial accidents. Like their smaller "cousins," magneto-cluster oozes seek out sources of electromagnetism. They also search for and consume magnetic objects and metals capable of being magnetized. They completely disregard organic life and usually are only a danger when a life-form doesn't want to relinquish their magnetic material. Some clusters consume so much metal and objects they're mistaken for scavenger slimes. Any metal consumed by a magneto-cluster immediately becomes magnetic.

As living magnetic accelerators, ferrofluid magneto-cluster oozes are capable of launching the metal they collect at high speeds as a defense mechanism. They do so at such high velocities as to make even a simple metal shard accelerate fast enough to puncture high-tech ballistic armor.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

special.label: in areas with no magnetic objects

description.value: You send the creature a mental message of 25 words or fewer, and it can respond immediately with its own message of 25 words or fewer.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You stitch the target to its current plane. While the target is affected by planar tether, the spell attempts to counteract any teleportation effect that would move the target, or any effect that would transport it to a different plane. Planar tether's duration is determined by the target's Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The effect's duration is 1 minute.

Failure The effect's duration is 10 minutes.

Critical Failure The effect's duration is 1 hour.

You unlock the target's inner heroism, granting it a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception checks, saving throws, and skill checks.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +2.

Heightened (9th) The status bonus increases to +3.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.l9HRQggofFGIxEse]{Spell Effect: Heroism}

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

You forcibly calm creatures in the area, soothing them into a nonviolent state; each creature must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success Calming urges impose a -1 status penalty to the creature's attack rolls.

Failure Any emotion effects that would affect the creature are suppressed and the creature can't use hostile actions. If the target is subject to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by calm.

Critical Failure As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Qr5rgoZvI4KmFY0N]{Spell Effect: Calm}

Gentle restorative magic pushes back the effects of toxins and more complex maladies. Choose an affliction on the target, such as a curse, disease, or poison. If it has advanced past stage one, reduce the stage by one. This reduction can be applied only once to a given case of an affliction, with the case ending when it's completely cured. Although the reduction can't occur again, heightened versions of this spell attempt to counteract with each casting.

Heightened (3rd) Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a disease or poison.

Heightened (4th) Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a curse, disease, or poison.

You drive mental contamination from the target's mind. Attempt to counteract an effect of your choice imposing one of these conditions on the target: @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied}. If you failed to counteract the effect but you would have if its counteract rank were 2 lower, instead suppress the effect until the beginning of your next turn. The effect's duration doesn't elapse while it's suppressed. This spell can't counteract or suppress conditions that are part of curses, diseases, or a natural state of the target.

Heightened (4th) Add @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed} to the list of conditions.

Heightened (6th) As 4th rank, plus add @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed}.

Heightened (8th) As 4th rank, plus add doomed and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned}.

You shield the target against dangerous temperatures. Choose severe cold or heat. The target is protected from the temperature you chose (but not extreme cold or heat).

Heightened (3rd) The target is protected from severe cold and severe heat.

Heightened (5th) The target is protected from severe cold, severe heat, extreme cold, and extreme heat.

Your gaze pierces through illusions and finds @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} creatures and spirits. You can see invisible creatures as though they weren't invisible, although their features are blurred, making them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} and difficult to identify. You can also see incorporeal creatures, like ghosts, phased through an object from within 10 feet of an object's surface as blurry shapes seen through those objects. Subtler clues also grant you a +2 status bonus to checks you make to disbelieve illusions.

Heightened (5th) This spell has a duration of 8 hours.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.T5bk6UH7yuYog1Fp]{Spell Effect: See the Unseen}

As long as you and the target are on the same plane of existence and both alive, you remain aware of its present state. You know the target's direction from you, distance from you, and any conditions affecting it.

Heightened (4th) The spell's range increases to 30 feet, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

duration.value: until the next time you make your daily preparations

description.value:
The target can understand the meaning of a single language it is hearing or reading when you cast the spell. This doesn't let it understand codes, language couched in metaphor, and the like (subject to GM discretion). If the target can hear multiple languages and knows that, it can choose which language to understand; otherwise, choose one of the languages randomly.

Heightened (3rd) The target can also speak the language.

Heightened (4th) You can target up to 10 creatures, and targets can also speak the language.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You ward an ally against the attacks and hostile spells from the target enemy. The target ally gains a +1 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws against the target enemy's attacks, spells, and other effects.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.ctMxYPGEpstvhW9C]{Spell Effect: Forbidding Ward}

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +2.

You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.3qHKBDF7lrHw8jFK]{Spell Effect: Guidance}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.3LyOkV25p7wA181H]{Effect: Guidance Immunity}

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
You channel vital energy to heal the living or damage the undead. If the target is a willing living creature, you restore 1d8 Hit Points. If the target is undead, you deal that amount of vitality damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing a living creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse vital energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the burst.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

You empower attacks with vital energy. The number of targets is equal to the number of actions you spent casting this spell. Each target's unarmed and weapon Strikes deal an extra 1d4 vitality damage. (This damage typically damages only undead). If you have the holy trait, you can add that trait to this spell and to the Strikes affected by the spell.

Heightened (3rd) The damage increases to 2d4 damage.

Heightened (5th) The damage increases to 3d4 damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.X7RD0JRxhJV9u2LC]{Spell Effect: Infuse Vitality}

Applying a combination of vital energy and acupressure, you help the target regain its footing. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition if it has it. The target becomes immune to the off-guard condition until the start of your next turn, even if the condition would be circumstantially applied, such as by being flanked or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The target is then immune to reorient for 10 minutes.

You ward a creature with protective energy that deters attacks. Creatures attempting to attack the target must attempt a Will save each time. If the target uses a hostile action, the spell ends.

Critical Success Sanctuary ends.

Success The creature can attempt its attack and any other attacks against the target this turn.

Failure The creature can't attack the target and wastes the action. It can't attempt further attacks against the target this turn.

Critical Failure The creature wastes the action and can't attempt to attack the target for the rest of sanctuary's duration.

You form a spiritual link with another creature, taking in its pain. When you Cast this Spell and at the start of each of your turns, if the target is below maximum Hit Points, it regains 2 Hit Points (or the difference between its current and maximum Hit Points, if that's lower). You lose as many Hit Points as the target regained.

This is a spiritual transfer, so no effects apply that would increase the Hit Points the target regains or decrease the Hit Points you lose. This transfer also ignores any temporary Hit Points you or the target have. Since this effect doesn't involve vitality or void energy, spirit link works even if you or the target is undead. While the duration persists, you gain no benefit from regeneration or fast healing. You can Dismiss this spell, and if you're ever at 0 Hit Points, spirit link ends automatically.

Heightened (+1) The number of Hit Points transferred each time increases by 2.

Life energy shuts death's door. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} condition, though it remains @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} at 0 Hit Points.

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. divine

description.value: 60 feet. All allied creatures in the aura gain a +5-foot status bonus to Speeds (if the dragon is photon-attuned), or all enemies take a –5-foot status penalty to Speeds (if the dragon is graviton-attuned).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

The cosmic dragon is always attuned to an underlying stellar force: either photons or gravitons. They can swap attunement as a free action once at the start of their turn in combat and is otherwise attuned to whichever force they choose at any time outside of combat. The dragon's melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage while they're photon-attuned and an additional @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage plus Knockdown while they're graviton-attuned. The damage of their cosmic blast Strike is likewise determined by their current attunement, as are the effects of some of their other abilities. The dragon can choose to exclude any creatures from any effect of the aura or action that uses the aura.

The dragon can always sense the distance and direction to the nearest Drift beacon and to the Starstone on Absalom Station.

The dragon unleashes divine power like that from the core of a star or black hole, dealing @Damage[7d10[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[will|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save). The dragon can't use Stellar Breath for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon conjures a mote of stellar energy that shares their current attunement. They direct the mote to a square within 90 feet. While the mote is deployed, the dragon's attunement aura also emanates from that square, with the same emanation radius. The dragon can Sustain to move the mote up to 90 feet or to recall the mote from any distance. The mote is made of cosmic energy, doesn't occupy a space, and can't be targeted or destroyed by any effect.

The dragon imposes an effect on creatures within their attunement aura. The effect is based on the dragon's current attunement. The dragon can't use Stellar Pulse again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Stellar Pulse]]{1d4 rounds}.

  • Graviton Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29|options:area-effect] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

  • Photon Each creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

details.publicNotes: These majestic dragons embody the stellar forces that underly and balance all that exists in the universe. Cosmic dragons are wise, social, and patient beings, accepting of all whom they come across; they see all creatures as equal components of the universe and are just as likely to be found discussing astrophysics in the company of scholars as they are to be found lamenting the twists and turns of fate in the company of the downtrodden. Most cosmic dragons can change their shape, though they usually only do so to make other creatures comfortable. They detest deception and always introduce themselves as dragons even while in another form.

description.value: A khefak chews on an unattended object to feed, dealing @Damage[4[piercing]] damage every 10 minutes to the object. This damage ignores 10 points of Hardness. If a khefak doesn't spend at least 1 hour Gnawing Scrap per day, it begins to starve until it spends 10 minutes Gnawing Scrap.

details.publicNotes:
Explorers who encounter khefaks for the first time find themselves surprised not only with its aggression, but the target of that aggression: armor and other gear. It isn't uncommon for inexperienced explorers in khefak territory to return from expeditions literally empty-handed, much to the amusement of locals.

Khefak Biogenetics bioengineered khefaks to consume scrap thasteron so the waste could be refined into starship fuel. These creatures eventually became a prime example of a solution becoming a problem of its own. After the discovery of Drift travel, Akiton's thasteron market plummeted, removing the need for mining infrastructure, including khefaks. Khefak Biogenetics destroyed the production facilities, thinking they'd completely eradicated their creations. Khefaks are hardy, however, and the few survivors left the facilities. Beyond thasteron, they subsisted on inorganic materials, and their rapid reproduction rate allowed them to quickly spread all over Akiton. Some stowed away on starships, allowing them to spread throughout the Pact Worlds.

description.value:
You deter enemies with a wave of hostile intent. The target takes 7d8 nonlethal mental damage, and the target must attempt a Will save. The psychic energy then arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the wave at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome).

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected, and the wave ends with that target.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d8.

target.value: 1 creature, plus any number of additional creatures

description.value:
You channel the bizarre powers that exist beyond the outer reaches of the universe, dealing 3d12 mental damage to each creature in the area as phantasmal claws, jaws, and tentacles lash out at each target. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. The area is difficult terrain for any creature sickened by the effects of this spell.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

You unleash a ray of entropy that rapidly and indiscriminately decays matter. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 void damage, or it takes 2d6 persistent acid damage and is glitching 1 if it has the tech trait. On a critical hit, double the damage, and if the creature has the tech trait, it's glitching 2 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1 die.

You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

A cacophonous noise blasts out, dealing 2d10 sonic damage. Each creature must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is deafened for 1 minute, and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d10.

You channel your life force into a spear of energy. You lose 6 Hit Points or, if you're in a bond with a vitality network that has at least 12 Hit Points, you can reduce the vitality bond's Hit Points by 12 instead. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d6 spirit damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage dealt by 2d6 and the Hit Points lost by 2 (or by 4 if you spend Hit Points from a vitality network).

You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You unleash a painfully rapid cone of vibrations by letting out a cathartic scream, dealing 1d8 sonic damage to creatures in the area with a basic Fortitude save. A creature that fails its saving throw also becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increases the damage by 1d8.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Regeneration]

100 feet. Liberoba constantly emits sonic pulses that are disruptive to biomatter. Creatures that enter or begin their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save to resist the nauseating pulses. On a failure, a creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} for 1 round; on a critical failure, it's also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} for 1 round.

Liberoba can sense the presence of any creature inside ultrasonic pulse's area. This functions as an imprecise sense. Liberoba treats a creature that fail its save against ultrasonic pulse as though they can always see it, ignoring the effects of any cover, concealment, invisibility, or similar effects, as long as the creature remains inside the aura.

Liberoba pulls all enemies within 30 feet up to 30 feet toward them, into an adjacent square if possible; each target can negate this movement with a successful @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save. Liberoba then makes an aucturnite shears Strike against each adjacent foe. These attacks count as one Strike for the purposes of calculating their multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. Targets that succeed at their saves are temporarily immune to Breach Delivery for 1 hour.

Trigger Liberoba's turn begins

Effect Liberoba radiates powerful contractions in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] around them. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[3d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save. On a critical failure, the creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Presulea teleports to the location of any aucturnite fragment within a planetary range. Presulea automatically uses this ability as a free action when reduced to 0 Hit Points as long as a piece of aucturnite is within range, reforming their body and recovering completely over the next 24 hours.

details.publicNotes:
Liberoba delivered the Newborn. Many appendages protrude from this creature's bizarre anatomy, each ending in bloody aucturnite shears.

Thirteen mortal Midwives attended the Newborn's birth and were transformed into eldritch beings by the nascent outer god's awakening. A Midwife always regenerates an identical new form so long as even a single speck of aucturnite—the name for the frozen chunks of planetcenta that once surrounded the Newborn—exists on this plane.

There are 13 types of Midwives, but their true numbers are unknown. Whether their names come from the mortal servants whose souls were the kindling for the Midwives' creation or have some other significance to the Newborn, also remains a mystery.

Each Midwife identifies themself by how they cared for the Newborn: if they swaddled, fed, carried, held, watched, warded, blessed, or performed other acts of nurturing for the outer god. The Midwives are powerful beings capable of transdimensional travel. They might be encountered in liminal spaces, such as the Drift or the Ghost Levels of Absalom Station, or they might be found among the clouds of aucturnite debris in the Gelid Edge. Their incomprehensible actions always advance the Newborn's inscrutable agenda—or their own.

The most well-known encounter the Pact Worlds have had with the Midwives took place in the immediate aftermath of the Newborn's birth. Amnieka, who swaddled the Newborn and has a hood of writhing tentacles around their visage, attacked Absalom Station directly, causing a massive power surge that threatened the lives of everyone on board. Chaos ensued, but a group of adventurers—who happened to be in the right elevator at the right time—found themselves in the station's Ghost Levels, where they thwarted Amnieka and prevented the Midwife from draining the station's reactors. The Midwives haven't acted so directly since that incident, but many suspect it's only a matter of time, and no one knows just how powerful the others might be.

description.value: A rivarin endling can sense the presence of bleeding, frightened, or wounded creatures as a precise sense at the listed range.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

When an endling is destroyed, it reforms after [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the area where it first emerged, fully healed. An endling can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting, such as an act of decolonization, ecological restoration, or curing the disease responsible for its extinction.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]
Creatures hit by a rivarin endling's Strikes must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save or take @Damage[1d10[persistent,bleed]] damage.

The rivarin endling Flies, then rears up in a terrifying display. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:29] save.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and must spend the first action of its next turn @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and fleeing for 1 round.

details.publicNotes:
Rivarins were furry, flat-toothed, three-eyed quadrupedal herbivores ranched as pack beasts by a feudal society on wintry Destalon-2. Rivarins were brought to extinction alongside their fledgling society by jinsuls. The jinsuls fled soon after, reportedly following attacks by ghostly giants with jagged fangs and baleful red eyes.

Every species follows a natural arc as it emerges and evolves: rising, thriving, declining, and falling into extinction. When extremely unnatural ends to these paths occur—when unexpected predation, industrialization, or alien pathogens wipe out a population—endlings are born. These spectral members of extinct species typically develop as "answers" to their eradication. Xenobiologists differ on what creates endlings, unable to confirm whether the creatures are a species' last collective sorrow made manifest or just vengeful individual spirits—the last lights to go out.

description.value: Once an animated firearm is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, it becomes permanently @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} until its Hit Points are fully restored (such as by being Repaired).

Using a built-in thruster pack, the animated armor Strides and then attempts a [[/act shove]], moving with the target on a success or critical success. After resolving the Shove, the animated tech armor attempts to [[/act trip]] the target using the same multiple attack penalty as the Shove. The animated armor then falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

details.publicNotes:
Animated suits of tech armor excel as loyal bodyguards, training dummies, or security.

Objects can be animated in multiple ways for a variety of functions. Since ancient times, weapons and armor have been given life as guardians and sentries through magic or ritual.

description.value:
A personal comm unit is a pocket-sized device that combines a minor portable computer and a cellular communications device, allowing instantaneous wireless communication with other comm units in both audio- and text-based formats at planetary range. A personal comm unit also includes a calculator, a flashlight (emits bright light in a 5-foot radius around you and dim light in the next 5 feet), a browser that accesses any local infospheres, and several entertainment options (including games).

Personal comm units integrated into armor have some voice-activated capabilities; you can communicate with other comm units in audio-based formats and receive responses without needing a free hand, but typing a message, browsing the infosphere, or using an app requires the use of at least one hand.

Comm units that function at longer ranges aren't as portable; they include built-in generators that provide the necessary amount of power and rely on active Drift beacons to communicate. Short-term use of these units can be purchased in most major settlements.

The comm unit of a necrolinked wight can be disabled for 1 hour with a successful DC 15 check to Disable a Device or Disable a System. A necrolinked wight with a disabled comm unit is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}, gains a +6 bonus to Perception, and can't Check its Feed.

The necrolinked wight is driven by a desire to continue their cycles of spiteful hatred and media consumption on the infosphere. They spend their first action of each round to Check Feed. Eternally obsessed, a necrolinked wight will always try to stay within range of an active infosphere signal. A necrolinked wight with no infosphere access or perceived threats functions as if their aligned comm unit was disabled and does whatever it can to regain infosphere access.

The necrolinked wight looks up information on their aligned comm unit about a specific creature using Computers instead of any other skill. On a success, the creature takes a –2 status penalty to its next Will save against necrotic transmission for 1 minute ( –3 status penalty on a critical success).

A creature that takes damage from a necrolinked wight's Shriek must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:15] save, or its subconscious is scanned and used to infect its non-augmentation tech items with a virus. Infected tech items display algorithmically generated content catering specifically to the creature's deepest desires, causing the creature to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} as long as they're holding or wearing the tech item. On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated}, and all creatures and objects other than the tech item are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from the creature. The creature or its allies can end the effect on any specific tech item by Interacting to reboot the affected item and succeeding at a @Check[computers|dc:15] check; on a failure, the item becomes glitching 1 (glitching 2 on a critical failure).
A living creature that dies while @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by necrotic transmission rises as a necrolinked wight after [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes: Hollow-eyed undead born from those who died obsessing over social media, necrolinked wights wander both the living and virtual worlds, desperate for engagement and relevance. They're incapable of seeing living creatures as anything more than potential followers, hunting those who engage with technology in the hopes of turning them into obsessed fans. They constantly wail in a condensed magitech code as though screeching at some long-forgotten online debate in an attempt to grab the attention of others by overriding devices with content supernaturally configured to vie for attention. Yet as soon as their victims become necrolinked, they lose interest, driven by the same dark necrotic algorithm.

description.value: You send the creature a mental message of 25 words or fewer, and it can respond immediately with its own message of 25 words or fewer.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. divine

description.value: 60 feet. All allied creatures in the aura gain a +5-foot status bonus to Speeds (if the dragon is photon-attuned), or all enemies take a –5-foot status penalty to Speeds (if the dragon is graviton-attuned).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

The cosmic dragon is always attuned to an underlying stellar force: either photons or gravitons. They can swap attunement as a free action once at the start of their turn in combat and is otherwise attuned to whichever force they choose at any time outside of combat. The dragon's melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage while they're photon-attuned and an additional @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage plus Knockdown while they're graviton-attuned. The damage of their cosmic blast Strike is likewise determined by their current attunement, as are the effects of some of their other abilities. The dragon can choose to exclude any creatures from any effect of the aura or action that uses the aura.

The dragon can always sense the distance and direction to the nearest Drift beacon and to the Starstone on Absalom Station.
The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Stellar Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

The dragon unleashes divine power like that from the core of a star or black hole, dealing @Damage[7d10[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[will|dc:29|basic] save). The dragon can't use Stellar Breath for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon conjures a mote of stellar energy that shares their current attunement. They direct the mote to a square within 90 feet. While the mote is deployed, the dragon's attunement aura also emanates from that square, with the same emanation radius. The dragon can Sustain to move the mote up to 90 feet or to recall the mote from any distance. The mote is made of cosmic energy, doesn't occupy a space, and can't be targeted or destroyed by any effect.

The dragon imposes an effect on creatures within their attunement aura. The effect is based on the dragon's current attunement. The dragon can't use Stellar Pulse again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Stellar Pulse]]{1d4 rounds}.

  • Graviton Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

  • Photon Each creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

details.publicNotes: These majestic dragons embody the stellar forces that underly and balance all that exists in the universe. Cosmic dragons are wise, social, and patient beings, accepting of all whom they come across; they see all creatures as equal components of the universe and are just as likely to be found discussing astrophysics in the company of scholars as they are to be found lamenting the twists and turns of fate in the company of the downtrodden. Most cosmic dragons can change their shape, though they usually only do so to make other creatures comfortable. They detest deception and always introduce themselves as dragons even while in another form.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Upgrades: 1

A phase cutlass resembles a traditional curved sword until it's powered. Then its blade morphs into a shimmering void, with a tiny star for each life it ended.

Upgrades: 1

This squad armor is a molded shell of reinforced polycarbon with a helmet and retractable visor.

20 feet. Undead that begin their turn in the aura (including the Corpse Fleet officer) gain 8 temporary Hit Points for 1 hour, then become temporarily immune to bolstering aura for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.ZWOPndNX0EIhYCG9]{Effect: Bolstering Aura}
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]
The Corpse Fleet officer has trained to decimate the living. Their Strikes deal an additional 1d6 void damage.

The Corpse Fleet officer makes a ranged or melee Strike against a creature. Each undead within 100 feet of the officer can immediately use a reaction and expend ammunition from a ranged weapon they're wielding equal to its expend value to increase the damage of Lead Salvo by @Damage[1d4[void]] damage for each undead ally, to a maximum of an additional @Damage[4d4[void]] damage.

The Corpse Fleet officer makes one ranged Strike and one melee Strike in any order. These attacks count toward their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks are made.

details.publicNotes:
A Corpse Fleet officer is a bold combat leader who bolsters their undead allies and has trained to destroy the living.

The Corpse Fleet is an army made up of undead supremacists who refuse to bow to the will of the living. Founded by defectors from the Eoxian navy, the Corpse Fleet aims to shatter Pact Worlds unity and liberate Eox from the influence of the living and those bone sages who sided with them. Unyielding and utterly ruthless, the Corpse Fleet deals in death and destruction, feeding on the living and exploiting the dead.

description.value: Snow doesn't impair an arabuk's vision; it ignores @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} from snowfall.

A non-arabuk creature standing on snow that fails its Reflex save against an arabuk's antler reverberation is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. Up to a @Template[cube|distance:20] of snow in the area of an antler reverberation collapses, dealing @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to adjacent creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect] save). On a slope, this causes the snow and any creatures that fail its save to move downhill 30 feet per round. At the start of its turn, each creature caught in the avalanche takes @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect] save) and, on a failure, is still caught in the avalanche.

The arabuk clatters its antlers together by shaking its head. Until the end of its turn, any creature hit by its antlers must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:inflicts:stunned] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).
The arabuk Strides twice with a +10-foot circumstance bonus to Speed, ignoring difficult terrain caused by ice, snow, or other arctic conditions.

details.publicNotes:
Arabuks take 3 years to reach maturity. Each year that an arabuk lives, its antlers grow more impressive, sometimes reaching lengths greater than 15 feet.

Those traveling through Akiton's Winterlands risk stumbling on its apex predator: the carnivorous arabuk.

description.value:
To the target, your words are honey and your visage seems bathed in a dreamy haze. It must attempt a Will save, with a +4 circumstance bonus if you or your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you use hostile actions against the target, the spell ends. When the spell ends, the target doesn't necessarily realize it was charmed unless its friendship with you or the actions you convinced it to take clash with its expectations, meaning you could potentially convince the target to continue being your friend via mundane means.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and aware you tried to charm it.

Success The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of charm, unless it identifies the spell.

Failure The target's attitude becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v66R7FdOf11l94im]{Friendly} toward you. If it was Friendly, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v44P3WUcU1j0115l]{Helpful}. It can't use hostile actions against you.

Critical Failure The target's attitude becomes Helpful toward you, and it can't use hostile actions against you.

Heightened (4th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations.

Heightened (8th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations, and you can target up to 10 creatures.
A quyu can sense nearby motion through vibration and air movement as a precise sense at the listed range.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NoBreath]

30 feet. While in their Facade Form (see below), the quyu emits an aura to infatuate nearby creatures. A creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save or it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the quyu for 1 minute and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack the quyu for 1 round, even if the quyu leaves Facade Form.

Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the quyu's jaws or is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} with the quyu
