Effect The quyu makes a claw Strike against the required creature. If the attack hits, that creature also takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

The quyu compresses and reshapes themself to appear as an adorable, helpless creature. They have an automatic result of 35 on Deception checks and DCs to pass as the creature. While in this form, the quyu is Tiny and can't attack, and their pathetic gaze aura is active. The quyu can Dismiss this form.

details.publicNotes:
At first look, quyus appears to be a small, adorable creatures, likely resembling a pet or helpless animal. This appearance is, however, a ploy meant to lure unsuspecting creatures into their grasp. Their true form is a two-headed twisted knot of flesh with eyeless sockets and gaping maws dripping with acid—and they're far from helpless.

Quyus can live without air, food, or water indefinitely. They have a dangerous combination of bottomless curiosity and complete lack of concern for other species' well-being. They originate from a now-Diasporan asteroid, though due to their disguises, they've spread to countless planets undetected, where they hunt and dissect creatures for their sadistic pursuits.

These solitary creatures prefer to observe an area's wild and domesticated species before infiltrating the peripheries of a civilization. There, they establish quiet cover identities, often as children's pets, only to capture and vivisect unsuspecting victims.

Quyus take on whatever appearance they believe is most likely to be considered cute by whatever beings inhabit their environments, even if the creature they mimic has no place in the world's ecology. They might take on the form of an adorable, mundane cat, rabbit, or similar fluffy creature, even in the middle of an asteroid mine or in the deep sea.

description.value:
The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

You sense the presence of thinking creatures around you. You detect the presence or absence of thoughts from conscious creatures that have Intelligence modifiers of –3 or higher. You can choose to ignore the thoughts of creatures you're familiar with so they don't interfere with your detection of other thoughts. The spell can penetrate some barriers, but 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force barrier blocks it. Creatures who are immune to mental effects are immune to detection by this spell.

Heightened (4th) You detect the number of thinking minds and whether or not the creatures are telepathic. Creatures with telepathy have louder thoughts than creatures who don't have telepathy and might be able to sense your intrusion, at the GM's discretion.

You magically broadcast grim news onto nearby devices, holo projectors, screens, and other displays (including comm units on armor). Creatures in the area who can see one or more displays must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the display.

Failure The creature is fascinated by the display and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (even if the fascination ends).

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated by the display, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (even if the fascination ends).

You ritually sift through the local infosphere and gain mystical insight about a topic. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveals the GM's best guess among the following outcomes.

  • Weal The results will be good.
  • Woe The results will be bad.
  • Weal and Woe The results will be a mix of good and bad.
  • Nothing There won't be particularly good or bad results. The GM rolls a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|traits:secret]. On a failure, the result is always "nothing." This makes it impossible to tell whether a "nothing" result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of sift the sphere during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, it's possible to get a different result.

Heightened (4th) The spell can predict results up to 1 week into the future.

You fill the minds of your enemies with doubt. Enemies in the area must succeed at a Will save or take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls as long as they are in the area. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet and force enemies in the area that weren't yet affected to attempt another saving throw.

Bane can counteract @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.XSujb7EsSwKl19Uu]{Bless}.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.UTLp7omqsiC36bso]{Spell Effect: Bane}

You make an appeal to a powerful devil, asking them to bind some of their subordinates to your service. If you succeed, the devil sends you their choice of one devil of level 2 or lower, two devils of level 0 or lower, or three devils of level –1 or lower.

Critical Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Weeks]]{1d4 weeks}.

Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Days]]{1d4 days}.

Failure Your request is denied.

Critical Failure Not only is your request denied, but the powerful devil sends word of its displeasure to your master.

Heightened (+1) Increase the level of devil sent to you by 2 for each option.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be a devil)

description.value:
You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.3qHKBDF7lrHw8jFK]{Spell Effect: Guidance}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.3LyOkV25p7wA181H]{Effect: Guidance Immunity}

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You ward a creature against harm. The target gains a +1 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws.

Heightened (3rd) You can choose to have the benefits also affect all your allies in a @Template[emanation|distance:10] around the target.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.RawLEPwyT5CtCZ4D]{Spell Effect: Protection}

For as long as a creature is taking persistent bleed damage from the wahtunshna's claw Strike, that creature can't be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} from, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected} by the wahtunshna.

The devil points at an item with the tech trait that they can see within 60 feet, forcing a firmware update so it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3}. A creature about to use the item can spend an Interact action to read through the item's new terms of service to remove this condition.

details.publicNotes:
Wahtunshnas are brokers, business negotiators, and war profiteers. They operate in the cities of Malfane and the watery planet Embroi and, increasingly, attend meetings with corporate legal teams in Near Space. Trained in negotiation, business administration, and the corporate buzzwords of a hundred cultures, wahtunshnas offer a competitive advantage to corporations while building a reputation of their own. The knowledge and industrial power of Hell are tempting to many companies looking to get an edge over their competitors, and wahtunshnas are more than happy to assist. In exchange for the incorporation of spyware into their products and Diabolic clauses in their terms of service, corporations gain access to the intellectual property of Hell, passing on infernal bloatware to their customers. Rumors claim that wahtunshnas contribute to the constant churn of start-ups whenever possible, sabotaging any companies that seek to break or circumvent the terms of their diabolical agreements.

In the shadows of space stations, halls of courtrooms, and offices of corporations, devils tempt mortals with promises of power and lay the groundwork for their conquest. A devil's pacts can vary from elaborate contracts to quick promises, but they all end the same way: a mortal soul condemned, and Hell's forces bolstered. From the open rule of Malfane, the unseen control of Embroi, and hidden pacts with souls across the galaxy, devils continue their relentless work to spread tyranny and force the universe to resemble the hellish bureaucracy of their home.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against the kyokor, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of the kyokor

Effect The kyokor deals @Damage[(6d10+28)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:line|distance:120], and each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is pushed 20 feet away. If flying, it descends 20 feet.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is pushed 40 feet, and descends 60 feet (if flying) or is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is pushed 60 feet away, descends 120 feet (if flying) or is knocked prone, and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

details.description:
The kyokor's tentacles snake out, causing devastation.

When a kyokor passes by or through a settlement—or even attacks!—it leaves a trail of destruction that can be represented by the following hazards.

Terrifying, skyscraper-sized beasts roam the wastelands of Daimalko. Called colossi by the population, these monsters erupted from deep within the earth to stomp cities into dust, decimating the population and destroying every surface civilization on the planet in a terrifying Awakening. Kyokors are among the most dreaded colossi. There's no hope of defeating one without a valko—a war machine created by Daimalko's stubborn survivors to fight the colossi.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:45] (legendary), @Check[occultism|dc:45] (legendary), or @Check[performance|dc:45] (legendary) to soothe or divert the kyokor's attention; using a guardian orb (see the sidebar on this page) to command the kyokor ends the hazard
details.reset: The kyokor's tentacles can't writhe again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge writhe]]{1d4 rounds}.

description.value: A proog can spend 1 minute altering its appearance to imitate any solid object of 1 Bulk or less, though it can't duplicate moving parts or specialized functions. A mimicking proog gains a +2 Circumstance bonus to Deception checks to conceal its true nature. Touching the proog negates this bonus.
A proog can climb onto a Medium or larger creature occupying its space and attempt a Stealth check to [[/act hide]], even if observed.

details.publicNotes:
Proogs, unique among oozes, are cherished pets in many Pact Worlds households. These gentle, affable creatures are considered harmless in all but the worst of circumstances. Proogs quickly adapt to domestic life, are easily trained, and seem to delight in pleasing their owners with favored or humorous transformations. Late in their life cycle, these creatures reproduce by splitting in half without warning. Owners have noted that each resultant proog must relearn any tricks the original knew, leading researchers to conclude that the original proog is lost in the reproductive process.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This rifle's oversized body contains a larger version of an arc pistol capacitor, with a short barrel and polycarbon stock designed to insulate the wielder from high voltage. The arc rifle's larger capacitor allows it to produce a charge with enough power to subdue a rampaging renkroda.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AllAroundVision]

The jinsul aims with their melee or ranged weapon against a target within 60 feet. For the rest of their turn, successful Strikes made with that weapon deal an additional 1d4 precision damage and reduce the target's cover bonus to AC by 1.
The jinsul Strides and then performs a [[/act long-jump]]. They can Stride less than 10 feet and not automatically fail their check to Long Jump.

details.publicNotes:
Warblooded are jinsuls who have survived at least a half-dozen engagements in service to the Jinsul Hierocracy. They take on roles of minor leadership or as elite sharpshooters and commandos in a unit of other jinsuls. Warblooded often elect to sit out of engagements that they deem "beneath" them, opting to train up or observe fights from a distance unless they need to step in as reinforcements. These jinsuls often use their extra training time to learn how to pick out critical foes in a battle.

Jinsuls are a warlike species scattered across the Vast. Six-legged creatures with a bulbous body covered in eyes, jinsuls have notable segments of chitin along with a set of upper forelimbs for using equipment. They long ago lived in the region of space known as the Scoured Stars but eventually departed and settled on the harsh world of Rax. In recent years, the divine herald Dhurus unified the jinsuls into a cohesive force known as the Jinsul Hierocracy, which briefly retook the Scoured Stars. A joint alliance between several species and members of the Starfinder Society defeated Dhurus and scattered the jinsuls back into the Vast.

With countless bases and fleets operating in deep space, jinsuls mostly act as raiders, scavenging for equipment and testing their mettle against other species. Indoctrinated to view themselves as a superior species, with only other inhabitants of the Scoured Stars having any value, jinsuls prefer to engage in short-ranged combat with their foes.

Jinsuls aren't numerous enough to fill every need on the many ships and starbases they control across the galaxy. Instead, they rely on robots and automated computer systems (always commanded by at least one jinsul officer) to keep their space stations running efficiently. These systems are programmed to be just as deadly and ruthless as jinsuls themselves; even the most innocuous automated system or robot, like a garbage collector, might wield deadly weapons or contain vicious technomagical countermeasures.

description.value: A hesper can sense radiation sources, such as reactors and power cores, as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

A hesper can deliver an enormous, mutating dose of radiation with a touch. Creatures struck by a hesper's fist Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save. On a failure, the creature gains a random mutation from the table below for 1 hour (or 24 hours on a critical failure).

[[/r 1d8]]Mutation
1Extra eyes: Target is dazzled in bright light and gains a +2 status bonus to sight-based Perception checks.
2Bioelectric cells: Tech items held by the target regain all spent charges at the end of the creature's turn. Tech items are glitching 1 for as long as the target holds them. If the target is a creature with the tech trait, it becomes glitching 1, and its melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d6[electricity]] damage.
3Spikes: Target gains an unarmed melee attack that deals @Damage[1d4[piercing]] damage. It takes a –2 status penalty to attack rolls with two-handed weapons.
4Glowing Tumors: Target sheds light as a flashlight and takes a –2 status penalty to Stealth checks.
5Gills: Target gains the amphibious trait and takes a –1 status penalty to Fortitude saves.
6Musk gland: Target gains a 30-foot stench aura with a DC equal to their class DC.
7Protective growths: Target gains a +1 status bonus to AC and takes a –2 status penalty to Dexterity-based checks.
8Cellular alteration: The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened} for the duration and gains @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.fJSNOw4zHGbIm4bZ]{Fast Healing} with a value equal to half its level (rounded up).

details.publicNotes: These nuclear fey make their homes in reactors and embody the rapidly changing nature of their radioactive homes. They shift colors to reflect their moods, which can sometimes be used to predict their dangerous bouts of boredom. Hespers never intend malice, but they rarely consider the consequences of their actions.

description.value:
Trigger The dragon critically fails a saving throw

Effect Swarming insects interfere with the effect, sacrificing themselves to protect their host's body and mind. The dragon attempts a @Check[flat|dc:17]. On a success, the dragon fails the saving throw instead of critically failing.

90 feet. @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. mental

description.value: The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Swarm Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

A creature that takes damage from a host dragon's jaws Strike or is swallowed by the dragon is also bitten by hundreds of tiny insects; the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 round.
The host dragon is immune to any attack made by a swarm of insects or similar creature (at the GM's discretion).

Large, @Damage[(3d10+7)[bludgeoning]] plus excruciating bites, Rupture 26

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

The dragon disgorges a swarm of biting insects that deals @Damage[9d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40] (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). They can't use Swarm Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Swarm Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon transforms into an insect swarm. They drop all held, worn, and carried items. While in this form, they can't use attack actions (except Swarming Bites and Swarm Breath) and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their individual insects to fit without having to Squeeze. They can use the same action to coalesce back into their normal form.

Requirements The dragon is a swarm

Effect Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[8d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

details.publicNotes: Innumerable insects follow these lumbering, ravenous dragons wherever they roam, swarming around them and living in the countless orifices along the host dragon's body. The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them. Host dragons despise answering questions and violently defy all attempts others make to understand their nature.

description.value: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]
The superior war machine's ranged Strikes are launched with such high velocity that they can be used underwater without penalty.
The remote war machine makes three Strikes against any combination of targets. Each Strike gains the agile trait, and each Strike counts toward its multiple attack penalty.
The war machine Steps or Strides twice. The war machine gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed during these moves.

details.publicNotes:
Devised by engineers based on Kalo-Mahoi, the robotic war machine, code name "atlapak," was built for aquatic warfare. Just like its amphibious creators, the atlapak is equally proficient on land. Code name "ksarik" was first observed in a terrifying attack on a Diasporan mining outpost operated by Ulrikka Clanholdings. When unfurled for battle, the ksarik becomes a mass of spiked tentacles and glaring red sensors that can tear through platoons of troopers in minutes.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This reinforced rod is commonly made from metal alloys and designed to strike and parry incoming blows.

Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Upgrades: 1

This overcoat is made of real or synthetic leather and reinforced with armored plates. Armored coats are often tailor-made to match the wearer's personal style. Standard versions are issued to military officers as part of their uniforms.

A Free Captain knave's melee attacks gain the trip trait and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.BCLvAx4Pz4MLa2pu]{Knockdown} ability against @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.
Any creature that has the frightened condition is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against the Free Captain knave's attacks.

Trigger A creature within melee reach is hit by a ranged weapon or rolls a failure or critical failure against an area attack

Effect The Free Captain knave makes a melee Strike against the triggering creature.

details.publicNotes:
The Free Captains aren't picky employers and will take almost anyone they believe capable of following the Pirate Code. Most Free Captain ships carry a handful of fresh recruits each trip, some of whom will actually return for a second voyage. While usually relegated to the most demeaning and mundane jobs on board, these sailors take every opportunity to prove themselves to the rest of the crew once combat starts.

The Free Captains have been a scourge of interstellar shipping across the Pact Worlds for generations, but since the adoption of Drift Lanes, the outlaws have expanded their reach and scale by targeting ships across the galaxy using the new standardized interstellar shipping routes.

description.value: While in space, the shipbreaker can sense electronic signals from starships in the same star system. While it can't necessarily interpret starship signals, it has a vague sense of the intent or purpose of any given starship signal, such as "to scan," "to communicate," or "to warn of danger."
A shipbreaker can detect creatures and items with the tech trait as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Trigger A creature hits the shipbreaker with a melee Strike

Effect The shipbreaker discharges a field of plasma, dealing @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage and @Damage[1[persistent,electricity]] damage plus the effects of plasmashock to the triggering creature.

The shipbreaker Flies, then Leaps vertically up to 25 feet, plus a cumulative 25 feet for each consecutive round the shipbreaker uses this ability, and makes a jaws Strike against a creature at any point during the jump. In low gravity, the shipbreaker Leaps 100 feet high the first round it jumps, cumulatively multiplied by 4 each consecutive round if trying to reach escape velocity to leave the surface of a planet (which requires 5 consecutive rounds of using Atmospheric Breach in a low-gravity environment).

A creature damaged by a shipbreaker's Plasma Shield or jaws Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to plasmashock for 1 hour.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} and loses its reactions until the start of its next turn. If the creature has the tech trait or the shipbreaker was attacked by a tech weapon, the creature or weapon becomes glitching 1.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. If the creature has the tech trait or the shipbreaker was attacked by a tech weapon, the creature or weapon becomes glitching 2.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} and glitching 3.

Large, @Damage[(2d10+4)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 17

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
Shipbreakers are bullet-shaped chunks of living steel crackling with electricity from their plasma fields. Shipbreakers propel themselves through space, seeking faint electronic signals emitted by starships. After tracking a target, shipbreakers slam into the starship using a powerful breach maneuver and then rip through the hull to get at the crew inside, ingesting passengers, components, and cargo in a vicious gulp that fuels their interplanetary predation.

Shipbreakers are especially attracted to batteries and even prioritize eating a fully charged battery over a more nutritious living creature. These batteries remain a permanent component of shipbreakers, storing and retrieving excess caloric energy from the elementals. Shipbreakers that have ingested a tremendous amount of batteries over their long existences often glow from the inside or crackle with electricity.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

description.value:
You emit a rocket of flame from behind you and blast forward. You move in a straight line up to twice your Speed; you can move through spaces occupied by creatures, and the movement doesn't trigger reactions. Creatures in the area take 6d12 fire damage with a basic Reflex save. On a critical failure, a creature also takes 2d6 persistent fire damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round. You reappear at the end of the line.

Heightened (+2) The fire damage increases by 2d12, and the persistent fire damage on a critical failure increases by 1d6.

You conjure a poisonous fog. This functions as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.9XHmC2JgTUIQ1CCm]{Mist}, except the area moves 10 feet away from you each round. You deal 6d8 poison damage to each breathing creature that starts its turn in the spell's area. You can Dismiss the spell.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You repel effects that would hinder a creature or slow its movement. While under this spell's effect, the target ignores effects that would give them a circumstance penalty to Speed. When they attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} an effect that has them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, they automatically succeed unless the effect is magical and of a higher rank than the unfettered movement spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.CdAyAiMGESvgNQtz]{Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement}

A roaring blast of fire detonates at a spot you designate, dealing 6d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You snap your fingers and point at a target, which begins to smolder. Make a spell attack roll against the target's AC, dealing 2d4 fire damage on a hit. If the target is within your melee reach, you can choose to make a melee spell attack with the flame instead of a ranged spell attack, which increases all the spell's damage dice to d6s.

Critical Success The target takes double damage and 1d4 persistent fire damage.

Success The target takes full damage.

Heightened (+1) The initial damage increases by 1d4 and the persistent fire damage on a critical hit increases by 1d4.

@Damage[(@item.rank)d4[persistent,fire]]{Scaling Persistent Fire Damage}

@Damage[(@item.rank)d6[persistent,fire]]{Scaling Persistent Fire Damage (Melee)}

items.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

description.value:
90 feet. @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]
90 feet. A creature that enters the radioactive aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save against extreme radiation sickness. It's then temporarily immune to radioactive aura for 10 minutes.

If the next action the dragon uses is Radiation Breath, the radiation becomes supercharged with arcane power. Each creature that fails its save against Radiation Breath takes @Damage[3d6[persistent,force]] damage as the supercharged radiation tears it apart from within. Each creature that critically fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} as long as it has this persistent force damage.

Abysium dragons have ultimate control over the radioactive energy that grows within their bodies. Any time an abysium dragon deals fire or poison damage, they can choose whether that damage is magical, and they can change any amount of the fire damage to poison (or any amount of poison damage to fire).
The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Radiation Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

The dragon breathes a blast of caustic superheated gas that deals @Damage[7d6[fire],4d12[persistent,poison]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40] (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic|options:area-effect] save). They can't use Radiation Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Radiation Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

allSaves.value: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

details.publicNotes: Drawing on radiation as both a force of destruction and a source of energy, abysium dragons are tied to the radioactive starmetal of the same name. These dragons grow deposits of pure abysium in their bodies, becoming more radioactive as they age, causing aggression, agitation, and even loneliness since not many creatures can long survive the company of an aging abysium dragon. Abysium dragons frequently turn to pursuits such as augmentation and weapon crafting (to take advantage of their innate power source) and the construction of robots (to keep them company). This work drives abysium dragons to seek out sturdy, radiation-resistant materials they can use for crafting, and they often spend years at a time scouring small moons and asteroids in search of precious materials.

description.value:
Trigger The plasmazer takes electricity damage

Effect The plasmazer redirects the triggering effect to a creature within 30 feet. The creature attempts a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save. On failure, the creature takes an equal amount of electricity damage as the triggering effect.
A plasmazer emits bright light of their color in a 30-foot radius and dim light in the next 30 feet.

The plasmazer Flies twice in a straight line, ignoring any creatures in their path as if the plasmazer is incorporeal. All creatures in or adjacent to the plasmazer's path take @Damage[4d6[electricity]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. On critical failure, a creature also takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,fire]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

details.publicNotes:
Plasmazers are air elementals of vivid colors and grumpy disposition who became commonly found in the Universe after the invention of neon lighting. They manifest as dangerous explosions of light and color that shut down city grids, redirecting the power into a strobing neon kaleidoscope. They often take the shape of legendary birds or other creatures from local myths. Experimental club owners have tried harnessing plasmazers to produce special effects for their dance parties, but such situations usually end with dancers dodging plasma beams. The plasma slide is the name of a line dance that went viral after a plasmazer broke free at the Neon Crypt, an all-hours club on Eox with a reputation for rowdy fetes.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

description.value: A nyssholora senses all creatures that have telepathy, limited telepathy, or a similar ability, such as a mystic's bond, as an imprecise sense at the listed range. This sense works through walls and other obstacles.

Frequency once per round

Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} in the nyssholora's jaws

Effect The nyssholora chomps down on the creature, dealing @Damage[(1d8+7)[piercing]] damage.

The nyssholora unleashes a blast of discordant sound that deal @Damage[5d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). Objects and constructs in the area are treated as if they had half as much Hardness as usual. Creatures that critically fail their saving throw are also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round. The nyssholora can't use Discordant Bellow again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Discordant Bellow]]{1d4 rounds}.
A nyssholora's phasic blade Strike treats any object or construct it hits as if the target had half as much Hardness as usual.

details.publicNotes:
Despite their age, juvenile nyssholoras remain dangerous and formidable predators.

Incredibly ferocious, nyssholoras are dinosaur-like creatures that number among the most dangerous and feared apex predators on the planet of Castrovel. These walking weapons brandish lethal biology: a massive maw lined with twin rows of razor-sharp teeth, scythe-shaped phasic blade forearms that vibrate at ultrasonic speeds, and a scourge-like tail capable of absorbing electricity from the surrounding atmosphere. Yet, nyssholoras are also cunning and perceptive—a frill of tentacles that crowns their head detect telepathic signals, making it impossible for the local formians and lashuntas to escape their notice. Their phasic blades can cut through walls and vehicles as easily as underbrush, ensuring prey have nowhere safe to hide.

description.value:
You sense the presence of thinking creatures around you. You detect the presence or absence of thoughts from conscious creatures that have Intelligence modifiers of –3 or higher. You can choose to ignore the thoughts of creatures you're familiar with so they don't interfere with your detection of other thoughts. The spell can penetrate some barriers, but 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force barrier blocks it. Creatures who are immune to mental effects are immune to detection by this spell.

Heightened (4th) You detect the number of thinking minds and whether or not the creatures are telepathic. Creatures with telepathy have louder thoughts than creatures who don't have telepathy and might be able to sense your intrusion, at the GM's discretion.

Illusions bend light around the target, rendering it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}. This makes it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected} to all creatures, though the creatures can attempt to find the target, making it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} to them instead. If the target uses a hostile action, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

Heightened (4th) The spell lasts 1 minute, but it doesn't end if the target uses a hostile action.

A wave of magical light washes over the area. You choose the appearance of the light, such as colorful, heatless flames or sparkling motes. A creature affected by revealing light is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled}. If the creature was @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} instead. If the creature was already concealed for any other reason, it is no longer concealed.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The light affects the creature for 2 rounds.

Failure The light affects the creature for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The light affects the creature for 10 minutes.

You touch one object and concentrate on a dataset contained within it, such as a story, message, file, infosphere site, audio recording, or video. You understand the gist of the dataset and can quickly locate the most relevant pages or sections to your search. If the dataset is written or otherwise communicated in a language you don't know, or you attempt to gain information from a computer you don't have access to (such as one with a password you don't know), the spell fails.

If you Cast this Spell as part of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.t5nBkpjroaq7QBGK]{Research} activity, you treat a critical failure as a failure instead.

target.value: one dataset contained within a touched object

description.value:
You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

target.value: 1 dataset or tech item with the tracking trait

description.value:
You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

15 feet. A creature that begins its turn within the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:14] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to buffering zone for 1 day.

Success The creature treats the buffering zone as difficult terrain for 1 round.

Failure As success, and the creature is pushed back 5 feet and takes a –1 status penalty to attack rolls for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.X5oMEjGHa8WsCiqW]{Effect: Buffering Zone (Failure)}

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is pushed back beyond the boundary of the buffering zone and can't reenter the buffering zone for 1 round.

Trigger A creature hits the erabryth with a melee or ranged Strike

Effect The erabryth reflects some of their attacker's anger and aggression back at them. The erabryth gains resistance 3 to the triggering damage and deals @Damage[3[mental]] damage to their attacker.

The erabryth forces a creature within 60 feet to relive its most painful memories and regrets in an instant, confronting it with it difficult memories to help it reconcile its actions and let go of its pain. The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:17|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:stunned] save. Regardless of the result, the target is then temporarily immune to Forced Reflection for 1 day.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} and frightened 1.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.
An erabryth's Strikes have the benefit of a ghost killer upgrade and deal an extra 1d4 void damage to living creatures or 1d4 vitality damage to undead.

details.publicNotes:
When androids die or give their bodies up for renewal—a celebrated and respected event akin to procreation—it's the silent and calm erabryths who usher their souls into the Boneyard. Beings of reflection and introspection, these psychopomps help androids come to terms with their choices, reconcile the trying events of their lives, and realize that the time for renewal has finally come.

Erabryths are lanky and androgynous, with reflective, ribboned flesh and a mirrored disk for a face. They communicate through posture and emotive lights, offering comfort through their soothing, persistent presence.

Psychopomps are death's shepherds who safeguard the cycle of life, death, and the afterlife by slaying undead or ushering souls safely through the Boneyard.

description.value:
You befuddle your target with strange impulses, causing it to act randomly. The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target babbles incoherently and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.

Critical Failure The target is Confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You channel the bizarre powers that exist beyond the outer reaches of the universe, dealing 3d12 mental damage to each creature in the area as phantasmal claws, jaws, and tentacles lash out at each target. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. The area is difficult terrain for any creature sickened by the effects of this spell.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

With a cursory mental touch, you attempt to read the target's mind. It must attempt a Will save. The target then becomes temporarily immune to your mind reading for 1 hour.

Critical Success The target perceives vague surface thoughts from you when you Cast the Spell.

Success You find out whether the target's Intelligence modifier is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.

Failure You perceive vague surface thoughts from the target when you Cast the Spell, and you find out whether its Intelligence is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.

Critical Failure As failure, and for the duration of the spell, you can Sustain the spell to detect the target's surface thoughts again. The target doesn't receive any additional saves.

duration.value: 1 round or sustained up to 1 minute

description.value:
You dilate the flow of time around the target, slowing its actions.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You refract latent energy from the Dimension of Time into your current plane in the form of a massive blade. The blade deals 5d6 slashing damage to all creatures in the area as it causes time to pass rapidly in a thin slice of their bodies. Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

You gain a vague glimpse of the future. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveal the GM's best guess among the following outcomes: good, bad, mixed (the results will be a mix of good and bad), and nothing (there won't be particularly good or bad results).

The GM rolls a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|showDC:all|traits:secret]. On a failure, the result is always "nothing." This makes it impossible to tell whether a "nothing" result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of augury during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, though, it's possible to get a different result.

You sense the presence of thinking creatures around you. You detect the presence or absence of thoughts from conscious creatures that have Intelligence modifiers of –3 or higher. You can choose to ignore the thoughts of creatures you're familiar with so they don't interfere with your detection of other thoughts. The spell can penetrate some barriers, but 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force barrier blocks it. Creatures who are immune to mental effects are immune to detection by this spell.

Heightened (4th) You detect the number of thinking minds and whether or not the creatures are telepathic. Creatures with telepathy have louder thoughts than creatures who don't have telepathy and might be able to sense your intrusion, at the GM's discretion.

You ritually sift through the local infosphere and gain mystical insight about a topic. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveals the GM's best guess among the following outcomes.

  • Weal The results will be good.
  • Woe The results will be bad.
  • Weal and Woe The results will be a mix of good and bad.
  • Nothing There won't be particularly good or bad results. The GM rolls a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|traits:secret]. On a failure, the result is always "nothing." This makes it impossible to tell whether a "nothing" result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of sift the sphere during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, it's possible to get a different result.

Heightened (4th) The spell can predict results up to 1 week into the future.

You share a mood by psychically projecting an intense emotion into a creature's mind. The target attempts a Will save. On a failure, the target experiences your choice of the following emotions, but if you pick anger or joy this spell gains the incapacitation trait.

  • Anger On its next turn, the target spends its first action to attack the nearest creature, even if it's an ally.
  • Joy The target can't take any hostile actions.
  • Fear The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.
  • Sorrow The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and bursts into tears.

Heightened (4th) The spell affects all creatures in a 20-foot burst. Each creature must be affected by the same emotion.

You execute a psychic search of the Akashic Record for information about a specific topic and magically transfer this data to your comm unit or computer. When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you're trained that has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} action.

Any time during the duration, you can consult your comm unit while attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This check gains a +1 status bonus. Checking your comm unit is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must be holding the comm unit to do so. The comm unit instantly compiles and displays snippets and selections from various media relevant to the topic, which grants you the bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the check is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference the comm unit, the spell ends. When the spell ends, your access to the Akashic Record ends, and all the information you downloaded returns to the Akashic Record.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.h1i8c47U0IOrMrHJ]{Spell Effect: Akashic Download}

Heightened (3rd) You can reference your comm unit three times before the spell ends.

system.requirements: You have a comm unit or computer, used as a locus.

description.value:
You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.
You link your mind to the target's mind and mentally impart to that target an amount of information in an instant that could otherwise be communicated in 10 minutes.

You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
Trigger The Dreamer is reduced to half or less than half their total Hit Points

Effect The injured Dreamer erupts into a terrifying state of psychic fury, requiring all non-Dreamers in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] to attempt a @Check[will|dc:24|options:area-effect] save. The Dreamer gains a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute but can't use their innate spells. After they stop raging, a Dreamer can't enter an Atavistic Rage again for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Any creature that uses an emotion or mental effect on a Dreamer must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:24|options:inflicts:slowed] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for the next [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} as fragments of prophetic songs fill its mind.

Any creature struck by a Dreamer's tentacle must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24|options:inflicts:dazzled,inflicts:stupefied,inflicts:stunned,inflicts:suppressed] save to resist a wave of sensory overstimulation. The creature is then temporarily immune to all synesthetic shocks for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the end of its next turn.

Failure As success, but the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the end of its next turn.

Critical Failure As failure, but with a duration of 2 rounds, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

details.publicNotes:
Dancing between Liavara's peach-colored clouds are the Dreamers, the psychically gifted cousins of barathus. Not native to Liavara, the Dreamers were originally barathu explorers. They arrived by sailing one of the first living oma-ships long before the Gap. What exactly transformed them is still unknown to this day, though barathus as a people have been extremely protective of their prophetic cousins ever since.

The Dreamers lack barathus' ability to combine consciousnesses as well as their collectivist culture. That said, the Dreamers often dance through the skies of Liavara in flotillas together. To most of the Pact Worlds, they're a curiosity. To barathus, they're charges to be protected. The Dreamers themselves seem unconcerned with any such attention, giving neither care nor notice as to what's going on around them.

You understand the gist of the dataset and can quickly locate the most relevant pages or sections to your search. If the dataset is written or otherwise communicated in a language you don't know, or you attempt to gain information from a computer you don't have access to (such as one with a password you don't know), the spell fails.

If you Cast this Spell as part of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.t5nBkpjroaq7QBGK]{Research} activity, you treat a critical failure as a failure instead.

A voidsage can share the senses and read the surface thoughts of creatures affected by their quantum nanites. A voidsage can project its mind into just one creature at a time, and choose which affected creature is the focus of its nanite projection as a single action, which has the concentrate trait. The creature also takes a –1 circumstance penalty to Will saves against any of the voidsage's spells as long as it's the focus of the voidsage's nanite projection.
5 feet. A voidsage exudes a cloud of nanites. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura is exposed to quantum nanites.

Frequency once per hour

details.publicNotes: Voidsages are alghollthus who survived the battles of youth with an intact nanite network. After consuming many of their peers' own nanites, a voidsage's network grows strong enough to control a remote war machine.

description.value: Diaspora worms communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Diaspora worms sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the diaspora worm.

Diaspora worms that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

Requirements The diaspora worm is in water

Effect The worm alters the structure of water within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10], forming light-absorbing nanotubes. This field moves with the worm and lasts for 1 minute. The emanation deactivates non-magical light sources and automatically attempts to counteract magical light (counteract rank 4, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+17 #Counteract]]{+17}). This field also provides cover for creatures inside of it from those outside, and vice versa.

Medium, @Damage[(1d10+8)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 12

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

A creature hit by the diaspora worm's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 19 that deals at least 12 slashing damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Requirements The diaspora worm has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

details.publicNotes:
Resembling fanged eels with manes of tendrils instead of eyes, diaspora worms originally ruled the inky depths of the ocean that later became the icy planetoid Nisis. The worms' hostility toward trespassers and lack of observable speech lead most people to consider them mere beasts, but xenobiologists know the worms' tendrils respond to electromagnetic waves, which they manipulate to see, communicate, and interact with their surroundings. Bioelectric radiation from coral samples recovered from the worms' home reef documents millennia of worm culture and history from even before the Diaspora's formation.

Juvenile members of this species are sleek, sinuous, and nimble despite their size. Their electromagnetic manipulation lets them briefly change the molecular structure of water, making it murky like their original depths. Young worms tend to commit brazen attacks, requiring decades of experience to value more complex strategies.

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

description.value:
You inject magical nanobots into a creature. Make a melee spell attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 acid damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round as nanobots swarm through the target's biological or mechanical systems.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

Your touch installs a dangerous self-replicating virus that disrupts a technological creature's programming. The target must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure The target is glitching 1 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

An arc of lightning leaps from one target to another. Each target takes 2d4 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

You repair the target item. You restore 5 Hit Points per spell rank to the target, potentially removing the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6dNUvdb1dhToNDj3]{Broken} condition if this repairs it past the item's Broken Threshold. You can't replace lost pieces or repair an object that's been completely destroyed.

Heightened (2nd) You can target a non-magical object of 1 Bulk or less.

Heightened (3rd) You can target a non-magical object of 2 Bulk or less, or a magical object of 1 Bulk or less.

target.value: non-magical object of 1 Bulk or less
non-magical object of 2 Bulk or less, or a magical object of 1 Bulk or less
non-magical object of light Bulk or less

description.value:
You touch a weapon with no remaining ammunition and recharge it with magical energy. The target gains ammunition or charges equal to its expend until the end of your next turn, allowing anyone holding the target to make one Strike with the weapon. This spell only recharges weapons that use ammunition with a cost of 10 credits or less.

Heightened (3rd) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to double its expend.

Heightened (6th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to triple its expend.

Heightened (9th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to quadruple its expend.

A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

You draw in and condense ambient magical energy from around you to power up a weapon. The next Strike made using that weapon is supercharged. That Strike deals an additional 1d6 damage of the same type as the weapon's normal damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.zzOekbiIKePQDQKy]{Spell Effect: Supercharge Weapon}

Heightened (+2) The extra damage increases by 1d6.

target.value: 1 weapon held by you or a willing ally

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This rifle's oversized body contains a larger version of an arc pistol capacitor, with a short barrel and polycarbon stock designed to insulate the wielder from high voltage. The arc rifle's larger capacitor allows it to produce a charge with enough power to subdue a rampaging renkroda.

When the tool rat's tail Strike deals damage to an object or creature with the tech trait, the tail also gains the following traits until the beginning of the scrap rat's next turn: deadly d4, hampering, and razing.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.GpzaKY9WlDFAhgcg]{Effect: Scoring}

Over the course of 1 hour of uninterrupted work, a tool rat can assemble a simple tactical weapon of any type, up to 2nd level, including a single @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.0jSc8zqUPnAByMf9]{commercial battery} or a full magazine of projectile ammunition. using nothing but junk. It falls apart and becomes unusable junk after 10 minutes.

details.publicNotes:
The tool rat resembles an infant of their kind, but closer study reveals coppery whiskers akin to a dwarven beard and eyebrows. Intuitive spellcraft helps them turn junkyards into minefields for the unwary

When pileups of metal garbage begin to conquer the wilderness, rodents conquer the garbage. Scrap rats adapt to every civilization that disposes their ruined gadgets and industrial debris, all of which the rats recycle and reuse to potentially deadly effect. While not especially intelligent, they have an innate knack for crafting.

description.value:
Upgrades: 0

A mainstay of the Azlanti Star Empire, this sleek laser rifle includes a slot for aeon stones to enhance and focus the intensity of its beams.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 1d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:5] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Upgrades: 1

Popularized in underground fighting circles, this heavy-duty nanocarbon glove amplifies and weaponizes sounds made by its wielder, generating a sonic boom strong enough to knock an enemy off their feet.

Upgrades: 1

Flexible but sturdy plates layered together form this traditional armor worn by lashunta cavalry on Castrovel. In times past, a shotalashu cavalry member forged their armor from their mount's shed skin, or more rarely, the skin of a fallen mount, to honor them in future battles. Synthetic imitations are fashionable on some parts of Castrovel.
This visor wraps around your visual organs, enhancing your natural senses. You gain darkvision.
@Damage[1[bleed]|options:area-damage] on critical failure

After the trooper attempts a Strike against a target (regardless of success or failure), the next attack roll one of the trooper's allies makes against the same target gains a +2 circumstance bonus. If a Strike with a bonus from coordinated fire hits, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until the end of the attacking ally's next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.x9nA4lIcHmE7x8Tt]{Effect: Coordinated Fire}
When the trooper makes a ranged Strike against a target with an AC bonus from lesser or standard cover, they reduce the AC bonus by 1 for that Strike.
The trooper gains a +2 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} targets.

details.publicNotes:
Rank-and-file Aeon Guard troopers are easily identified by their distinctive green armor consisting of ceramic and polycarbonate plates overlaid with an energy-reflective molecular coating. Aeon Guards frequently upgrade their armor with aeon stones, further distinguishing them from soldiers of "lesser" armies.

Even the newest recruits have sworn personal oaths of loyalty to the Aeon Throne—a traitor or deserter of the Azlanti Star Empire is extraordinarily rare. These troopers are also loyal to one another and highly trained to fight in coordinated formations, and they obey their commanding officers without question, even if their orders mean certain death.

description.value: A thermatrod can detect nearby changes in temperature as a precise sense to detect living creatures and other heat sources.
10 feet. A creature that begins its turn within this aura takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save).

The thermatrod sprays part of its molten core in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that hardens moments later. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[4d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). A creature that fails the save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} ([[/act escape dc=20]]). The thermatrod can't use Slag Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Slag Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
A standard thermatrod dwells near volcanic vents and other sources of intense heat.

Originally thought to be exiles from the Plane of Fire, thermatrods actually developed independently in multiple star systems in the Universe. A thermatrod resembles a molten bipedal creature, though they often walk on their front knuckles to support their heavy frames. Thermatrods sport distinctive raised dorsal plates that resemble hexagonal basalt columns, which researchers use to estimate an individual's age. A thermatrod's hardened exterior varies greatly based on local mineral deposits that make up a thermatrod's diet.

description.value:
The squox Strides, attempts an @Check[acrobatics|against:perception] check against an adjacent creature's Perception DC, and then makes a jaws Strike.

Critical Success Thrown by the squox's sudden leap, the target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the end of the squox's next turn.

Success The target is only momentarily startled and is off-guard until the end of the squox's current turn.

Critical Failure The squox misjudges the distance to its target's face and falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

A squox can [[/act disarm skill=acrobatics]] and [[/act trip skill=acrobatics]] creatures with its tail using Acrobatics instead of Athletics, even if it doesn't have a free hand. A squox can attempt to Disarm or Trip @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures up to two sizes larger than it and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Disarm or Trip an off-guard target

details.publicNotes:
The tiny vulkarikis are a common animal in the Pact Worlds, originating from the planet Castrovel. Human explorers from Lost Golarion gave them the name "squox," as they interpreted the creatures as being a mix of fox and squirrel. Squoxes are cunning to the point of being devious and employ scare tactics to routinely abscond with shiny items they uncover on their travels. They exhibit complex social behaviors, including a distinct hierarchy between individuals in a skulk, and have a vast vocal repertoire used to assign signature sounds to different individual squoxes.

Having long since been exported from the Pact Worlds, squoxes are found throughout the galaxy, with many civilizations in Near Space maintaining growing communities of these tiny furry creatures, much to the growing consternation of the Xenowardens who worry that the highly adaptable squox has become an invasive species in several biomes. While some advocate for culling these populations, the popularity of the animal as a pet has made this view an incredibly unpopular stance.

Young squoxes are referred to as kits and can be purchased from the open market on most Pact Worlds. Squoxes tend to live for about 10 years. A typical squox weighs about 10 pounds and is about 3 feet long, with much of its length being its tail. Squoxes come in all colorations, and many even take on unique environmental adaptations to specific climates, such as desert-dwelling squoxes growing longer ears to help disperse heat.

description.value:
You make an appeal to a powerful devil, asking them to bind some of their subordinates to your service. If you succeed, the devil sends you their choice of one devil of level 2 or lower, two devils of level 0 or lower, or three devils of level –1 or lower.

Critical Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Weeks]]{1d4 weeks}.

Success The devils are sent to you and serve you for [[/gmr 1d4 #Days]]{1d4 days}.

Failure Your request is denied.

Critical Failure Not only is your request denied, but the powerful devil sends word of its displeasure to your master.

Heightened (+1) Increase the level of devil sent to you by 2 for each option.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be a devil)

description.value: 20 feet. The othinni is constantly surrounded by a cloud of poisonous black ink.
