You demolish the target's corrupted essence with energy from Creation's Forge. You deal 2d6 vitality damage with a basic Fortitude save. If the creature critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

target.value: 1 creature that is undead or otherwise has void healing

description.value:
You summon a Tiny bouncing wisp of light that easily distracts even the most determined of combatants. The wisp floats near your target. A wisp doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would. The wisp generates bright light in a 10-foot radius. When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the spell, your wisp attempts to distract your target, which must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. A creature who critically succeeds becomes immune to wisp ally for 1 hour.

Heightened (+1) You create one additional wisp, allowing you to choose a different target. Each wisp you create must have a different target, and all of them attempt to distract their targets at the same time when you cast or Sustain this spell.

Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Trigger A willing fey or creature sworn to serve an Eldest dies within 100 feet of an obrhinnshee

Effect The creature's soul is drawn into the obrhinnshee's hammer to be carried home.

When an obrhinnshee dies, they burst into a 10-foot aura of blinding stardust. All non-fey in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:16] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round (dazzled for 1 hour and blinded for 1 minute on a critical failure).

Frequency once per hour

Requirements A soul is contained in the obrhinnshee's hammer

Effect The obrhinnshee wreathes themself in flame. All of the obrhinnshee's Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage for 1 minute.
In the hands of an obrhinnshee, all hammers gain a reach of 10 feet and are treated as magical weapons.

The obrhinnshee grants a willing creature within 100 feet a +2 circumstance bonus on its next attack roll, check, or saving throw before the end of its next turn. The target becomes immune to Minor Wish for 1 minute.
The obrhinnshee Strides twice and makes a melee Strike. On a critical hit using this action with a feyhammer, the target is automatically shoved 10 feet away from the obrhinnshee.

details.publicNotes:
Obrhinnshees travel the Universe to expedite the return of fey souls to the First World after they die, preventing psychopomps from bringing them to the Boneyard. They believe the Eldest shouldn't allow the gods to take those they turned their backs on. In addition to this duty, the small fey seem drawn to people in need and feel compelled to render aid if they can. They're most commonly encountered in areas influenced by the First World but can be found traveling any region of space.

An obrhinnshee resembles a gnome with a shock of green, purple, or deep blue hair and glowing marks on their bodies that resemble star charts. These marks glow brighter when an obrhinnshee uses magic or whenever their hammer contains a soul. Obrhinnshees are small but strong creatures who prefer to wreathe themselves in flame and charge into battle, using their spells only if necessary.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

Instead of traditional ammunition, this bulky pistol contains a capacitor that generates arcs of electricity. Its stock is crafted from polycarbon or other synthetic insulators, often tinted bright yellow and inscribed with the universal bolt symbol for high voltage.

Upgrades: 1

This short, bulky assault rifle incorporates a piston system that allows the user to switch to auto-fire and unload a magazine with one trigger squeeze.

Upgrades: 1

These durable gloves fit over a wielder's hands (or other appendages) and feature weighted plates or spikes over the knuckles.

Upgrades: 1

Flexible but sturdy plates layered together form this traditional armor worn by lashunta cavalry on Castrovel. In times past, a shotalashu cavalry member forged their armor from their mount's shed skin, or more rarely, the skin of a fallen mount, to honor them in future battles. Synthetic imitations are fashionable on some parts of Castrovel.

Trigger An adjacent ally moves away from the Corpse Fleet infantry

Effect The Corpse Fleet infantry Strides up to half their Speed. They must move in the same direction as the triggering ally.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The Corpse Fleet infantry's ranged Strikes deal an additional 1d4 precision damage when the Corpse Fleet infantry is within the first range increment of at least one other member of the Corpse Fleet wielding a ranged weapon.

details.publicNotes:
The Corpse Fleet's rank and file infantry are trained to work as a unit.

The Corpse Fleet is an army made up of undead supremacists who refuse to bow to the will of the living. Founded by defectors from the Eoxian navy, the Corpse Fleet aims to shatter Pact Worlds unity and liberate Eox from the influence of the living and those bone sages who sided with them. Unyielding and utterly ruthless, the Corpse Fleet deals in death and destruction, feeding on the living and exploiting the dead.

description.value: A digital ghost senses creatures and items with the tech trait at the listed range.

An offline copy of the digital ghost is stored in a device. When an active digital ghost is destroyed, it reforms after [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the device that the copy is stored in, fully healed, but with no memory of what destroyed them. A digital ghost can be permanently removed from that device by attempting a @Check[computers|dc:24] check against the digital ghost's Will DC.

On a success, the copy of the digital ghost is purged from the device, and no new copies will be generated. On a critical success, any active copies are also purged.
A digital ghost can stray only a short distance from the device where they're stored. A typical limit is 120 feet.

Frequency once per day

Effect The digital ghost attempts to infect an item with the tech trait being held or used by a creature within 30 feet. The bearer can attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The item is unaffected, and the digital ghost can't use Malware Infection for 1 week.

Success The item is unaffected.

Failure The item becomes glitching 2.

Critical Failure The item becomes glitching 2, and an offline digital ghost based on the bearer is created on the device in [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days}.
A digital ghost's weapons deal mental damage, have the ghost killer upgrade, and disappear if dropped.

details.publicNotes:
Some vidgamers are so terminally online that they can manifest as a digital ghost.

Digital ghosts are data constructs crafted from forgotten user accounts and metadata that are gathered and formatted into a simulacrum of the source user.

description.value: A finitrium fatebender gains a +5 circumstance bonus when rolling Perception for initiative, as it acts on flashes of insight harvested from potential futures.

20 feet. A finitrium fatebender is surrounded by an aura of countless shifting potential futures—fragmented milliseconds of possibility all overlapping in a tumultuous blur. A creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save. If it fails, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} for 1 round (or [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} on a critical failure). A creature that critically succeeds becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 day.

Frequency once per day

Trigger The finitrium fatebender fails or critically fails an attack roll or saving throw

Effect The finitrium fatebender rerolls the triggering check and takes the better result.

Frequency once per round

Trigger The finitrium fatebender would be hit by a Strike

Effect Having foreseen the incoming attack, the finitrium fatebender shifts position, gaining a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

The finitrium fatebender releases a wave of twisted futures and shattered possibilities, dealing @Damage[16d6[mental]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] (@Check[will|dc:36|basic] save). The finitrium fatebender can't use Fate Wave again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Fate Wave]]{1d4 rounds}.

Any creature hit by the finitrium fatebender's fist or ray of twisted fate Strikes receives a vision of their impending death at the hands of the finitrium fatebender. It must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}; on a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. If the creature is already affected by foretold doom, it increases the appropriate stunned and stupefied values by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}. When the finitrium fatebender is destroyed, the creature has avoided this tragic fate and immediately recovers from its stupefied condition.

The finitrium fatebender Strides up to its Speed, pushing back each creature whose space it moves into and damaging creatures that try to stop its movement. A creature can attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40] save to bar the way, stopping the finitrium fatebender's movement on a success. On a critical success, the resisting creature takes no damage; otherwise, it's damaged as if hit by the finitrium fatebender's fist Strike.

details.publicNotes: Finitrium fatebenders are powerful magical constructs created from refined finitrium, a rare starmetal with divinatory properties. Thanks to their ability to see—and react to—the future, finitrium fatebenders make exceptional guardians and formidable foes.

description.value:
Trigger A creature passes within 20 feet of the glass serpent and hasn't detected it

Effect The glass serpent lunges out of hiding. It Strides directly toward the triggering creature and makes a jaws Strike against it. The target creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to this attack.

The glass serpent is naturally invisible. When a glass serpent takes a hostile action of any kind, it is hidden instead of undetected until the start of its next turn, as parts of its body become momentarily tangible. A glass serpent that has swallowed a creature becomes visible for the next day as its meal is being digested. If the swallowed creature escapes or is otherwise removed, the glass serpent immediately returns to being invisible.

Requirements The glass serpent's last action was a successful tail whip Strike

Effect The creature the glass serpent hit is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from all creatures except the glass serpent until that creature moves out of the glass serpent's reach or until the start of the glass serpent's next turn.

Large, @Damage[(2d8+6)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 12

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
The great glass serpents of the barren planet of Eox are one of the Pact Worlds' most notorious apex predators. With an average length of almost 60 feet and a weight of about 5,000 pounds, the size and grace of glass serpents are only part of what makes them such efficient killers. Thanks to an innate invisibility provided by their unique scales, glass serpents remain outside of the visual spectrum of most creatures, allowing them to silently approach unsuspecting prey. Having evolved through Eox's numerous cataclysms, these serpents find sustenance from the rare bands of living creatures who visit the world, and in an odd turn, while they're consuming the living is when glass serpents are at their most vulnerable. The combination of acids and digestive fluids used in consuming a living creature also deprives the serpents of their invisibility, and canny glass serpent hunters actively tempt their quarry with animals to consume before attacking in force.

Glass serpents have made their way well beyond the confines of Eox, and some have even found an accepting ecosystem in the Diaspora's strange waterway, the River Between. The local sarcesians blame Eox for intentionally polluting their waterways with predators, though Eox denies all accusations of intentional meddling. Such debates have only intensified since the Diaspora was granted provisional Pact World status under the sarcesians' stewardship.

description.value: If a cloud ray dies due to fire or electricity damage, its internal reservoirs of explosive gases and combustible fluids detonate in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] that deals @Damage[11d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save).

@Check[reflex|dc:30], @Damage[(3d8+8)[bludgeoning]] damage, [[/act escape dc=30]], Rupture 25

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Engulf]

The cloud ray makes two consecutive tail lash Strikes at a –2 penalty against any targets within reach. These attacks count toward the cloud ray's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all its attacks.

Frequency once per hour

Effect Until the beginning of its next turn, the cloud ray gains a +80-foot status bonus to its fly Speed and deals an additional 1d10 slashing damage with its tail Strikes.

A creature that takes electricity damage from a cloud ray's tail Strike is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical hit), then becomes temporarily immune to stunning lash for 1 minute. Creatures with a weakness to electricity don't become temporarily immune.

details.publicNotes: Enormous cloud rays are apex predators on gas giants such as Bretheda and Liavara. Cloud rays are incredibly maneuverable and can fly through the atmosphere at speeds few can match, rendering them nearly inescapable when on the hunt.

description.value:
You unleash the power of a supermassive black hole. All creatures in the area take 6d10 bludgeoning and 6d10 void damage. Event horizon ignores up to 10 resistance creatures in the area have to bludgeoning, and up to 10 resistance they have to void. Each creature attempts a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of its next turn.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 3} until the end of its next turn, and slowed 1 for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is slowed 3 until the end of its next turn, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 minute.

You sever yourself from cause and effect. For the duration of the spell, you are immune to any effect or damage that would harm you, excluding effects caused by artifacts, deific power, and similarly powerful sources. You can selectively allow yourself to be affected by anything you're willing to have affect you.

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
You crush the target by causing it to collapse in on itself, dealing 75 damage. The first time each round you Sustain the spell, you must choose a new target to be subject to the same effect; the same creature can never be targeted more than once with a single casting of this spell. You also can't affect more than one creature per turn with implosion. You can't target a creature that's incorporeal, gaseous, or liquid, or otherwise lacking a solid form.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 10.

You slice through reality, forming a chasm of life-draining entropy in a straight line 20 feet high, 120 feet long, and 2 inches thick. The chasm provides cover. The chasm deals 7d8 void damage to each creature that's in the chasm's space when it's created, that attempts to pass through the chasm, or that ends its turn inside the chasm, with a basic Reflex save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the chasm fail to do so if they critically fail the save, ending their movement next to the chasm.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You deter enemies with a wave of hostile intent. The target takes 7d8 nonlethal mental damage, and the target must attempt a Will save. The psychic energy then arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the wave at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome).

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected, and the wave ends with that target.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d8.

target.value: 1 creature, plus any number of additional creatures

description.value:
Frequency once per round

Trigger A creature would trigger Paradox 17's Reactive Strike, but it has no reactions remaining

Effect Paradox 17 Steps or Strikes prior to resolving the triggering action.

100 feet. When a creature in the aura observes Paradox 17 for the first time each day, it must attempt a @Check[will|dc:42] save or Performance check. On a failure, reality rejects the creature for as long as it can observe Paradox 17. The affected creature becomes hidden from all other creatures (except Paradox 17) as though invisible itself.

Requirements Another creature is observing Paradox 17 or has triggered interdict definition in the last hour

Effect Paradox 17 teleports to any location that both Paradox 17 and the required creature can observe across any distance, even to another galaxy or plane.
Paradox 17 can move through all creatures, objects, and structures as though they were incorporeal.

Paradox 17 automatically and instantly learns and can observe the exact location of any creature on any plane that succeeds at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check to learn any information about Paradox 17 or any creature who communicates information about Paradox 17 in any way. The ability to observe the creature and its surroundings, wherever the creature goes, lasts 1 hour.
Paradox 17 is naturally invisible. It becomes visible only when using a hostile action.

Paradox 17 burns away the fabric of reality with intense sunlight. If no creatures are observing Paradox 17, the light fills a @Template[type:cone|distance:60]. If any creature is observing Paradox 17, the area is a @Template[type:line|distance:60]. Creatures and unattended objects in the area turn temporarily translucent. Creatures in the area take @Damage[24d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:45] save. Paradox 17 can't use Waking Light again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Waking Light]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The target takes no damage.

Success The target takes half damage becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1}.

Failure The target takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 3}.

details.publicNotes:
As Paradox 17 is normally invisible, most recorded observations of it start with hearing a roar "like static from a dead signal." All reports mention a profound yet hard-to-place sense of meaningfulness. One survivor saw it illuminated by a blast of light she described as "a spiral galaxy made up of shining eyes and glass feathers." Another described it as "dozens of interleaved wings scattered with embers." The only thing consistent about Paradox 17, however, is how deadly it can be if confronted directly.

Can you ever really know that the Universe you perceive exists? That you'll never awaken one day to find it was all a dream? A planet full of philosophers sought the answers to these questions once upon a time… shortly before they all vanished en masse. They left behind confusing records stating that the light of a "whirling star" revealed that physical matter had no substance and the Universe was secretly a mental prison. These records are all that remain from the first known encounter with the being known as Paradox 17.

description.value:
The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You launch a torrent of magical nanites that begin dissolving your target. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, you deal 3d8 acid damage plus 1d6 persistent acid damage. On a critical hit, double the initial damage, but not the persistent damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

You inject magical nanobots into a creature. Make a melee spell attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 acid damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round as nanobots swarm through the target's biological or mechanical systems.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You touch one object and concentrate on a dataset contained within it, such as a story, message, file, infosphere site, audio recording, or video. You understand the gist of the dataset and can quickly locate the most relevant pages or sections to your search. If the dataset is written or otherwise communicated in a language you don't know, or you attempt to gain information from a computer you don't have access to (such as one with a password you don't know), the spell fails.

If you Cast this Spell as part of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.t5nBkpjroaq7QBGK]{Research} activity, you treat a critical failure as a failure instead.

target.value: one dataset contained within a touched object

description.value:
You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

target.value: 1 dataset or tech item with the tracking trait

description.value: You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

100 feet. An excuba can communicate telepathically with any item or creature with the tech trait within their aura as well as with other technologically telepathic creatures (such as those with shortwave and similar abilities). This ability also allows the spectra to remotely attempt Computers checks within their aura.

The excuba can communicate through items and creatures with the tech trait, projecting sound from and speaking through the item or creature's voice, speakers, or other audio output. In addition, the excuba can use any item or creature with the tech trait within their digital telepathy aura as the point of origin for their spells and abilities when calculating range, distance, line of effect, and area of effect.

An excuba has an internal biomechanical Drift engine, allowing them to function as a Drift engine for a starship or vehicle they pilot as a 1-minute activity. An excuba can enter or exit the Drift themself as a 1-minute activity. When an excuba gets a critical failure on a Piloting check to Navigate or Plot a Course through the Drift, they get a failure instead.
The excuba senses creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

An excuba's visual appearance is altered when the excuba is perceived by creatures, items, and objects with the tech trait (such as robotic creatures, cameras, and sensors) unless they succeed at a @Check[perception|against:deception] check against the excuba's Deception DC. Excubas in their swarm form appear to be formless shadows and are additionally @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, while excubas in their humanoid forms register as familiar- or unremarkable-looking humanoids.
An excuba can store data within their body as if they were a computer. Hacking an excuba requires three successes against their Will DC to access their data.

The excuba can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

Frequency once per minute

Effect The excuba selects one creature with the tech trait in their space, searching its mind and memories. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and immune to Data Search for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The excuba accesses the creature's memories of the last 24 hours, downloading a holographic copy into their data vault and gaining insights into the creature's tactics. The creature must roll twice and take the lower result on its next attack roll, skill check, or saving throw against the excuba or one of the excuba's spells or abilities. This effect has the misfortune trait.

Critical Failure As failure, but the excuba also obtains a specific type of memory (such as what happened during the last time the creature encountered a Drift beacon) regardless of how long ago it was. The target creature loses this memory until it gains access to the excuba's data vault.

Each enemy creature in the swarm's space takes @Damage[2d8[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:glitching] save). On a failure, creatures and objects with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} on a critical failure).

languages.details: Arc network, Digital telepathy, Truespeech cant speak any language

details.publicNotes:
Unlike all other known types of spectra, excuba spectra are fragmented creatures made of countless tiny pieces that act together as a single networked whole. No accounts of an excuba encounter exist before the Drift crashed, and prevailing theories hold that either the trauma of the crisis created them, or they were created by the Drift as a response to the crisis itself. Whatever the case, excuba encounters have become all too common. These spectra swarms are often dispatched to infiltrate mortal worlds as spies or observers. They collect information for more powerful spectra like wyspirias to analyze and preserve. Some speculate that excubas have always lurked in the galaxy as invisible spies for other spectra, and that the Drift Crisis merely caused them to take a new, less cohesive form.

In the Great Beyond, time is measured not in hundreds, thousands, or millions of years, but rather in eons—in iterations of reality itself. Only rising to prominence within the last three centuries alongside the advent and proliferation of Drift technology, spectra have thrived in a burgeoning niche of the otherwise stagnant zeitgeist of the Great Beyond.

Though their origins and objectives remain shrouded in mystery, encounters with spectra revolve around new technological discoveries, sharing the knowledge of faster-than-light travel, and protecting the Drift. No one knows if spectra existed before the Drift, but their connection to the hyperspace plane is indisputable. Prominent metaphysicians believe spectra were created as amalgams of raw quintessence pulled into the Drift, just as the Drift itself grows from pieces of the Multiverse. If this is true, spectra's interest in the proliferation of Drift technology takes a subtle sinister turn, as each new Drift engine serves to harvest the Great Beyond and all its denizens for the growth of the Drift and spectra themselves.

description.value: The dust manta can feel the slightest disturbance in nearby sand, granting it the ability to sense other creatures moving through or on top of the same body of sand that it's in. In addition, the dust manta ignores @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} from sandstorms, and they're immune to being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} by sand or other grit.

The dust manta surges through sand to attack unwary prey. It Burrows up to twice its burrow Speed, then Leaps vertically up to 25 feet high, making a stinger Strike against a creature at any point during the jump (this lets it attack a creature within 35 feet of the surface). After the Leap, the dust manta falls to the ground but takes no damage from the fall if it lands on sand.
The dust manta's sand-colored hide allows it to [[/act hide]] in sand or any other fine, similarly colored substance even without cover or concealment.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:24]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[2d4[poison]] damage (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} (1 round)

Stage 5 death, and the victim's body disintegrates into dust and can only be brought back via a wish ritual or similarly powerful magic

The dust manta sprays a cloud of sand from its gills in a @Template[type:burst|distance:30]. All creatures in the cloud become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}, and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. The cloud lasts until the end of the dust manta's next turn. The dust manta can't use Spray Sand again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Spray Sand]]{1d4 rounds}. A non-dust manta creature that enters or starts its turn in the cloud must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24|options:area-effect,inflicts:blinded,inflicts:dazzled] save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute or until it spends an Interact action to wipe the dirt and grit from its eyes.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, after which it's dazzled for 1 minute or until it spends an Interact action to wipe its eyes.

details.publicNotes: Deadly stealth predators, dust mantas lurk just below the surface on desert worlds across the Universe. Despite a wingspan averaging 10 feet across and weighing 1 ton, dust mantas are nearly undetectable in their native habitat thanks to their pebbly, sand-colored hide. Like a ray through water, they can sense nearby vibrations and glide through sand flats and dunes to ambush unfortunate prey. Their wicked stinger injects a lethal toxin that breaks down flesh and bone, dissolving it into sand so filter-feeding dust mantas can strain particles of food from the resulting suspension.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]
A creature critically hit by an enraged noquasar's graviton flare is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target is flying, it falls up to 30 feet.

The enraged noquasar unleashes plasma in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] or @Template[type:line|distance:30]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[3d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). A creature that critically fails its save also takes @Damage[1d8[persistent,electricity]|options:area-damage] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The enraged noquasar can't use Ionized Plasma Blast again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ionized Plasma Blast]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
A noquasar's rage increases as its torture extends over many years.

When a solarian perishes while out of sync with the cycle of stars, their soul might become entangled with their solar mote. Extreme cosmic forces act on the soul as it attempts to reach the River of Souls, stretching it infinitely thin for all of eternity.

description.value:
Trigger The irradiated undead plant is reduced to 0 Hit Points by energy damage of any kind

Effect The energy acts as a catalyst for a nuclear reaction, causing the plant to explode and dealing @Damage[5d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save.
20 feet. A creature entering or starting its turn inside the aura is exposed to severe radiation.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The irradiated undead flyer Strides twice up to its Speed in a straight line, making a single wing slash Strike against all creatures whose spaces it flies adjacent to. All creatures damaged by the Strafing Snap are considered to be within the flyer's aura at the start of their next turn. The flyer can't use Strafing Snap again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Strafing Snap]]{1d4 rounds}.

Frequency once per minute

Effect The irradiated undead flyer channels energy into its wings, forcing all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] to attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage and is pushed back 5 feet.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[4d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage and is pushed back 10 feet.

details.publicNotes:
Flyer is a general term referring to winged creatures such as drakes, gigantic dragonflies, raptors, and other flying beasts that have been reanimated by severe radiation.

To survive in the unforgiving expanse of space, creatures must be naturally adapted or take protective measures. While the dead rarely need such precautions against these threats, sometimes radiation can animate a corpse (or several) to a state of undeath!

Scientists and religious scholars alike debate whether a specific type of radiation is to blame for this phenomenon that spontaneously triggers unlife, but all agree on the dangers such irradiated creatures pose. Many types of radiation could be the cause: intense bursts of cosmic radiation emitted by the stars, magically enhanced radioactive isotopes found in a planet's or moon's crust, or even the advent of new energy sources in a society (or its radioactive waste) contaminating the land. Regardless of their specific origin, irradiated undead all pose the same threat—they emit ionizing radiation that ravages everything around them.

description.value: A skarak's horn Strike bypasses the Hardness of structures made primarily of wood, stone, and metal.

A creature hit by a skarak's etheric web is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the skarak. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the maximum range of the skarak's etheric web. A creature can sever the etheric web with a Strike against AC 30 that deals at least 18 fire or force damage. This deals no damage to the skarak.

The skarak makes a horn Strike against each creature within reach. These attacks count toward the skarak's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the skarak makes all these attacks.

Large, @Damage[(3d8+6)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 24

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the skarak's etheric tether

Effect The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:33] save or be teleported to a space adjacent to the skarak of the skarak's choosing. This ends the grabbed condition from etheric tether unless the creature critically fails the save.

details.publicNotes:
The skarak is a ravenous mass of spiked chitin and teeth propelled with frightening speed by a collection of spindly, arachnid legs. It rampages across its territories, consuming everything smaller than itself and taking delight in the destruction it leaves in its wake. It's difficult to hide from a skarak, as it'll rip down fortifications and smash through concrete walls to get at prey cowering inside buildings. It's even more difficult to run from a skarak since it can spit a corrosive webbing that magically binds its prey to itself, allowing it to pull its prey across any distance to within reach of its waiting maw.

A skarak possesses a terrible cunning to complement its destructive might. It often watches a targeted settlement for days, analyzing defenses before launching a devastating offensive. It's typically a solitary creature except when training a newly hatched brood to hunt. During this period, it ranges ahead of its young, rampaging through densely populated areas and leaving wounded, weakened targets behind it. Survivors of the initial rampage soon discover they aren't the lucky ones as the skarak young come after, devouring all in their path.

description.value: An assembly ooze can detect creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

Over the course of 1 minute, the assembly ooze crafts a random piece of gear (rolling a [[/r 1d6]]: 1–armor, 2–shield, 3–weapon, 4–armor upgrade, 5–weapon upgrade, or 6–tech item) using the UPBs stored inside of it. An assembly ooze can craft items with a cost up to the UPBs stored, but with an item level and Bulk of no greater than 4 each.

The assembly ooze engulfs an adjacent, unattended piece of gear of no more than 4 Bulk, deconstructing it into its component parts. The ooze gains a number of UPBs equal to the item's price in credits. An assembly ooze can hold up to 1,000 UPBs at a time.

Requirements The assembly ooze's last action was a successful pseudopod Strike

Effect The ooze attempts to [[/act disarm options=sticky-disarm]] the creature it hit. It gains a +4 status bonus to the Athletics check. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the ooze's multiple attack penalty.

details.publicNotes:
The assembly ooze is believed to have been created by accident when a manufacturing company attempted to automate an object printer as a cost cutting measure. The original creature has long since left containment in the Pact Worlds, replicating itself across the cosmos. While often considered a nuisance akin to pests of yore, an assembly ooze left unchecked on a starship or space station can eventually turn into an existential threat.

An assembly ooze typically takes the form of an amorphous metallic cube. It has an outer shell composed of a liquid polycarbonate slurry that encases a core of replicating nanobots. The ooze's color can change based on the materials it breaks down, but it's most often a metallic amalgamation of brown or gray. To survive, the ooze attempts to constantly disassemble technological items in following its programmed subroutines then manufacture something new. The nanites within an assembly ooze do have a drive for reproduction, splitting in a process similar to cellular mitosis when the ooze absorbs enough material in a short period of time. Assembly oozes generally don't assault those not carrying the technological items they require as fuel but do lash out to protect themselves.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

items.name: Interplanar Teleport (at will; self only; to and from Akashic Record and the Universe only)

description.value:
Requirements You have a planar key for the destination plane, used as a locus

You and your allies traverse the barriers between planes of existence. The targets move to another plane, such as the Plane of Fire, the Netherworld, or the Outer Rifts. You must know the destination plane exists and use a magic planar key created from material from that plane as a locus for the spell. While the planar keys for most prominent planes are uncommon, just like the spell interplanar teleport, more obscure planes and demiplanes often have rare or possibly even unique planar keys.

The spell is highly imprecise, and you appear 1d20×25 miles from the last place one of the targets (of your choice) was located the last time that target traveled to the plane. If it's the first time traveling to a particular plane for all targets, you appear at a random location on the plane. Interplanar teleport doesn't provide a means of return travel, though casting interplanar teleport again allows you to return to your previous plane unless there are extenuating circumstances.

You create an @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} floating eye at a location within range (even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect). The eye can't move, but you can see in all directions from that point as if using your normal visual senses.

You befuddle your target with strange impulses, causing it to act randomly. The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target babbles incoherently and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.

Critical Failure The target is Confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You execute a psychic search of the Akashic Record for information about a specific topic and magically transfer this data to your comm unit or computer. When you Cast this Spell, choose any skill in which you're trained that has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} action.

Any time during the duration, you can consult your comm unit while attempting a Recall Knowledge check using your chosen skill. This check gains a +1 status bonus. Checking your comm unit is part of the action to Recall Knowledge. You must be holding the comm unit to do so. The comm unit instantly compiles and displays snippets and selections from various media relevant to the topic, which grants you the bonus. If you roll a critical failure on this check, you get a failure instead. If the check is successful and the subject is a creature, you gain additional information or context about the creature. Once you reference the comm unit, the spell ends. When the spell ends, your access to the Akashic Record ends, and all the information you downloaded returns to the Akashic Record.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.h1i8c47U0IOrMrHJ]{Spell Effect: Akashic Download}

Heightened (3rd) You can reference your comm unit three times before the spell ends.

system.requirements: You have a comm unit or computer, used as a locus.

description.value: Instead of using their own memories, an Akashic dragon can ascertain information from the Akashic Records. This gives it the Akashic Record Lore skill, which it can use to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} of any kind.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AllAroundVision]

items.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Occult

description.value:
90 feet. The swirl of constant uploads and downloads near the Akashic dragon make attempts to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} unpredictable. A creature other than an akashic dragon that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23|options:area-effect] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's Akashic interference for 1 minute.

Critical Success For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it gains a +3 status bonus to its check.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure For 1 round, when the creature attempts to Recall Knowledge, it takes a –3 status penalty to its check.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.

Trigger The dragon is critically hit by an attack

Effect The young Akashic dragon raises a web of deflecting lasers using the eyes embedded all over their body, gaining resistance 8 to all damage against the triggering attack and absorbing some of the blow's energy, recharging their Akashic Barrage. If the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check against the creature in the last minute, they gain resistance 11 instead.
The Akashic dragon can activate magic and tech items with the Cast a Spell activation of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells.

The dragon condenses cutting truths and fiery revelations from the Akashic Record into sizzling laser fire that they project as a cone from their primary eye to assail their foes. This deals @Damage[10d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:stupefied] save). If a creature critically fails its Reflex save against Akashic Barrage and the Akashic dragon has succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about the creature in the last minute, the creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round. The dragon can't use Akashic Barrage again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Akashic Barrage]]{1d4 rounds}.
The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Akashic Barrage whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

The first time each encounter the Akashic dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if they'd succeeded at a check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about that creature.

details.publicNotes: Though rarely encountered outside their crumbling, relic-filled lairs, Akashic dragons occasionally live in hubs of knowledge, such as bustling space stations or cities. These dragons, bestowed with a direct connection to the Akashic Record, are arrogant curators of knowledge, assuming (often correctly) that no other being could possibly have a deeper understanding of the universe's secrets. Akashic dragons spend extensive time in the Akashic Record, often emerging after what they perceive as many days while mere hours have passed for the rest of the galaxy.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

A cybernetic zombie's cybernetics can be disabled for 1 hour by a creature that succeeds at a DC 15 check to [[/act disable-device dc=15]] or Disable a System. Attempting to do so can typically be done only while within reach of the zombie and is a 2-action activity that has the manipulate trait. The cybernetics are also disabled for 1 hour if the zombie takes any electricity damage. A cybernetic zombie with disabled cybernetics is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use any reactions.

A cybernetic zombie must use this reaction unless its cybernetics are disabled

Trigger The cybernetic zombie is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The zombie's damaged cybernetics explode, dealing @Damage[1d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect] save). This ability destroys the zombie's integrated items and any tech items on the zombie of 1st level or lower.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

details.publicNotes:
The most common form of tech zombie is the cybernetic zombie, but tech zombies can also arise through other types of technologies, including nanites.

Technological zombies are created when a dead creature is reanimated (or otherwise kept moving) by an internal or integrated technological item—usually an augmentation. Despite the vast differences in form and hardware between different tech zombies, they do have similarities—they're mindless undead driven to harm the living, with technological components and weaknesses that can be leveraged.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

The edge of this precision-crafted foil is sharpened to the thickness of a single atom, using regenerating nanites to maintain itself after countless strikes.

Upgrades: 1

This pistol automatically ejects used cartridges and loads new ammunition after every shot fired.

Upgrades: 1

This sniper rifle comes with a compound technological scope that allows snipers to adjust focus and magnification with natural eye movements instead of using moving parts that might disrupt their aim.

Trigger The target of the elimination-class assassin robot's Target Tracking attempts a Strike against the robot

Effect The robot reads its target's movements, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering Strike.

The circuitry of an assassin robot self-destructs upon the robot's destruction, making tracing its owner extremely difficult. Add 10 to the DC of a Computers check to recover data from a destroyed assassin robot.

Frequency once per hour

Effect The elimination-class assassin robot becomes invisible for 10 minutes. If the robot uses a hostile action or moves more than half its Speed in a single action, the robot becomes visible again after it completes that action.

Frequency once per day

Effect The elimination-class assassin robot restores @Damage[45[healing]] Hit Points to one technological creature or item.

The elimination-class assassin robot can Interact to draw or retract any of its integrated weapons as a free action. An observer must succeed at a @Check[perception|dc:40] check to notice a retracted weapon.
The elimination-class assassin robot's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

The elimination-class assassin robot establishes a target lock on a creature it can see within 120 feet. The creature can't become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} against the robot or [[/act create-a-diversion]] against the robot. This ability lasts until the robot loses line of sight with the target or the target otherwise becomes hidden.

details.publicNotes:
Elegant and deadly, elimination models rely upon their stealth fields to keep foes guessing and strike where least expected. Their advanced intelligence gives them the capacity to set up more complex ambushes as well as react to changes in the situation as needed and handle requests from the client. This could mean making the killing as subtle or as gruesome as possible, depending on the intent and message intended.

In some societies, machines automate many processes. It only makes sense, then, that some would attempt to automate the act of murder. Most manufacturers would never admit to creating such designs, forcing wealthy figures to go through hidden channels to acquire units from secret facilities or unscrupulous manufacturers. Regardless of their origins, these robots can be programmed with a target and set loose, the owner assured that either their target will be dead or will be unable to prove who tried to kill them thanks to the unit's fried circuitry.

description.value:
The tashtari causes their filaments to glow with intense light. Non-tashtari creatures within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save. A creature that attempts this save is immune to all Bristle Flashes for 1 minute. The tashtari's fur then dims, and they can't use this ability until they bask in sunlight for at least 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is dazzled for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.

details.publicNotes: Native to the green world of Castrovel, the tashtari has become known throughout the wider galaxy as the laser wolf. Wolflike pack hunters, tashtaris have a layer of flexible filaments that emit phosphorescent lights and allow them to silently communicate through a bespoke visual language. Each tashtari possesses a node within their throat that allows them to channel their stored solar energy and unleash a beam of focused light, giving them their moniker of laser wolves. A mating pair, consisting of one or two alphas, leads packs of tashtaris, who generally become the apex predators of large areas with one or more forested regions. The presence of trees is important for the nocturnal tashtaris, who sleep on the treetops during the day to bask in sunlight and recharge their solar energies.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

Coming in a variety of styles, these traditional and time-tested weapons have been honed with advanced smithing techniques and modern materials.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d4[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 2d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical 5 feet

These grenades use an ignited chemical to explode in a blast of intense heat and deal 2d8 fire damage with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save (@Template[burst|distance:10]). If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, creatures who critically fail their saving throw against incendiary grenades are knocked away from the grenade.

Upgrades: 1

These dependable, mass-produced rifles shoot concentrated beams of energy out of square-shaped barrels. The universal standard Pact Worlds model is highly modifiable and designed to function with a variety of accessories and upgrades, with wielders adapting the laser rifle to their preferences and even kit-bashing it into a nearly unrecognizable weapon.

Upgrades: 1

A projector fastened to a belt or lapel encases the wearer's body in this shimmering hardlight shell. Military-issue hardlight armor follows a predictable standard, but private users enjoy a range of customizable fashion options.
@Damage[1d4[bleed]] on critical failure
Pushed 5 feet on critical failure

Trigger The armiger takes fire damage

Effect The armiger absorbs a fraction of the fire to empower their attacks. Until the end of their next turn, their Strikes deal an additional @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage.

You have four arms, which allows you to wield and hold up to four hands' worth of weapons and equipment. At any time, one pair of hands is designated as your active hands. You can change this designation from one pair of hands to another by taking the Switch Hands action, which is a single action. You can wield items only with your active hands.
The armiger draws two grenades, then Area Fires both grenades. Creatures in the area of both grenades take a –1 circumstance penalty to their saving throws against both grenades.

details.publicNotes:
Hellknight armigers are the lowest-ranked Hellknights, though attaining this rank requires them to spend six years as an initiate and then pass the Hellknight Test, during which they must summon, combat, and defeat a fiend of Hell.

Hellknights are infernal knights, and while they once ruled entire nations on Lost Golarion, the Hellknights of the Pact Worlds enforce law and order more holistically, traveling in massive citadel starships to spread their influence rather than remaining in any one place. In battle, Hellknights are orderly, unrelenting, and deadly.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This imposing pistol fires a jolt of paralyzing electricity from its barrel. Its remaining charges are visible as glowing lines of energy radiating down its body. Pulsecaster pistols are often used by bounty hunters and law enforcement who need to keep their targets alive.

Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the security robot with an attack

Effect The robot overclocks its processing power to attempt to avoid an attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.

details.publicNotes:
Programmed to patrol small areas, an observer-class security robot is akin to a watch dog, but with a built-in gun. Some manufacturers design these robots with canine forms, and their capability to fly can surprise would-be burglars who were expecting a more terrestrial defense.

Security robots are ubiquitous throughout industrialized space and have major presences in both the Pact Worlds and Veskarium, employed and customized by corporations and private citizens alike.

While most soldier robot models are built to be as destructive as possible, and elimination robots are designed to kill, security robots usually have fail-safes programmed into their behavior to prevent them from causing any more violence than is necessary to perform their designated security function. This makes them especially useful in cities, where unintended destruction might harm bystanders or cause legal issues, and even on starships, where a stray laser blast might cause serious damage to vital starship systems.

description.value:
Bending the laws of the Universe to your will, you increase the forces of gravity in an area. All flying creatures in the area descend 40 feet. The entire area is greater difficult terrain for Flying and Climbing creatures, and difficult terrain for creatures on the ground. Any creature pulled to the ground by a gravity field takes bludgeoning damage as though it had fallen.

Gravity in the area increases by one step (for example, zero-g becomes low gravity, low gravity becomes standard, standard gravity becomes high gravity, and high gravity becomes extreme gravity).

You supercharge the targets' electromagnetism and pull them with a tether of lightning. Make a ranged spell attack against each target. If you attack two targets, both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after both attacks. The targets each take 4d8 electricity damage and are pulled 5 feet closer to you, or 10 feet closer on a critical hit. If both attacks hit, you can choose to move the creatures closer to one another instead of closer to you. Moving a target into a barrier, creature, or obstacle causes the target to stop before entering that space.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You alter gravity's effects on you. You treat gravity as if it were your choice of one step higher or lower (for example, if you were in an area of low gravity, you could choose either standard gravity or zero-g). While the spell is active, you reduce the distance of any forced movement that would affect you by 5 feet (minimum 0 feet), and you ignore being clumsy and untethered because of gravity effects on you. You can Dismiss the spell.

You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

You create an illusion that causes the target to appear more charming and genteel to observers. The spell accentuates the target's appearance, clothing, voice, and mannerisms according to what an observer finds appealing. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

The target gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression} and lets the target add its level to Diplomacy checks if untrained. In addition, the target can attempt a Diplomacy check to Gather Information in only 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hOp5ybOzyJlECU6B]{Spell Effect: Glow Up}

Heightened (+2) The bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th rank.

A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

While on their bonded celestial body, a nymph can sense approaching starships within a day's travel from the celestial body. This doesn't give them any information about the ship, its occupants, or its exact position. The nymph can send a message to the ship as if using a personal comm unit.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ConstantSpells]

A gwereiad is mystically bonded to a single black hole. If they're away from their bonded black hole for more than 24 hours, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save each additional 24 hours or increase their drained value by 1. A gwereiad can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond themself to a new black hole.

30 feet. The area around a gwereiad inexorably draws things toward its center. Creatures entering or starting their turns inside the aura must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save or be pulled 5 feet toward the gwereiad (10 feet on a critical failure).

The gwereiad summons a gravitational collapse around a creature within 30 feet, dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage. The affected creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature's body crumples under the gravity, and it takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} by the gravitational collapse. It takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bludgeoning]] damage until it [[/act escape dc=29]]{Escapes}.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature takes double damage and the persistent damage is 4d6.

details.publicNotes:
Gwereiads are aloof and enigmatic nymphs who keep to themselves, nestling deep inside the phenomena known as black holes. Whether their solitary nature is an active preference or simply a consequence of their homes is up for debate, as few creatures can brave the gravitational forces of a black hole long enough to make contact with gwereiads. They're rarely hostile unless provoked and occasionally even rescue starships at risk of being devoured by their homes.

Gwereiads adore art and music, though their tastes tend toward the strange and avant-garde. While most nymphs aren't overtly religious, gwereiads devote themselves to esoteric spirituality focused on space-time.

Nymphs are beautiful fey who appear as humanoids with elven features. They generally take female forms, but nymphs of all genders and presentations exist. While many nymphs live on planets and act as protectors of natural ponds, forests, caves, and cliffsides, other nymphs—often called space nymphs—are known for their association with celestial bodies. Like their planet-bound kin, space nymphs guard their bonded territories from exploitation or harm.

As admirers of the arts, nymphs are especially friendly toward creatives, and offering to depict a nymph in story, picture, or song is a sure way to win their favor—unless the nymph doesn't like the final result and lashes out at the artist for insulting them with a mediocre product. Vidcasting and livestreaming are popular pastimes among more sociable nymphs, whether to show off their remote environments or find audiences for performances. Some nymphs entice movie crews to film in their territories, with two provisos: first, that the production not damage the environment, and second, that the nymph receive a prominent role in the film.

Unless their territories are threatened, most space nymphs are friendly or indifferent to mortals, but they have long memories. Anyone visiting a space nymph's territory would be wise to research that nymph's grudges, past encounters with mortals, or any recent events in the area that might color the nymph's perspective of those traveling near their home.

A mortal who demonstrates the right attitude and abilities, such as by protecting a stellar body from destruction or creating an especially beautiful work of art, might draw a space nymph's attention. From time to time, space nymphs will offer their services as mentors to solarians or mystics, though they seldom seek pupils on their own. Space nymphs rarely enter into more than one such arrangement at a time and typically only make commitments that promise to last decades or centuries, so they're extremely selective in their choice of mentee.

description.value:
Trigger The zalyop is reduced to 0 Hit Points by physical or fire damage

Effect The zalyop explodes, dealing @Damage[4d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:20] centered on itself (@Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save). This also triggers a Befuddling Cloud, even if it's been used in the last hour.

Frequency once per hour

Effect The zalyop expels a gaseous cloud in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Creatures within the cloud must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:18] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure). Confused creatures that are also engulfed by the zalyop don't receive flat checks to end the confused condition.

@Check[reflex|dc:18], @Damage[2d6[acid]], [[/act escape dc=18]], Rupture 7

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Engulf]

details.publicNotes:
The jet streams of the gas giant Bretheda are filled with large, bulbous silhouettes called zalyops, sometimes referred to as balloon plants. Gas-filled and hardy, they're buffeted around by the planet's eternal storms. It's common for a cascade of these plants to be found trailing in the wake of an oma or plastered against one of the many spacecrafts and aircrafts frequenting the gas giant.

The sound of zalyops bouncing against a metal hull at high velocity can be a humorous one, but travelers should be careful not to assume these plants are harmless. Zalyops are known to expel high-speed organic shrapnel by reflex. Additionally, their confusion-inducing gaseous waste easily spreads across the atmosphere and can be caught in a ship's air-intake valve.

Zalyops can also cause chaos at ground level. When they float into small settlements, flocks of them will descend on small creatures, which they engulf for nutrients. The widespread befuddling clouds caused by such incursions are a dangerous nuisance. Pedestrians and pets alike must take care during such events to stay inside and simply wait the invasive balloons out.

Pact Worlds chemists have developed (and patented) Balyoon, an anesthetic drug from zalyop gases. In addition to being difficult to obtain in the first place, the drug is highly unstable until refined in the laboratory, and more than one starship has been lost when its crew failed to properly store the gases they just harvested from a zalyop.

Balyoon also plays a role in Eoxian soap operas and other dramatic vidshows across the Pact Worlds. It's used as a plot device in a myriad of ways, such as a villain contriving to discredit a hero or for a family member to suddenly lose their mind or memory in a soap opera. How these characters obtained the processed gas of a predatory Brethedan plant is rarely as important as the exciting drama, however.

description.value:
A willing creature you touch becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} to everyone except a second target within the spell's range. If the invisible creature uses a hostile action against anyone who isn't the target that can see them, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

Heightened (5th) You can make up to 6 willing creatures within 30 feet invisible, but they all must have the same target.

target.value: 1 willing creature touched and one within range

description.value:
You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

The target's form appears blurry. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}. As the nature of this effect still leaves the target's location obvious, the target can't use this concealment to Hide or Sneak.

Blessings from beyond help your companions strike true. You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls while within the emanation. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet.

Bless can counteract @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.7ZinJNzxq0XF0oMx]{Bane}.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.RfCEHpMoEAZvB9IZ]{Aura: Bless}

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You vent heat toward a nearby target. You deal 2d6 fire damage to the target. On a failed save, the target's held equipment becomes hot to the touch for the next round. Unless the target drops its currently held equipment at the start of its turn, it takes @Damage[(3*@item.rank)[fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6. Increase the damage of touching affected objects by 3.

You release a surge of magical energy that temporarily changes the type of damage dealt by a weapon. The next Strike the weapon makes deals your choice of acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage and deals an additional 1d6 extra damage of the same type as the weapon's normal damage.

Heightened (+2) The extra damage increases by 1d6.
