This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AllAroundVision]
A jinsul pontiff can channel vitality to up to four bonded allies as though they were a mystic. This network stores up to @Damage[20[healing]] Hit Points and begins combat full.

Frequency once per round

Effect The jinsul pontiff restores @Damage[6[healing]] Hit Points to their vitality network, up to its maximum.
The jinsul Strides and then performs a [[/act long-jump]]. They can Stride less than 10 feet and not automatically fail their check to Long Jump.
The jinsul pontiff transfers any number of Hit Points from their vitality network to a visible bonded target.

details.publicNotes:
Pontiffs rally jinsuls to a near suicidal frenzy in the throes of combat. Part magic wielder and part orator, they act as inspirational battlefield leaders. Each serves one of the major jinsul hierarchs, who in turn act as the leaders of the overall Jinsul Hierocracy.

Jinsuls are a warlike species scattered across the Vast. Six-legged creatures with a bulbous body covered in eyes, jinsuls have notable segments of chitin along with a set of upper forelimbs for using equipment. They long ago lived in the region of space known as the Scoured Stars but eventually departed and settled on the harsh world of Rax. In recent years, the divine herald Dhurus unified the jinsuls into a cohesive force known as the Jinsul Hierocracy, which briefly retook the Scoured Stars. A joint alliance between several species and members of the Starfinder Society defeated Dhurus and scattered the jinsuls back into the Vast.

With countless bases and fleets operating in deep space, jinsuls mostly act as raiders, scavenging for equipment and testing their mettle against other species. Indoctrinated to view themselves as a superior species, with only other inhabitants of the Scoured Stars having any value, jinsuls prefer to engage in short-ranged combat with their foes.

Jinsuls aren't numerous enough to fill every need on the many ships and starbases they control across the galaxy. Instead, they rely on robots and automated computer systems (always commanded by at least one jinsul officer) to keep their space stations running efficiently. These systems are programmed to be just as deadly and ruthless as jinsuls themselves; even the most innocuous automated system or robot, like a garbage collector, might wield deadly weapons or contain vicious technomagical countermeasures.

description.value: A necrograft zombie's necrografts can be disabled for 1 hour by a creature that succeeds at a @Check[religion|dc:22] check. Attempting to do so can typically be done only while within reach of the zombie, and is a 2-action activity that has the manipulate trait. A necrograft zombie with disabled necrografts is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use any reactions.

A necrograft zombie must use this reaction unless its necrograft is disabled

Trigger The zombie is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The zombie rapidly decomposes into a heap of rotting biomass, dealing @Damage[2d8[poison],2d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save) and making that area hazardous terrain for 1 minute (@Damage[1d4[void]|options:area-damage] damage). This ability destroys any organic items on the zombie (including necrografts).

10 feet. @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened,inflicts:slowed]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Stench]
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

@Damage[(1d8+9)[bludgeoning]], @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]
The crest-eater necrograft zombie ignores difficult terrain caused by rubble, sand, and uneven ground made of earth and stone.

details.publicNotes:
Necrograft zombies are animated by necrografts created from the body parts and internal organs of undead creatures. On Eox, it's common to augment animals and beasts with necrografts to ensure their survival on the dead planet. After death, many of these creatures return as true undead to continue serving their creators.

Technological zombies are created when a dead creature is reanimated (or otherwise kept moving) by an internal or integrated technological item—usually an augmentation. Despite the vast differences in form and hardware between different tech zombies, they do have similarities—they're mindless undead driven to harm the living, with technological components and weaknesses that can be leveraged.

description.value:
You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You sap the target's strength, depending on its Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} for 1 minute.

You fire a shard of solidified magic toward a creature that you can see. It automatically hits and deals 1d4+1 force damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, increase the number of shards you shoot by one, to a maximum of three shards for 3 actions. You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

Heightened (+2) You fire one additional shard with each action you spend.

You manifest an injury from the future or past to harm a creature in the present. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The target takes 1d8 of the selected damage type with a basic Will save. If the target fails its save and then takes the same type of damage before the end of your next turn, it takes an additional @Damage[ceil((@item.level)/2)d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a failure increases by 1d4.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You focus on the target object, opening your mind to perceive magical auras. When the casting is complete, you know whether that item is magical. You or anyone you advise about the aura gains a +2 circumstance bonus to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.eReSHVEPCsdkSL4G]{Identify Magic} on the item. If the object is illusory, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your read aura spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.J6OhRNSN23KzOPRL]{Spell Effect: Read Aura}

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to 10 objects.

Heightened (6th) You can target any number of objects.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

You call upon the Void to harm life force. The target takes 2d4 void damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

60 feet

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Telepathy]

Elindrians can communicate nearly instantaneously via a primal bond similar to a formian hive mind. While within telepathic range of at least one other elindrian with husk mind, an elindrian acolyte gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls, Perception checks, and saving throws against mental effects. If one elindrian is aware of a combatant, all members of the husk mind within range are aware of it.

The elindrian's husk mind is integrated with their scent; for purposes of detecting other elindrians, formians, undead, or corpses, their scent is a precise sense, rather than imprecise.

An elindrian always knows the precise distance and direction to the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.alien-core-bestiary.Actor.BFtNnevWKUCnocyn]{Terror of Ukulam}, so long as they're on the same plane of existence.

While the elindrian acolyte is within 30 feet of another elindrian who's unable to move or act normally (such as if they're buried, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, stunned, or otherwise impeded), the elindrian acolyte becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} 1 and can use the extra action to Burrow, Fly, [[/act shove]], Stride, or Strike.

details.publicNotes:
These elindrians are the rank-and-file worshippers of the Terror of Ukulam. Recently risen from the dead by some primal force or occult upheaval—no one is sure—they single-mindedly pursue their grisly task of unearthing fellow elindrians across Castrovel; many others have risen from the dead, but most still remain buried. They stop only to attack other living creatures that intervene.

The people known as elindrians vanished eons ago into desolate husk-hives beneath the surface of the planet Castrovel. Until recently, they were creatures of legend, existing only in the most obscure formian histories. Now, scholars on Castrovel desperately search for additional records about elindrians, their origins, and—most importantly—the truth about their connection to the terrifying ooze-like creature that recently descended from their moon, Elindrae. One theory is that elindrians share some primal bond with the ooze-thing called the Terror of Ukulam, and perhaps both originate from Elindrae—other theories claim that elindrians created the ooze in the first place or that they're distant cousins to formians, corrupted by the Terror long ago.

description.value:
Requirements You have a planar key for the destination plane, used as a locus

You and your allies traverse the barriers between planes of existence. The targets move to another plane, such as the Plane of Fire, the Netherworld, or the Outer Rifts. You must know the destination plane exists and use a magic planar key created from material from that plane as a locus for the spell. While the planar keys for most prominent planes are uncommon, just like the spell interplanar teleport, more obscure planes and demiplanes often have rare or possibly even unique planar keys.

The spell is highly imprecise, and you appear 1d20×25 miles from the last place one of the targets (of your choice) was located the last time that target traveled to the plane. If it's the first time traveling to a particular plane for all targets, you appear at a random location on the plane. Interplanar teleport doesn't provide a means of return travel, though casting interplanar teleport again allows you to return to your previous plane unless there are extenuating circumstances.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You channel the bizarre powers that exist beyond the outer reaches of the universe, dealing 3d12 mental damage to each creature in the area as phantasmal claws, jaws, and tentacles lash out at each target. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. The area is difficult terrain for any creature sickened by the effects of this spell.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

You reach into the target's mind, extract a specific memory you have some awareness of, and project it into a hologram. Alternatively, when casting this spell, you can choose to pull a random memory left to the GM's discretion. The holographic memory can be no longer than 5 minutes; if the extracted memory is longer than this, the hologram simply ends at that point. The hologram includes audio as well as visual components, but it doesn't contain haptic, olfactory, or other components. The hologram typically plays a memory that takes place in a single scene, though it can cut to multiple scenes if they're accurate to the memory and its length allows. The hologram plays the memory exactly as the target experienced it and from the target's perspective, so it often lacks context.

This spell can target any living, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or deceased sentient creature with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher. If the target is deceased, it can't have been dead for longer than 1 week, or this spell fails; once a corpse has been subjected to holographic memory by any caster, any subsequent castings of holographic memory fail.

If the target is unwilling to share this memory, it can attempt a Will save to resist this spell.

You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

You magically broadcast grim news onto nearby devices, holo projectors, screens, and other displays (including comm units on armor). Creatures in the area who can see one or more displays must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the display.

Failure The creature is fascinated by the display and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (even if the fascination ends).

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated by the display, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (even if the fascination ends).

You channel your life force into a spear of energy. You lose 6 Hit Points or, if you're in a bond with a vitality network that has at least 12 Hit Points, you can reduce the vitality bond's Hit Points by 12 instead. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d6 spirit damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage dealt by 2d6 and the Hit Points lost by 2 (or by 4 if you spend Hit Points from a vitality network).

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You transport yourself to either a celestial plane or a world in the Universe where worshippers of your patron can be found. You must be of no higher level than double angelic messenger's spell rank.

Critical Success As success, but if you've never visited that plane or world before, you appear right in front of the message's intended recipient.

Success You arrive on the target plane or world at the last location you visited on that plane or world, or to a random location within [[/r 10d10 #Miles Off]] miles of your message's intended recipient if you've never visited that plane or world before.

Failure You don't travel.

Critical Failure You accidentally travel to the wrong plane, possibly a dangerous plane.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be an angel)

description.value:
A spinning disk or smooth orb of shimmering steel leaps from one target to another. You deal 2d4 bludgeoning or slashing damage (with a basic Reflex save).

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

Upgrades: 1

Coming in a variety of styles, these traditional and time-tested weapons have been honed with advanced smithing techniques and modern materials.

Upgrades: 1

These dependable, mass-produced rifles shoot concentrated beams of energy out of square-shaped barrels. The universal standard Pact Worlds model is highly modifiable and designed to function with a variety of accessories and upgrades, with wielders adapting the laser rifle to their preferences and even kit-bashing it into a nearly unrecognizable weapon.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ConstantSpells]
20 feet. All creatures within the angel's aura gain a +1 status bonus to their AC and saving throws against attacks and effects from creatures and hazards with the unholy trait.

The barachius makes a digital dagger Strike against an adjacent creature or item that has the tech trait.

On a hit, the target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25|options:inflicts:controlled,inflicts:dazzled,inflicts:stunned] saving throw as the angel's holy influence attempts to seize control over it. A target with the holy trait use the outcome one degree of success better than the result of its roll, while one with the unholy trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target's vision is filled with holy sigils and code, giving it the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} condition until the end of its next turn.

Failure As success, and the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The target's core processes are seized by the angel, giving the target the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled} condition for 1 minute. While the target is controlled by the barachius, the angel can also use any of their spells as if it originated from the target rather than the angel. The target can attempt a new saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the controlled condition.
In the hands of a barachius angel, all weapons gain the holy and magical traits and are powered by divine will, negating the need for batteries or ammunition.

details.publicNotes:
Barachius angels are imposing, helmed figures with glowing wings made of digital circuitry. They monitor the development of technology to prevent it from being used for malevolent purposes. Toward this divine end, their armaments have been blessed with holy energy. They can produce a retractable data blade that extends from their hand and that can be used to interface universally with computer terminals and data ports on robots.

Barachius angels serve as divine vanquishers to benevolent gods—purging technology across the Universe deemed to be twisted or evil. Their philosophies on what constitutes evil can be unclear, as these angels might hunt down an individual malfunctioning robot yet leave nuclear missile silos untouched.

While many angels in modern times ascended from mortal souls across the cosmos, these celestial beings were among the first creatures to exist in all the cosmos. Warriors and messengers of the gods, they're utterly devoted to their cause, whether that be destroying evil or spreading a deity's message across the galaxy.

description.value: The apari's carapace has Hardness 10, reducing any damage the apari takes by that amount. Once the apari is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon taking damage from a critical hit, the carapace breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its AC to 20. An apari with a broken carapace gains resistance 5 to precision damage and weakness 5 to both area damage and splash damage. It also gains the Swarming Bites aura.

10 feet. The constituents of an apari with a broken carapace angrily swarm around its body. A creature that begins its turn in this aura takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. A creature that fails this save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

Can alternatively switch to @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] or @Damage[4d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] with the Fungible ability.

Requirements The apari's carapace isn't broken

Effect The apari releases a bevy of tiny, flying constituents in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that sting all within. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[4d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save.
The apari Seeks by dispersing constituents to scan a wide area, attempting to find hidden or undetected creatures within 90 feet.

The apari's constituents can change their physiology, taking advantage of an opponent's weakness. The apari alters the type of damage it deals with its claw attack (which also affects the type of persistent damage), its Constituent Sortie ability, or its swarming bites aura, choosing from the following: bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.

details.publicNotes:
An apari is a living hive beholden to its beetle-like queen, whose massive carapace houses generations of its insect brood. Millions of constituents across various developmental stages animate the exoskeleton of a single apari. At the direction of their queen's chemical signals, individual constituents, most no larger than a grain of rice, adapt to the hive's current needs, assuming diverse forms to fulfill specialized roles and supporting its gestalt biological functions.

Once introduced within an ecosystem, an apari asserts itself rapidly. Winged constituents forage for food as the hive itself hunts living prey, tearing its victims open with sharp claws while grub-like constituents writhe into fresh wounds and consume the prey from within. When an apari is threatened, combat-ready drones selflessly rush to defend the hive or to cover its escape.

description.value:
You unleash a cloud of corrosive nanites. Creatures that start their turn in the area take 6d8 acid damage and 1d6 persistent acid damage with a basic Fortitude save. The nanites also corrode any unattended objects in the area, dealing 3d8 acid damage. Each round you Sustain the spell, you can move the cloud up to 10 feet in any direction. You can Dismiss the spell.

Heightened (+1) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

You flash freeze a target then send subsonic waves through it, potentially reducing it into fine powder. Make a spell attack against the target. If you hit a target, the target takes 6d8 cold damage and must attempt a Fortitude save. If you critically hit, the target gets a result one degree of success worse than it rolled on its Fortitude save. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points is blasted to fine powder; its gear remains.

Critical Success The target is unaffected by further damage.

Success The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level -3)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Failure The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Critical Failure The target takes an additional @Damage[((@item.level +6)d8)[sonic]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The cold and sonic damage each increase by 1d8.

You flood the area with a wave of electrical interference. You deal 7d8 electricity damage to creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You emit a pulsating mental blast that penetrates the minds of all enemies in the area. Each enemy in the area must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is Stunned for 1 round.

You instantly transport yourself and any items you're wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space within range you can see. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an extradimensional container—the spell is lost.

Heightened (5th) The range increases to 1 mile. You don't need to be able to see your destination, as long as you have been there in the past and know its relative location and distance from you. You are then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

target.value: 1 creature or object with the tech trait

description.value:
Your touch installs a dangerous self-replicating virus that disrupts a technological creature's programming. The target must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure The target is glitching 1 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

The deh-nolo senses a creature's mental essence as a precise sense with the listed range; they can't sense mindless creatures with thoughtsense.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Thoughtsense]

items.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

description.value: If a deh-nolo takes 60 damage from a critical hit or takes 50 mental damage, they must succeed at a DC 34 save (@Check[fortitude|dc:34] for critical damage or @Check[will|dc:34] for mental damage) or one of their brain sacs is destroyed.

A deh-nolo snatcher has four brain sacs that they use to store harvested brains. A deh-nolo knows one additional language for each brain they have stored in a brain sac, chosen from the languages that the creature knew when its brain was harvested. A deh-nolo without all four sacs full is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied} with a value equal to the number of empty sacs.
When the deh-nolo takes damage, pustules on their body burst, spraying foul poison. Each adjacent creature is exposed to dominion bile.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:34]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[4d6[poison]] damage, enfeebled 1, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[6d6[poison]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} (1 round)

Stage 4 death
The deh-nolo harvests the brain of a creature that's been dead for no more than 1 minute. They can then use an Interact action to secure the brain in one of their empty brain sacs.

languages.details: Any four others (see brain sacs), Telepathy 120 feet

details.publicNotes:
Deh-nolos are the Dominion's foremost engineers, seamlessly and deviously merging technology and biological systems to create any device imaginable, from sinister weapons to massive starships to shipmind oozes. These creations are grown, not built, but they're neither cybernetics nor biotech—rather, something in between. Deh-nolos seek to understand all technology they encounter, and they believe it's their right to consume the brain of anyone they think might help them better understand an unfamiliar device. Then again, deh-nolos have no qualms about consuming brains for nearly any reason, seeing all forms of life outside the Dominion as lesser beings.

While mostly organic, deh-nolos can concentrate their shimmering liquid metal secretions into foul crystals that they can use as projectiles or mechanical components for their twisted inventions. A casual onlooker might assume these creations are biotechnological in nature, as they often look like atrophied organs and mutated body parts held together by seemingly crude or incomplete circuitry. Those who attempt to incorporate deh-nolo technology into their bodies as augmentations typically experience terrible pain and grisly deaths, even if they somehow manage to isolate or drain the toxins naturally produced by a deh-nolo's alien flesh. Even less intrusive experiments with deh-nolo technology are unpredictable and dangerous, and the results of a "successful" outcome are disturbing at best.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Upgrades: 1

These dependable, mass-produced rifles shoot concentrated beams of energy out of square-shaped barrels. The universal standard Pact Worlds model is highly modifiable and designed to function with a variety of accessories and upgrades, with wielders adapting the laser rifle to their preferences and even kit-bashing it into a nearly unrecognizable weapon.

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect The line-class soldier robot salvages an adjacent corpse of a tech creature that was destroyed no more than 1 month ago to regain @Damage[18[healing]] Hit Points.

When a line-class soldier robot is adjacent to at least two other allies, the robot gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws. This bonus increases to +2 for Reflex saves against area effects.

details.publicNotes:
Most line-class soldier robots are lanky, headless humanoid machines. They have four-fingered hands and armored torsos painted in their owner's livery, with a single red sensor in the upper torso. By default, they're armed with bargain-brand laser rifles and breaching hammers. Each is 5 feet tall and weighs 300 pounds.

Line soldiers are the final refinement of the soldier robot concept, made dangerous through their simplicity. Line-class robots can be adapted to any battlefield, download skill packages via infosphere, cannibalize damaged allies for repairs, and are simple to make, maintain, and modify. As such, they are relatively cheap to purchase in large numbers and can be deployed with little regard for casualties.

Living soldiers are a liability. They have families who don't want them to die, need salaries, have morale, require discipline, commit atrocities, and have opinions on the war. They're a necessary, but unfortunate, part of warfare for any society without an abiding love for necromancy.

Cutting-edge soldier robots promise to solve this problem. Soldier robots have no loved ones, opinions, self-preservation instinct, or tendency toward random atrocities. Many companies market their soldier robots as a humane solution to modern combat.

In practice, they're anything but.

Without lives on the line, soldier robots encourage their owners to engage more often and more violently. Standoffs turn into skirmishes, skirmishes into battles, and inevitably the innocent are caught up in the mix. They're particularly popular with corporations and non-governmental organizations that appreciate being able to throw a dozen soldier robots at any naysayer, public critic, or explorer they dislike while being able to deny true responsibility for what happens.

Though there's constant public fear of soldier robots going rogue, it has yet to prove a serious concern. If a soldier robot wants you dead, it's because someone doesn't mind if it kills you.

description.value:
The tashtari causes their filaments to glow with intense light. Non-tashtari creatures within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:40] must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save. A creature that attempts this save is immune to all Bristle Flashes for 1 minute. The tashtari's fur then dims, and they can't use this ability until they bask in sunlight for at least 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is dazzled for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.

The tashtari alpha unleashes a searing, rainbow muzzle beam that soars into the sky before splitting into countless rays of multicolored light that crash down in every direction. Each non-tashtari creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] takes @Damage[4d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save). On a critical failure, a creature that can see the tashtari alpha is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute. Other tashtaris in the area recharge their Bristle Flash. The tashtari alpha's glowing heart dims, and they can't use this ability until they bask in sunlight for at least 1 hour.

details.publicNotes: Native to the green world of Castrovel, the tashtari has become known throughout the wider galaxy as the laser wolf. Wolflike pack hunters, tashtaris have a layer of flexible filaments that emit phosphorescent lights and allow them to silently communicate through a bespoke visual language. Each tashtari possesses a node within their throat that allows them to channel their stored solar energy and unleash a beam of focused light, giving them their moniker of laser wolves. A mating pair, consisting of one or two alphas, leads packs of tashtaris, who generally become the apex predators of large areas with one or more forested regions. The presence of trees is important for the nocturnal tashtaris, who sleep on the treetops during the day to bask in sunlight and recharge their solar energies.

description.value: An athraxer can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.
An adjacent creature that deals slashing or piercing damage to the athraxer is sprayed with acidic blood and takes 2 persistent acid damage (@Check[reflex|dc:14|basic] save).

30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Trigger The athraxer would die

Effect The athraxer first makes a claw Strike against a creature in reach, then dies.

details.publicNotes:
Swarm invasions deploy athraxers as infantry. Fearless and violent, they're crucial for providing reconnaissance of a world's environment and defenses. A single Swarm ship can carry thousands of eggs in stasis.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. Thanks to its pre-Gap development of star travel, the Swarm had already consumed innumerable systems before history short-circuited, and the Swarm's pace only accelerated once it received access to the Signal and the hyperspace dimension known as the Drift.

Worlds touched by the Swarm are drained of their natural resources and rendered barren places known as huskworlds. What outsiders consider to be "Swarm space" is little more than a swathe of lifeless worlds that the Swarm have consumed in its reckless advance. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

description.value: 120 feet. The void palm causes antigravitational technology to malfunction. Upon entering this aura, any items or objects with the tech trait that employ some form of antigrav tech, including antigrav harnesses as well as most hover vehicles and starships, become glitching 1.

Trigger A creature with darkvision within 60 feet of the void palm begins its turn

Requirements The void palm is in darkness

Effect The patterns on a void palm's mottled red fronds resemble a distant star field when viewed in the darkness. The void palm sways its fronds in a disorienting manner. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to Disorienting Patterns for 24 hours.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the end of the void palm's next turn.

Failure As success, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the void palm's melee Strikes while it's dazzled.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute

The void palm makes two sweeping frond Strikes against the same target, each using its current multiple attack penalty. If both attacks hit, combine their damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating the void palm's multiple attack penalty.

Frequency once per day

Effect The void palm creates a zero-gravity area of darkness in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], negating the effects of gravity and non-magical light sources in the area. Magical light sources in the area work normally. The void palm is unaffected by this lack of gravity, but other creatures that enter or start their turn in the area become untethered unless they can move normally in zero-gravity environments; they're also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} while untethered in the area. An untethered creature attempting to Push Off in the area must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or the action is disrupted. The Gravity Void lasts for 10 minutes.

details.publicNotes:
A void palm poses a danger to sentient creatures and their antigravity technology alike. The plant propagates slowly yet steadily by catapulting an annual yield of brilliant blue seeds spaceward. The imparted momentum provides sufficient velocity for most seeds, aided by their supernatural ability to ignore gravity, to escape into the depths of space. Each seed drifts unremittingly until it encounters an object suitable for sustaining it, whether a wandering asteroid, planetary body, or sizable starship.

Once embedded in a fitting surface, a void palm seed germinates rapidly. Upon sprouting, the plant begins drawing nutrients from its surroundings, which periodically become enriched by the decomposing corpses or the oxidizing scraps of the plant's victims. The plant's fronds routinely sweep its area clear of remains or other debris that could serve as handholds or counterbalance or that could alert potential prey to their peril.

description.value:
You discharge a powerful bolt of lightning at the target, dealing 8d12 electricity damage. The target must attempt a basic Reflex save. The electricity arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the chain at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome). The chain ends if any one of the targets critically succeeds at its save.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

target.value: 1 creature, plus any number of additional creatures

description.value:
You flood the area with a wave of electrical interference. You deal 7d8 electricity damage to creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 1 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage, and any of its items with the tech trait become glitching 2 for 1 minute or until the condition is removed.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

target.value: 1 creature or object with the tech trait

description.value: While not a language, starsong allows omas to communicate telepathically with one another across space; omas can communicate with other omas in the same star system. Any other creature with telepathy can receive but not necessarily understand starsong at a range of 10,000 miles.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NoBreath]

Frequency once per hour

Effect The oma sings a telepathic song that encourages its friends and dispirits its foes within 120 feet. Each ally who can hear the song gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks for 1 minute. Each enemy must attempt a @Check[will|dc:35] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure), as the song affects their inner ear.

When an oma scores a critical hit with its electric discharge Strike, the target must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save or all tech items held by the target become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.

Huge, @Damage[(3d8+10)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 37

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
Also known as space whales, omas project powerful magical electromagnetic fields that protect them from both vacuum and toxic atmospheres while allowing them to feed. Omas gain sustenance from the particulate rings and dense atmospheres of gas giants, although exactly which substances are nutritious to an oma is still unknown. Studying a feeding oma isn't without danger. Omas sometimes accidentally swallow drones and small starships, such as fighters and interceptors, and they've been known to do so deliberately when provoked.

Most omas have been observed traveling alone. On occasion, omas form pods of three to five individuals and migrate across systems, on currents and schedules only they know. In very rare cases, omas gather among the rings of a planet in massive numbers. Their energy fields intermingle and react with local gases to light up large areas of space in a multicolored show.

Their most enigmatic yet well known feature is their starsong. Traveling through space for billions of miles, the starsong is a haunting telepathic melody. Most people perceive starsong as crooning, slow and mournful. No one can agree on the details, suggesting that each listener hears something different. Attempts to translate starsong have failed thus far, stumping even the most brilliant magic and linguistics experts. Since the Drift Crisis, for unknown reasons pods of omas have begun bombarding Drift beacons with a seemingly new starsong frequency, in some cases causing catastrophic malfunctions.

description.value:
The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

You create an illusory image of a Large or smaller creature. It generates the appropriate sounds, smells, and feels believable to the touch. If you and the image are ever farther than 500 feet apart, the spell ends.

The image can't speak, but you can use your actions to speak through the creature, with the spell disguising your voice as appropriate. You might need to attempt a Deception or Performance check to mimic the creature, as determined by the GM. This is especially likely if you're trying to imitate a specific person and engage with someone that person knows.

In combat, the illusion can use 2 actions per turn, which it uses when you Sustain the spell. It uses your spell attack modifier for attack rolls and your spell DC for its AC. Its saving throw modifiers are equal to your spell DC – 10. It is substantial enough that it can flank other creatures. If the image is hit by an attack or fails a save, the spell ends.

The illusion can cause damage by making the target believe the illusion's attacks are real, but it cannot otherwise directly affect the physical world. If the illusory creature hits with a Strike, the target takes @Damage[3d4[mental]] damage. The illusion's Strikes are nonlethal. If the damage doesn't correspond to the image of the monster—for example, if an illusory Large dragon deals only 5 damage—the GM might allow the target to attempt an immediate Perception check to disbelieve the spell. Any relevant resistances and weaknesses apply if the target thinks they do, as judged by the GM. For example, if the illusion wields a warhammer and attacks a creature resistant to bludgeoning damage, the creature would take less mental damage. However, illusory damage does not deactivate regeneration or trigger other effects that require a certain damage type. The GM should track illusory damage dealt by the illusion.

Any creature that touches the image or uses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion. When a creature disbelieves the illusion, it recovers from half the damage it had taken from it (if any) and doesn't take any further damage from it.

Heightened (+1) The damage of the image's Strikes increases by 1d4, and the maximum size of creature you can create increases by one (to a maximum of Gargantuan).

When a creature disbelieves the illusion, it recovers from half the damage it had taken from it (if any) and doesn't take any further damage from it.

Heightened (+1) The damage of the image's Strikes increases by 1d4, and the maximum size of creature you can create increases by one (to a maximum of Gargantuan).

You dull the target's mind, depending on its Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} for 1 minute.

You unleash a swirling multitude of colors that overwhelms creatures based on their Will saves.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, and dazzled for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature is stunned for 1 round and blinded for 1 minute.

You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

You create a simple illusory sound or vision. A sound adds the auditory trait to the spell and the sound can't include intelligible words or elaborate music. A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{Create a Diversion} with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check. If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{Spell Effect: Figment}

You create an illusory visual image of a stationary object. The entire image must fit within the spell's area. The object appears to animate naturally, but it doesn't make sounds or generate smells. For example, water would appear to pour down an illusory waterfall, but it would be silent.

Any creature that touches the image or uses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} action to examine it can attempt to disbelieve your illusion.

Heightened (2nd) Your image makes appropriate sounds, generates normal smells, and feels right to the touch. The spell gains the auditory and olfactory traits. The duration increases to 1 hour.

Heightened (5th) As the 2nd-rank version, but the duration is unlimited.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

You create a floating, magical hand, either invisible or ghostlike, that grasps the target object and levitates it slowly up to 20 feet in any direction. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the object an additional 20 feet. If the object is in the air when the spell ends, the object falls.

Heightened (3rd) You can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.

Heightened (5th) The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.

Heightened (7th) The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less.

1 unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less
1 unattended object of light Bulk or less

You call upon the Void to harm life force. The target takes 2d4 void damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.
A wayward warp can sense creatures within their warp field as a precise sense at the listed range.
The wayward warp has concealment against attacks originating outside of their warp field.

15 feet. Reality within the area becomes a different type of terrain. Structures and creatures in the area aren't transformed, and the change immediately ends when the aura moves or the wayward warp dies. The nature of the aura is chosen randomly using the table below each time the wayward warp moves. A wayward warp is immune to their own warp field's effects and to the aligned damage type, but they gain weakness 5 to the opposing damage type.

[[/r 1d6]]{D6}FIELDALIGNED DAMAGEOPPOSING DAMAGEFIELD EFFECT
1CosmicVoidVitalityZero gravity
2EarthPiercingAcidDifficult terrain (sand)

A creature hit by a wayward warp's spatial tendril must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26] save or become misaligned to reality for 1 minute, gaining a condition based on the field effect while it remains in the warp field: @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.z1ucw4CLwLqHoAp3]{Untethered} (cosmic), @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} (earth or water), @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (inferno or frozen), or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{glitching 1} (any tech creature or held tech item in storm).

details.publicNotes: A wayward warp is a living dimensional rift that causes unexpected havoc as they twist the world around them. No two wayward warps look the same, and no wayward warp keeps the same appearance for very long; every time a wayward warp moves, a different potential reality reveals itself. The warp themself is a rift at the center of the aura that appears like a tear in reality, often taking a shape vaguely resembling a feather star whose tentacles are lined with shard-like cracks in space itself.

description.value:
Trigger The irradiated undead plant is reduced to 0 Hit Points by energy damage of any kind

Effect The energy acts as a catalyst for a nuclear reaction, causing the plant to explode and dealing @Damage[9d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save.
20 feet. A creature entering or starting its turn inside the aura is exposed to extreme radiation.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The irradiated charger triggers a localized radioactive reaction as it charges. It Strides once and then Strikes. A creature damaged by this attack takes 1 additional point of void damage for every space the charger moved prior to making its Strike. On a critical hit, the creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Medium or smaller, Tusk, @Check[reflex|dc:24|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Trample]

details.publicNotes:
Chargers, known by as many different names as their planets of origin, usually spawn from the irradiated corpses of beasts slain by natural or manufactured nuclear disasters. These quadrupedal, animalistic undead are so named for their mighty charge attack.

To survive in the unforgiving expanse of space, creatures must be naturally adapted or take protective measures. While the dead rarely need such precautions against these threats, sometimes radiation can animate a corpse (or several) to a state of undeath!

Scientists and religious scholars alike debate whether a specific type of radiation is to blame for this phenomenon that spontaneously triggers unlife, but all agree on the dangers such irradiated creatures pose. Many types of radiation could be the cause: intense bursts of cosmic radiation emitted by the stars, magically enhanced radioactive isotopes found in a planet's or moon's crust, or even the advent of new energy sources in a society (or its radioactive waste) contaminating the land. Regardless of their specific origin, irradiated undead all pose the same threat—they emit ionizing radiation that ravages everything around them.

description.value: A bloodbrother can detect infrared radiation (such as that given off by warm-blooded creatures) as a precise sense at the listed range.

10 feet. A bloodbrother's flesh radiates intense cold. A creature that starts its turn in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:fatigued] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} while it remains in the area. Creatures immune to environmental cold effects or with any cold resistance are immune.

Frequency once per round

Requirements A creature is imprisoned in the bloodbrother's ribcage prison

Effect The bloodbrother drains the vital fluids from the imprisoned creature. The victim is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or increases its drained value by 1), and the bloodbrother regains @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points, gaining any excess Hit Points as temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute.

A creature that begins its turn @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the bloodbrother takes @Damage[1d8[cold]] damage.

Trigger The bloodbrother hits a creature in the first round of combat, and the bloodbrother was hidden from that creature at the start of combat

Effect Each enemy within 30 feet that witnesses the attack (including the target of the attack) must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened] save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4} on a critical failure).
A bloodbrother ignores difficult terrain from ice and snow and doesn't risk falling when crossing ice.

Requirements The bloodbrother has a Large or smaller creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} with their bone spur

Effect The bloodbrother tries to imprison the required creature in their ribcage prison. The bloodbrother attempts an @Check[athletics|against:reflex] check against the creature's Reflex DC, imprisoning it on a success. The bloodbrother's bone spur is no longer grabbing or restraining the creature they've imprisoned, so the bloodbrother is free to use their bone spur Strike once again. The bloodbrother can't attack a creature they've imprisoned and can have only one creature imprisoned in their ribcage prison at a time. An imprisoned creature is restrained ([[/act escape dc=25]]) and temporarily immune to the bloodbrother's frigid touch while restrained.

Upgrades: 1

These dependable, mass-produced rifles shoot concentrated beams of energy out of square-shaped barrels. The universal standard Pact Worlds model is highly modifiable and designed to function with a variety of accessories and upgrades, with wielders adapting the laser rifle to their preferences and even kit-bashing it into a nearly unrecognizable weapon.

The protection-class security robot enters a position that grants adjacent allies the benefits of lesser cover until the start of its next turn. A Medium or smaller creature can [[/act take-cover]] while adjacent to the robot and gains the benefits of standard cover.

Frequency once per day

Effect The security robot restores @Damage[24[healing]] Hit Points per the robot's level to one technological creature or item.

details.publicNotes:
These security robots are often constructed out of more durable materials than other robots, granting them the ability to protect their charges. Their bulky frames can be seen guarding important individuals or valuable items

Security robots are ubiquitous throughout industrialized space and have major presences in both the Pact Worlds and Veskarium, employed and customized by corporations and private citizens alike.

While most soldier robot models are built to be as destructive as possible, and elimination robots are designed to kill, security robots usually have fail-safes programmed into their behavior to prevent them from causing any more violence than is necessary to perform their designated security function. This makes them especially useful in cities, where unintended destruction might harm bystanders or cause legal issues, and even on starships, where a stray laser blast might cause serious damage to vital starship systems.

description.value: Dust worm augurs communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Dust worm aurgurs sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the diaspora worm.

Dust worm aurgurs that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:43] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

The dust worm augur emits a concentrated wave of electromagnetism in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40|options:area-effect,inflicts:glitching,inflicts:stunned,inflicts:paralyzed] save. A creature with the tech trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to Electromagnetic Surge for 1 hour.

Success The creature's items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}, and its items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} for 1 minute. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round, and its items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3} for 10 minutes. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

The dust worm augur conjures a hurricane of sand and metallic bits in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15]. This vortex moves with the worm and lasts for 1 minute. The vortex grants the worm resistance 11 to physical damage and makes any creature inside of it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} to creatures outside of it, and vice versa. Any creature that enters or starts its turn within the vortex must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:40|options:area-effect] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes @Damage[3d10[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[6d10[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage and takes a –15-foot status penalty to all Speeds while within the vortex.

Critical Failure As failure, but @Damage[12d10[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage, and the creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Large, @Damage[(6d12+10)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 38

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

A creature hit by the dust worm augur's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 42 that deals at least 38 slashing damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Requirements The dust worm aurgur has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

details.publicNotes:
Ancient diaspora dust worms become paragons of worm colonization, conquering asteroids and painstakingly terraforming them to support worm society.

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

description.value:
You alter your perceptions to convince yourself that another creature or object is an illusion. Choose one object or creature you can perceive within range. For you, the target becomes transparent and doesn't create sound, smell, or other sensory output. You can move through the target, and the target can move through you. You gain resistance to all damage originating from the target equal to your level. If you are targeted by or are in the area of one of the target's abilities or effects, the target must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5], and if it fails, you're unaffected by the ability or effect. You're immune to all of the target's auditory, linguistic, and scent-based attacks and effects. Your attacks deal no damage to the target, and the target is immune to your spells and other effects. You and the target can affect one another indirectly, such as through a Strike from a minion or through the environment affected by a witchwarper's quantum field. When this spell ends, the target becomes immune to subjective reality for 24 hours.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.4P2rPLVKjSpUbOWI]{Spell Effect: Subjective Reality}

Your honeyed words are difficult for creatures to resist. You suggest a course of action to the target, which must be phrased in such a way as to seem like a logical course of action to the target and can't be self-destructive or obviously against the target's self-interest. The target must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and knows you tried to control it.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target immediately follows your suggestion. The spell has a duration of 1 minute, or until the target has completed a finite suggestion or the suggestion becomes self-destructive or has other obvious negative effects.

Critical Failure As failure, but the base duration is 1 hour.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You instantly transport yourself and any items you're wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space within range you can see. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an extradimensional container—the spell is lost.

Heightened (5th) The range increases to 1 mile. You don't need to be able to see your destination, as long as you have been there in the past and know its relative location and distance from you. You are then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

You forcibly calm creatures in the area, soothing them into a nonviolent state; each creature must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success Calming urges impose a -1 status penalty to the creature's attack rolls.

Failure Any emotion effects that would affect the creature are suppressed and the creature can't use hostile actions. If the target is subject to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by calm.

Critical Failure As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Qr5rgoZvI4KmFY0N]{Spell Effect: Calm}

You share a mood by psychically projecting an intense emotion into a creature's mind. The target attempts a Will save. On a failure, the target experiences your choice of the following emotions, but if you pick anger or joy this spell gains the incapacitation trait.

  • Anger On its next turn, the target spends its first action to attack the nearest creature, even if it's an ally.
  • Joy The target can't take any hostile actions.
  • Fear The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.
  • Sorrow The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and bursts into tears.

Heightened (4th) The spell affects all creatures in a 20-foot burst. Each creature must be affected by the same emotion.

You summon a Tiny bouncing wisp of light that easily distracts even the most determined of combatants. The wisp floats near your target. A wisp doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would. The wisp generates bright light in a 10-foot radius. When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the spell, your wisp attempts to distract your target, which must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. A creature who critically succeeds becomes immune to wisp ally for 1 hour.

Heightened (+1) You create one additional wisp, allowing you to choose a different target. Each wisp you create must have a different target, and all of them attempt to distract their targets at the same time when you cast or Sustain this spell.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round

A flash grenade unleashes a blast of bright light that requires creatures in a @Template[burst|distance:10] to attempt a @Check[fortitude|against:class] save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 2 rounds.

Upgrades: 1

This compact pistol is the most popular sidearm in the Pact Worlds thanks to its reliability, durability, and affordability. Most common laser pistols have a lightweight polycarbon body ending in a long, slim barrel.

Upgrades: 1

This glove features dozens of delicate tools for building or repairing computers and other technological items, requiring an entire hand (or suitable appendage) to manipulate. Overclocking the polyglove's components generates voltage between a wielder's fingertips.

Upgrades: 0

This comfortable coverall is made from lightweight thermal fabric and often adorned with patches displaying personal insignia, military rank, or even favorite bands or sports teams.
Effect @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 2 rounds on failure (@Check[fortitude|dc:26] save)

Trigger The solipsist succeeds at a Will save, or an attack against them is a critical failure

Effect The solipsist teleports up to 25 feet in any direction of their choice. Creatures adjacent to the solipsist after they teleport are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to them until the end of the solipsist's next turn.

The solipsist treats all creatures, objects, and structures they're observing (except for Paradox 17) as quasi-illusory, allowing the solipsist to move through them as though the solipsist were incorporeal. They can also choose to use Will for any saving throw against any non-damaging effect.

Flash Grenade (@Template[type:burst|distance:10], range 70 feet, tracking +1)

Effect @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 2 rounds on failure (@Check[fortitude|dc:26] save)

The solipsist deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures, and on the first successful melee Strike they make against an off-guard creature each turn, the target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} while stunned.

details.publicNotes:
On six recorded occasions, individuals having prolonged contact with Paradox 17 continued to display anomalous behavior afterward. Two expressed belief they were dreaming, four that they were in a hostile simulation. One of these latter individuals insisted on "finding the light again" during an interview before walking through their guards and the holding cell wall, and they haven't been seen since.

Can you ever really know that the Universe you perceive exists? That you'll never awaken one day to find it was all a dream? A planet full of philosophers sought the answers to these questions once upon a time… shortly before they all vanished en masse. They left behind confusing records stating that the light of a "whirling star" revealed that physical matter had no substance and the Universe was secretly a mental prison. These records are all that remain from the first known encounter with the being known as Paradox 17.

description.value:
You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

target.value: 1 dataset or tech item with the tracking trait

description.value:
An arc of lightning leaps from one target to another. Each target takes 2d4 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

You create a simple illusory sound or vision. A sound adds the auditory trait to the spell and the sound can't include intelligible words or elaborate music. A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{Create a Diversion} with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check.
