If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{Spell Effect: Figment}

You touch a weapon with no remaining ammunition and recharge it with magical energy. The target gains ammunition or charges equal to its expend until the end of your next turn, allowing anyone holding the target to make one Strike with the weapon. This spell only recharges weapons that use ammunition with a cost of 10 credits or less.

Heightened (3rd) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to double its expend.

Heightened (6th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to triple its expend.

Heightened (9th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to quadruple its expend.

You call down a tendril of lightning that cracks with thunder, dealing 1d12 electricity damage and 1d4 sonic damage to the target with a basic Reflex save. A target wearing metal armor or made of metal takes a –1 circumstance bonus to its save, and if damaged by the spell is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12 electricity and 1d4 sonic.

A botnib can communicate telepathically with digital devices and with other creatures that can do so (such as other botnibs). This allows the botnib to attempt Computers checks to access any computer within the telepathy's range.
The botnib can sense items and creatures with the tech trait within 100 feet.

20 feet. Creatures and items with the tech trait struggle to function reliably when near a botnib. Creatures with the tech trait, unattended items with the tech trait, or creatures holding equipment with the tech trait that start their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:16|options:area-effect,inflicts:glitching] save.

Critical Success No effect, and the creature or item is temporarily immune to the botnib's glitch aura for 1 minute.

Success The creature or items are unaffected.

Failure The creature, item, or all the creature's held items become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.

details.publicNotes:
The lowly botnib, also called a computer glitch gremlin, is intuitively tied with the computerized devices and networks of the modern age. These cavorting fey make it their mission to create as much havoc as they can by influencing computer systems and creating chaos in their wake. Known for their innate ability to communicate with digital devices, computer glitch gremlins routinely become infatuated with such systems, which they subvert like one might put together a fun puzzle.

Gremlins have plagued the Universe with cruel tricks since long before the Gap, and they've fully adapted to a galaxy filled with technology. Whether meddling with a comm unit, a starship, or an entire space station, gremlins delight in ruination and chaos.

description.value: A thresher lord can sense nearby creatures through vibrations in air or water caused by movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

30 feet. All allies with colony mind within the listed range of each other are in constant telepathic communication and are aware of all threats any individual knows about. While within range of at least one ally with colony mind, when a creature rolls a success on a save against a mental effect, it gets a critical success instead.

Trigger A creature adjacent to the thresher lord targets the thresher lord with a melee attack

Effect The thresher lord gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

The thresher lord makes three arm blade Strikes. Each Strike uses the thresher lord's current multiple attack penalty, but each Strike takes a –2 penalty. This counts as three attacks when calculating its multiple attack penalty.

Frequency three times per day

Effect The thresher lord Strides up to three times its Speed in a straight line.
On a critical hit with an arm blade Strike, the thresher lord inflicts @Damage[1d8[persistent,bleed]] damage to its target.

details.publicNotes:
Engineered to be heavy assault forces, thresher lords break enemy formations and cut paths through fortifications to make way for masses of infantry components. The enormous blades protruding from their forearms have evolved not just as weapons, but to provide both aerodynamic support and a modicum of protection while thresher lords close into melee range.

Developing far out in the Vast from an insectile species known as kucharns, the Swarm is considered the greatest threat in existence for several cultures. Composed of countless subcolonies, each consisting of myriad component subspecies, the Swarm is a vast host that acts in psychic union for the purpose of consuming all matter across the cosmos. Most frightening of all is the Swarm's ability to assimilate the knowledge and DNA of those they consume. Thanks to its pre-Gap development of star travel, the Swarm had already consumed innumerable systems before history short-circuited, and the Swarm's pace only accelerated once it received access to the Signal and the hyperspace dimension known as the Drift.

Worlds touched by the Swarm are drained of their natural resources and rendered barren places known as huskworlds. What outsiders consider to be "Swarm space" is little more than a swathe of lifeless worlds that the Swarm have consumed in its reckless advance. The Swarm so thoroughly strips these worlds that surveyors struggle to find even trace amounts of inorganic resources, such as rare minerals and water. Few of these huskworlds have been thoroughly explored, as oftentimes exploration awakens inert drones, theorized to have been left behind in case the devoured system was ever colonized and repopulated. Pockets of Swarm activity exist well beyond this region of space, as the Swarm sent out countless subcolonies in the ages before the Gap, and even now, only some of them have reached their intended destinations.

The Swarm is perhaps most known in the current era for two important facts. First, its assault on the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium led to the cessation of hostilities and tenuous alliance between the two powers. Second, the Swarm is the direct ancestor of the shirren species. Having broken away sometime during the Gap, shirrens found succor with the deity Hylax, who was believed to have evolved from a pre-Swarm kucharn and who turned her back on the ravenous entity the Swarm has evolved into.

In recent years, the Swarm had been seen as little more than a distant boogeyman with no activities save for classified actions in the Suskillon system in the Vast. However, the recent discovery of the missing Szandite world of Krethiskar inadvertently awoke a dormant subcolony that had been responsible for the world's consumption. Since then, the newly dubbed Krethiskar subcolony has become a scourge of Near Space, engaged in an active invasion of Szandite Collective worlds and poised to spill over into other powers like the Veskarium and Pact Worlds. This rediscovered subcolony has begun rapid evolution of its components and remains cut off from the wider Swarm, but it still represents a clear danger to Near Space.

description.value: The ranonek can detect blood inside creatures' bodies as an imprecise sense at the listed range. This is a precise sense against actively bleeding creatures.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Ferocity]

The ranonek gains a +4 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures it has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A Large or smaller creature remains grabbed or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the ranonek even if the ranonek moves. If a ranonek would take falling damage while it has a creature grabbed, the grabbed creature takes the falling damage instead (in addition to any falling damage it would normally take).

Requirements The ranonek has a creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The ranonek sucks blood from the grabbed creature. This requires an Athletics check against the creature's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic if the creature is restrained or if it took 50 or more damage since the start of the ranonek's last turn. The victim is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} until it receives healing (of any kind or amount), and the ranonek regains @Damage[25[healing]] Hit Points, gaining any excess Hit Points as temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute.

The Ranonek Strides, Climbs, or Leaps twice (in any combination), then makes a claw or jaws Strike at the end of the movement. If it ends this movement by Leaping, the ranonek doesn't begin to fall until the end of the ranonek's turn, but it needs a solid surface to Leap from for each Leap.

Large, @Damage[(2d6+7)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 24

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
These enormous, bearlike vampiric beasts stalk the chilly jungle environments of Triaxus. While they hibernate most of the year, ranoneks awaken before deep freezes to catch beasts, dragons, or anything else migrating with the encroaching glaciers. With muscular, lean legs like a large cat, these white-furred predators use the tree line to hide from and ambush flying prey by leaping up to snatch them from midair. Using the hunted creatures' bodies to break the fall, ranoneks slam them into the ground below to reduce their insides to pulp before feeding on their blood.

A ranonek's digestive system utilizes several highly compressive tracts that allow them to squeeze the blood from smaller prey. When another meal tries to fly away, they can hock up and shoot the dried pellets containing the remains of their desiccated meals, knocking down prey before it gets too far away. Those documenting these remains have confirmed that these huge beasts prefer dragon prey over any other; this comes as no surprise given that ranoneks can drain many young dragons in under a minute and swallow entire dragonkin whole.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

Frequency once per round

Effect The driftdead teleports up to its Speed. If it's in the Drift, the distance a driftdead can teleport is doubled.

details.publicNotes:
The least of these wretched spirits are the returned. Appearing as twisted versions of the shapes they wore in life, the returned drag themselves through space with wriggling, clawed appendages or float with hideous grace. The returned haunt Drift beacons, castaway ships, and airless rocks.

Driftdead are created when a restless soul fuses with planar energies and reanimates its own corpse into a disturbing parody of life. Rarely, disturbed spirits snatched from elsewhere fuse together to inhabit a single dead body. Any death within the Drift might spawn a driftdead, but creatures that die in a frenzy of intense emotion are more likely to make the transition.

description.value: Unless they've fed recently, the trawler is naturally fully transparent, allowing them to [[/act hide]] without concealment or cover and granting them a +4 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks to Hide. A trawler loses this transparency if they take a hostile action of any kind.

Requirements The trawler has a Medium or smaller creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} with its lasso filament

Effect The trawler pulls the creature toward themself. They attempt an Athletics check against the target's Fortitude DC. A trawler can lift a creature off the ground in this way.

Critical Success The target is moved 20 feet closer to the trawler.

Success The target is moved 10 feet closer to the trawler.

Critical Failure The trawler releases the target.

A creature hit by the trawler's lasso filament becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the trawler. The creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the range of the lasso filament. A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for as long as it's grabbed by a lasso filament. A creature can sever the filament with a Strike against AC 19 that deals at least 6 fire or slashing damage. This deals no damage to the trawler but destroys the filament. A trawler has three lasso filaments, and they can't use their lasso filament Strike if all three are destroyed. It takes a week for the trawler to regrow all three of their filaments.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:20]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d4[poison]] damage, enfeebled 2, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 round)

Stage 4 @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage, enfeebled 2, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (1 round)

details.publicNotes:
Experienced explorers of Castrovel know to monitor the sky, lest they be caught unaware by a diaphanous trawler. A trawler can spin a filament with sticky coating that has a mild anesthetic effect, allowing them to stalk at a distance and then pull their prey in close, all while comfortably airborne.

It's extremely rare for an adult trawler to land or alight on anything voluntarily. Usually, they maintain at least a minimal air gap between themself and any object. If forced to be on the ground, a trawler can use their tentacles to clumsily ambulate, though more often than not, they'll launch themself into the air given the first opportunity.

description.value:
You send disturbing nightmares to your target. The next time the target falls asleep, it must attempt a Will save. If you know the target only by name and have never met them, the target gets a +4 circumstance bonus to the Will save.

Critical Success The target suffers no adverse effects and is temporarily immune for 1 week.

Success The target experiences the nightmares but suffers no adverse effects other than unpleasant memories.

Failure The target experiences the nightmares and awakens @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}.

Critical Failure The target experiences the nightmares, awakens Fatigued, and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} until it is no longer Fatigued.

Your honeyed words are difficult for creatures to resist. You suggest a course of action to the target, which must be phrased in such a way as to seem like a logical course of action to the target and can't be self-destructive or obviously against the target's self-interest. The target must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and knows you tried to control it.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The target immediately follows your suggestion. The spell has a duration of 1 minute, or until the target has completed a finite suggestion or the suggestion becomes self-destructive or has other obvious negative effects.

Critical Failure As failure, but the base duration is 1 hour.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You create an @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} floating ear at a location within range (even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect). It can't move, but you can hear through the ear as if using your normal auditory senses.

With a cursory mental touch, you attempt to read the target's mind. It must attempt a Will save. The target then becomes temporarily immune to your mind reading for 1 hour.

Critical Success The target perceives vague surface thoughts from you when you Cast the Spell.

Success You find out whether the target's Intelligence modifier is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.

Failure You perceive vague surface thoughts from the target when you Cast the Spell, and you find out whether its Intelligence is higher than, equal to, or lower than yours.

Critical Failure As failure, and for the duration of the spell, you can Sustain the spell to detect the target's surface thoughts again. The target doesn't receive any additional saves.

duration.value: 1 round or sustained up to 1 minute

description.value:
A willing creature you touch becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} to everyone except a second target within the spell's range. If the invisible creature uses a hostile action against anyone who isn't the target that can see them, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

Heightened (5th) You can make up to 6 willing creatures within 30 feet invisible, but they all must have the same target.

target.value: 1 willing creature touched and one within range

description.value:
You telepathically share the sensation of agonizing pain with a creature. The target takes 3d12 mental damage and attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute.

Heightened (6th) The spell targets up to four creatures and deals 6d12 mental damage.

You sense the presence of thinking creatures around you. You detect the presence or absence of thoughts from conscious creatures that have Intelligence modifiers of –3 or higher. You can choose to ignore the thoughts of creatures you're familiar with so they don't interfere with your detection of other thoughts. The spell can penetrate some barriers, but 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force barrier blocks it. Creatures who are immune to mental effects are immune to detection by this spell.

Heightened (4th) You detect the number of thinking minds and whether or not the creatures are telepathic. Creatures with telepathy have louder thoughts than creatures who don't have telepathy and might be able to sense your intrusion, at the GM's discretion.

You magically broadcast grim news onto nearby devices, holo projectors, screens, and other displays (including comm units on armor). Creatures in the area who can see one or more displays must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the display.

Failure The creature is fascinated by the display and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (even if the fascination ends).

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated by the display, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (even if the fascination ends).

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You manifest an injury from the future or past to harm a creature in the present. Choose bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. The target takes 1d8 of the selected damage type with a basic Will save. If the target fails its save and then takes the same type of damage before the end of your next turn, it takes an additional @Damage[ceil((@item.level)/2)d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a failure increases by 1d4.

100 feet

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Telepathy]

A civusdaemon knows the presence of any creatures connected to any local infospheres it has access to. A creature who is connected to an infosphere is visible to the civusdaemon as a precise sense. Disconnected creatures who've been active on an infosphere within the past day leave enough residual trace that a civusdaemon can sense them as an imprecise sense.

items.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Mental

description.value: A civusdaemon appears as a strangely two-dimensional version of a creature of their choice. Creatures targeting the civusdaemon must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] to do so, as if the civusdaemon were @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}. A creature who succeeds at this check automatically succeeds at further such flat checks for 1 round.

Frequency once per day

Trigger An enemy critically hits the civusdaemon or the civusdaemon critically fails a save against an enemy's effect

Effect The attack becomes a hit instead of a critical hit, or the critical failure on the save becomes a failure instead.

The civusdaemon targets the online reputation of a creature it's aware of on a shared infosphere. After 1 day of downtime, the civusdaemon attempts a @Check[computers|defense:computers] check against the target's Computers DC.

If successful, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all checks made on that infosphere because of damage to their reputation. The civusdaemon can maintain this effect each day by spending another day of downtime without needing to attempt an additional check.

If the civusdaemon maintains this for a week or more, the penalty increases to –4. The creature or an ally of the creature can spend a day of downtime to attempt a @Check[computers|dc:26], @Check[deception|dc:26] or @Check[diplomacy|dc:26] check to counter the character attack.

On a success, the civusdaemon's efforts end, and the target becomes immune to this ability for 1 month.

On a critical success, the target or ally also identifies a specific location that the civusdaemon operates out of, such as one where it frequently connects to the infosphere.

allSaves.value: +1 status to all saves vs. mental

details.publicNotes:
Born from infospheres and digital networks, the civusdaemon is a force of malign intent who bends online spaces to torment mortals. Representing death through oppression and hate from social media, these daemons act in the shadows, tormenting their foes to such a ruinous extent that they often take their own life.

Hailing from the plane of Abaddon, daemons are utterly devoted to the destruction of all life. Their existence is based around specific means of death, and they seek the death of all things in service to their lords: the Apocalypse Riders. Each daemon represents a different way to die, and they use their specific powers to further that means of death.

Advents in technology have only created new means of death and torment, so new types of daemons enter existence with regularity, each created once a sufficient mass of new deaths occurs to spawn them. These new advent daemons often prefer to spend their time in the galaxy rather than Abaddon, as they can better spread their torments to the mortals of creation.

description.value: The skreeling's melee Strikes deal 1d4 persistent bleed damage to creatures within reach of one or more other skreelings.

details.publicNotes:
Skreelings hunt in packs and can subsist on ash, metal, wood, or near anything else.

Skreesires, and their offspring skreelings, are vicious predators who skulk and prowl in the crags and caverns of the cosmos.

description.value:
The target appears to be a gruesome and terrifying creature. The effect is unique to each observer, so a human viewing the target might see a demon with bloody fangs, but a demon observing the target might see a glowing angelic visage.

When any creature attempts a hostile action against the target, the creature must attempt a Will save. It is then temporarily immune until the end of its next turn.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} before using its action.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}, and its action fails and is wasted.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 5 creatures. If a creature uses a hostile action or reaction that affects multiple targets simultaneously, it needs to attempt only one save against mask of terror.

You take command of the target, forcing it to obey your orders. If you issue an obviously self-destructive order, the target doesn't act until you issue a new order. The effect depends on its Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} as it fights off your commands.

Failure You control the target. It gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled} condition, but it can attempt a Will save at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the spell ends.

Critical Failure As a failure, but the target receives a new save only if you give it a new order that is against its nature, such as killing its allies.

Heightened (10th) The duration is unlimited.

duration.value: until the next time you make your daily preparations

description.value:
You slice through reality, forming a chasm of life-draining entropy in a straight line 20 feet high, 120 feet long, and 2 inches thick. The chasm provides cover. The chasm deals 7d8 void damage to each creature that's in the chasm's space when it's created, that attempts to pass through the chasm, or that ends its turn inside the chasm, with a basic Reflex save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the chasm fail to do so if they critically fail the save, ending their movement next to the chasm.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You telekinetically grasp a creature's throat or similar vital anatomy with a vicelike grip, forcefully lifting it a few inches off the ground as it struggles to breathe. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d12 bludgeoning damage, and the target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}. While immobilized in this way, it must hold its breath or begin suffocating. The first time each round you Sustain the spell, the target takes 1d12 bludgeoning damage, remains immobilized, and must continue holding its breath. The target can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} (the Escape DC is your spell DC, and the attempt is affected by the incapacitation trait).

Area varies

Defense @Check[reflex|basic|options:area-effect] or @Check[will|basic|options:area-effect] (target's choice)

You shape the shadow substance of the Netherworld into a blast. Choose @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[acid]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[cold]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[electricity]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[fire]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[force]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[piercing]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[slashing]|options:area-damage], @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[sonic]|options:area-damage], or @Damage[(@item.rank+1)d8[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage, and choose a @Template[cone|distance:30], a @Template[burst|distance:15] within 120 feet, or a @Template[line|distance:50]. The blast deals 6d8 damage of the type you chose to each creature in the area.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You implant a subconscious suggestion deep within the target's mind for them to follow when a trigger you specify occurs. You suggest a course of action to the target. Your directive must be phrased in such a way as to seem like a logical course of action to the target, and it can't be self-destructive or obviously against the target's self-interest. The target must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and knows you tried to control it.

Success The target is unaffected and thinks you were talking to them normally, not casting a spell on them.

Failure The suggestion remains in the target's subconscious until the next time you prepare. If the trigger occurs before then, the target immediately follows your suggestion. The effect has a duration of 1 minute, or until the target has completed a finite suggestion or the suggestion becomes self-destructive or has other obvious negative effects.

Critical Failure As failure, but the duration is 1 hour.

Heightened (9th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You channel the bizarre powers that exist beyond the outer reaches of the universe, dealing 3d12 mental damage to each creature in the area as phantasmal claws, jaws, and tentacles lash out at each target. Each creature in the area must attempt a Will save. The area is difficult terrain for any creature sickened by the effects of this spell.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

You burden a creature's body and mind with the pains and sorrows of a lifetime. The target takes 3d8 bludgeoning damage and 3d8 mental damage and must attempt a Will saving throw.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.D5mg6Tc7Jzrj6ro7]{Encumbered} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is encumbered for 1 minute.

Heightened (+1) The mental damage increases by 2d8.

You unleash a ray of entropy that rapidly and indiscriminately decays matter. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 void damage, or it takes 2d6 persistent acid damage and is glitching 1 if it has the tech trait. On a critical hit, double the damage, and if the creature has the tech trait, it's glitching 2 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1 die.

Your touch leeches the lifeblood out of a target to empower yourself. You deal 6d6 void damage to the target. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the void damage the target takes (after applying resistances and the like). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You create a shroud of darkness that prevents light from penetrating or emanating within the area. Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a torch or lantern, do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical light of your darkness spell's rank or lower. Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. From outside, it appears as a globe of pure darkness.

Heightened (4th) Even creatures with darkvision (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the darkness as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a counteract check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, the item becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an artifact or similar item, you automatically fail.

target.value: 1 spell effect or unattended magic item

description.value:
Illusions bend light around the target, rendering it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}. This makes it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected} to all creatures, though the creatures can attempt to find the target, making it @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} to them instead. If the target uses a hostile action, the spell ends after that hostile action is completed.

Heightened (4th) The spell lasts 1 minute, but it doesn't end if the target uses a hostile action.

A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.con2Hzt47JjpuUej]{Spell Effect: Resist Energy}

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You sap the target's strength, depending on its Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3} for 1 minute.

You fire a shard of solidified magic toward a creature that you can see. It automatically hits and deals 1d4+1 force damage. For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, increase the number of shards you shoot by one, to a maximum of three shards for 3 actions. You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

Heightened (+2) You fire one additional shard with each action you spend.

You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

duration.value: until the start of your next turn

description.value:
You summon a creature that has the undead trait and whose level is -1 to fight for you.

Heightened As listed in the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.journals.JournalEntry.S55aqwWIzpQRFhcq.JournalEntryPage.8gcp880pEWZ9VPnF]{summon} trait.

You call upon the Void to harm life force. The target takes 2d4 void damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the start of your next turn.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.
Necrovites often craft and wield a horrific weapon known as a wrackstaff. In the hands of any creature other than its maker, a wrackstaff is a nonmagical club with no value.

Staple items within the Azlanti Star Empire, these gemstones are popular status symbols for adventurers and wealthy collectors. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to build the Universe in ancient times.

When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head (or another visible part of your anatomy if you don't have a head) instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an aeon stone with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into specific items, such as weapons with the aeon trait or antique magical devices called wayfinders.

This deep-blue prism with cloudy inclusions is light as a feather. Its resonant power gives you a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks.

Activate—Fly Free 1 (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You gain a fly Speed for 1 minute equal to your Speed or 20 feet, whichever is greater.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.xZ5XMY55S42aGyjg]{Effect: Aeon Stone (Uplifting)}

Trigger The necrovite is targeted by a spell with the emotion trait

Effect The necrovite attempts to counteract the triggering spell (counteract rank 7, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+30 #Counteract]]{+30}). If the spell is disrupted, the necrovite gains temporary Hit Points equal to twice the rank of the counteracted spell that last for 1 minute.

30 feet. A creature that first enters the aura must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to the necrovite's fatiguing presence for 24 hours.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

Void damage dealt by a necrovite damages undead and creatures with void healing as if it were vitality damage. A necrovite's melee Strikes have the benefits of the ghost killer upgrade on attacks against incorporeal undead.

When the necrovite damages a living creature with its wrackstaff, the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:33] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}. Further wrackstaff damage increases the drain value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4}.

When a necrovite is destroyed, their consciousness is immediately transferred to their electroencephalon. A necrovite can be permanently destroyed only if their electroencephalon is found and destroyed.

The necrovite touches an adjacent creature and scrambles its thought patterns. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:33] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute. The necrovite is considered hidden to the confused target for as long as the confusion lasts.

Trigger The coposirix takes damage

Effect The coposirix's body absorbs some of what damaged it until the end of its next turn, the material seeping into its constituent copi and changing its composition. The effects of this change depends on the nature of the damage.

  • Energy Damage The coposirix's attacks deal an additional 2d6 damage of the same type as the energy damage dealt to it.

  • Physical Damage The coposirix gains resistance 6 to physical damage. If the damage was dealt by a weapon made of a special material, such as silver, the coposirix's battering sludge Strike counts as that same material for the duration of the effect.

The coposirix bends gravity around itself, causing it to fall toward its target. The coposirix Strides up to twice its Speed in a straight line in any direction. If it ends this movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, it can make a battering sludge Strike against that target. After this attack, the coposirix falls if it isn't on a solid surface, taking half the normal amount of damage for the distance fallen and not falling @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the coposirix passes within 10 feet of another creature at any point during this movement, that creature is affected by the fluctuating gravity and must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The creature can use the fluctuating gravity to its advantage to reposition itself. It can Step as a free action.

Success No effect.

Failure The creature is pulled 5 feet in the direction of the coposirix's travel. This follows the rules for forced movement. If at the end of this movement the creature is unsupported, it falls and lands prone, taking fall damage as normal.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is pulled 10 feet and falls prone even if supported.

details.publicNotes: The rapid and careless industrialization of Tabrid Minor ruined the planet, particularly ravaging the population of copi—the coral that's the ancestor of the native copaxi people. In areas where the copi's magical connection to gravity was strongest, however, the rampant pollution instead mutated it into grotesque new forms. In a twisted mockery of copaxi evolution, clusters of copi fused together to form coposirixes, shambling mounds of mutated copi fused with a partial shell of crystallized waste that crackles and creaks as it moves. The form of a coposirix constantly changes, sometimes even taking on hauntingly humanoid features that melt away just as quickly.

description.value:
Trigger The kyokor moves at least 60 feet

Effect The ground shakes in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:120] from the kyokor's space.

The ground becomes difficult terrain, and creatures on it are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 round. Ground vehicles move at half speed.

Fissures open above underground spaces (like sewers, sinkholes, or catacombs). Creatures in the area tumble into the resulting 40-foot-deep hole unless they succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:40] save. Near a body of water, these tremors creates dangerous waves. Creatures in the water or within 60 feet of the waterline are struck by waves that deal @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save). On a critical failure, a creature is instantly swept 60 feet out to sea and 60 feet under the water's surface.

details.description:
The footsteps of a kyokor (or other kaiju) shake the ground, causing the earth around it to tremble as though from a powerful earthquake.

When a kyokor passes by or through a settlement—or even attacks!—it leaves a trail of destruction that can be represented by the following hazards.

Terrifying, skyscraper-sized beasts roam the wastelands of Daimalko. Called colossi by the population, these monsters erupted from deep within the earth to stomp cities into dust, decimating the population and destroying every surface civilization on the planet in a terrifying Awakening. Kyokors are among the most dreaded colossi. There's no hope of defeating one without a valko—a war machine created by Daimalko's stubborn survivors to fight the colossi.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:39] (trained), @Check[civil-engineering-lore|dc:39] (trained), or @Check[crafting|dc:39] (trained) to brace a small structure or surface to cancel the effects of the quake in that area, @Check[arcana|dc:41] (legendary), @Check[occultism|dc:41] (legendary), or @Check[performance|dc:41] (legendary) to calm or divert the kyokor; three total successes are required to disable the hazard; @Check[piloting|dc:30] (trained) if in a vehicle to avoid the kyokor's path (disables the hazard only for that vehicle)
details.reset: The kyokor (or other kaiju) doesn't move this way again for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

description.value:
You manipulate the target's genetic code and accelerate the inevitable, pressuring the target to undergo carcinization and become a crab. A willing target fully transforms into a crab, as if it had cast animal form heightened to the same rank as this spell and using the following statistics.

  • Crab

    • Speed 25 feet, swim 15 feet
    • Melee 1 big claw, Damage 2d8 piercing
    • Melee 1 claw (agile), Damage 2d4 piercing.
    • Can breathe air and water.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.16Li37zRYKClxqsv]{Spell Effect: Carcinization}

Unwilling targets must instead attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target's body gains minor features of a crab, its appendages turning into claws and causing it to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}.

Failure The target transforms into a partial crab-like being, becoming clumsy 1, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is transformed into a partial crab-like being and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

You conjure a poisonous fog. This functions as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.9XHmC2JgTUIQ1CCm]{Mist}, except the area moves 10 feet away from you each round. You deal 6d8 poison damage to each breathing creature that starts its turn in the spell's area. You can Dismiss the spell.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You shape a wall of solid stone. You create a 1-inch-thick wall of stone up to 120 feet long, and 20 feet high. You can shape the wall's path, placing each 5 feet of the wall on the border between squares. The wall doesn't need to stand vertically, so you can use it to form a bridge or set of stairs, for example. You must conjure the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost.

Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the wall has AC 10, Hardness 14, and 50 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage. A destroyed section of the wall can be moved through, but the rubble created from it is difficult terrain.

Heightened (+2) The Hit Points of each section of the wall increase by 15.

You repel effects that would hinder a creature or slow its movement. While under this spell's effect, the target ignores effects that would give them a circumstance penalty to Speed. When they attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} an effect that has them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, they automatically succeed unless the effect is magical and of a higher rank than the unfettered movement spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.CdAyAiMGESvgNQtz]{Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement}

Using the weight of earth, you hamper a target's flight, with effects based on its Fortitude save. If the creature reaches the ground safely, it doesn't take falling damage.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target falls safely up to 120 feet.

Failure The target falls safely up to 120 feet. If it hits the ground, it can't Fly, levitate, or otherwise leave the ground for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target falls safely up to 120 feet. If it hits the ground, it can't Fly, levitate, or otherwise leave the ground for 1 minute.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

You create a barrier of gusting winds that hinders anything moving through it. The wall of swirling winds is 5 feet thick, 60 feet long, and 30 feet high. The wall stands vertically, but you can shape its path. Though the wall of wind distorts the air, it does not hamper sight. The wall has the following effects.

  • Ammunition from physical ranged attacks-such as arrows, bolts, sling bullets, and other objects of similar size-can't pass through the wall. Attacks with bigger ranged weapons, such as javelins, take a -2 circumstance penalty to their attack rolls if their paths pass through the wall. Massive ranged weapons and spell effects that don't create physical objects pass through the wall with no penalty.
  • The wall is difficult terrain to creatures attempting to move overland through it. Gases, including creatures in @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.V8wXOsoejQhe6CyG]{Vapor Form}, can't pass through the wall.
  • A creature that attempts to fly through the wall using a move action must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature can move through the wall normally this turn.

Success The flying creature can move through the wall this turn, but the wall is difficult terrain.

Failure The wall stops the movement of the flying creature, and any remaining movement from its current action is wasted.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is pushed 10 feet away from the wall.

area.details: all squares in a 20-foot-radius burst that contain plants

description.value:
Plants and fungi burst out or quickly grow, entangling creatures. All surfaces in the area are difficult terrain. Each round that a creature starts its turn in the area, it must attempt a Reflex save. On a failure, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it leaves the area, and on a critical failure, it's also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round. Creatures can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} to remove these effects.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.rjM25qfw5BKj9h97]{Spell Effect: Entangling Flora}

You shield the target against dangerous temperatures. Choose severe cold or heat. The target is protected from the temperature you chose (but not extreme cold or heat).

Heightened (3rd) The target is protected from severe cold and severe heat.

Heightened (5th) The target is protected from severe cold, severe heat, extreme cold, and extreme heat.

You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals. The spell doesn't make them more friendly than normal. Cunning animals are likely to be terse and evasive, while less intelligent ones often make inane comments.

The targets can breathe underwater.

Heightened (3rd) The duration increases to 8 hours.

Heightened (4th) The duration increases to until your next daily preparations.

You reinforce the target's musculoskeletal system to bear more weight. The target can carry 3 more Bulk than normal before becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.D5mg6Tc7Jzrj6ro7]{Encumbered} and up to a maximum of 6 more Bulk.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.5yCL7InrJDHpaQjz]{Spell Effect: Ant Haul}

You fling a large glob of acid that immediately detonates, spraying nearby creatures. Creatures in the area take 1d8 acid damage with a basic Reflex save; on a critical failure, the creature also takes @Damage[(1+floor((@item.rank -1)/2))[persistent,acid]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a critical failure increases by 1.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You detect whether a creature is venomous or poisonous, or if an object is poison or has been poisoned. You do not ascertain whether the target is poisonous in multiple ways, nor do you learn the type or types of poison. Certain substances, like lead and alcohol, are poisons and so mask other poisons.

Heightened (2nd) You learn the number and types of poison.

You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

A vine appears from thin air, flicking from your hand and lashing itself to the target. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.TwtUIEyenrtAbeiX]{Spell Effect: Tangle Vine}

Critical Success The target gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} condition and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. It can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} against your spell DC to remove the penalty and the immobilized condition.

Success The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. It can attempt to Escape against your spell DC to remove the penalty.

Failure The target is unaffected.

Heightened (2nd) The effects last for 2 rounds.

Heightened (4th) The effects last for 1 minute.

Trigger The dragon critically fails a saving throw

Effect Swarming insects interfere with the effect, sacrificing themselves to protect their host's body and mind. The dragon attempts a @Check[flat|dc:17]. On a success, the dragon fails the saving throw instead of critically failing.

90 feet. @Check[will|dc:30|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. mental

description.value: A creature that takes damage from a host dragon's jaws Strike or is swallowed by the dragon is also bitten by hundreds of tiny insects; the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 round.
The host dragon is immune to any attack made by a swarm of insects or similar creature (at the GM's discretion).

Large, @Damage[(3d10+7)[bludgeoning]] plus excruciating bites, Rupture 26

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

The dragon disgorges a swarm of biting insects that deals @Damage[9d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40] (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save). They can't use Swarm Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Swarm Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon transforms into an insect swarm. They drop all held, worn, and carried items. While in this form, they can't use attack actions (except Swarming Bites and Swarm Breath) and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their individual insects to fit without having to Squeeze. They can use the same action to coalesce back into their normal form.

Requirements The dragon is a swarm

Effect Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[8d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

details.publicNotes: Innumerable insects follow these lumbering, ravenous dragons wherever they roam, swarming around them and living in the countless orifices along the host dragon's body. The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them. Host dragons despise answering questions and violently defy all attempts others make to understand their nature.

The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them.
