A flashlight sheds bright light. A commercial flashlight emits light in a 30-foot emanation (and dim light in the next 30 feet).

These contain analgesics, bandages, sterile gauze, and suturing tools necessary for Medicine checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.MHLuKy4nQO2Z4Am1]{Administer First Aid}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.TC7OcDa7JlWbqMaN]{Treat Disease}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.KjoCEEmPGTeFE4hh]{Treat Poison}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds}.

Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the robot with an attack

Effect The robot overclocks its processing power to dodge, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.

After receiving a request for information about a named location or a local law from a non-hostile creature, the ordinance-class robot spends 5 minutes to consult its database, automatically succeeding at a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} check to answer the question. At the GM's discretion, the information might not be available to the robot.

The ordinance-class civil robot scans all creatures in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15], attempting a single Legal Lore check and comparing the result to the Deception DC of each creature in the area. If the robot succeeds and the creature is known to have broken local laws, the robot gains a +1 circumstance bonus to Strikes and Athletics checks to [[/act grapple]] the creature for 24 hours.

details.publicNotes:
Ordinance-class civil robots exist to help others learn the local laws, navigate around a location, and generally remind everyone to follow the rules. What they lack in raw strength, they make up for with an extensive database of knowledge. Most of these robots are designed to mimic the shape of prevalent humanoid ancestries, and thus the standard configuration for such robots includes two arms and two legs. Some have been modified with extra attachments, providing maps produced from a rectangular opening in their chest plate or featuring touchscreens that allow passersby to consult every ordinance, law, and other legal documents relevant to the area.

Some pranksters enjoy leaving doodles and other drawings in spots the robot can't easily detect, and thus an ordinance-class civil robot that has been operating for an extensive period of time can be found with a rainbow of shapes, colors, and the occasional advertisement placed on its back.

Civil robots can be found in almost any large settlement, employed by the local government. They aren't meant to function as keepers of the peace, but instead perform minor functions, such as collecting refuse, aiding citizens with information, or collecting taxes. However, they can still defend themselves against vandals if necessary.

You choose the target for each shard individually. If you shoot more than one shard at the same target, combine the damage before applying bonuses or penalties to damage, resistances, weaknesses, and so forth.

Heightened (+2) You fire one additional shard with each action you spend.

details.publicNotes:
A haeshi-shaa in their haeshi form is aggressive and persistent, unlikely to back down from an argument or a fight unless their life is in danger. While aggressive, haeshis are far from foolish; they prepare carefully for any encounter and often have dozens of nested contingencies to fall back on should any situation evolve beyond their control.

Haeshi-shaas are relatively new members of the Dominion of the Black. These long-lived creatures shift unpredictably between a liquid and a gaseous form in a mysterious cycle that lasts their entire lives. In their liquid haeshi form, they're a churning, chunky fluid, while in their vaporous shaa form, they're more akin to hazy brown fog. Regardless of their form, they hum with atonal harmonies that cause great discomfort in others.

Haeshi-shaas serve as a sort of priest caste for the Dominion of the Black, each form preaching a doctrine of entropy and annihilation, though with slightly different nuances between each form. Haeshis tend toward assertive, aggressive practices, while shaas are meditative, often spending many months or years in contemplation. Haeshi-shaas believe that the most perfect and divine state of any object or being is complete separation into its most basic, individual particles. They claim (dubiously) that they can see, even predict, the journey of all things as they travel toward this ultimate entropic end.

In truth, haeshi-shaas can't even predict when they'll shift into their alternate form. Some have tried to catalyze the change by seeking out specific types of life experience or knowledge, but despite the many claims to the contrary, there are no verified instances of a haeshi-shaa forcing the change. Most choose to simply await their next transformation, during which time they have many centuries to spread the Dominion's disturbing doctrines and pursue their inscrutable agendas.

When the scrap rat's tail Strike deals damage to an object or creature with the tech trait, the tail also gains the following traits until the beginning of the scrap rat's next turn: deadly d4, hampering, and razing.

A proog can spend 1 minute altering its appearance to imitate any solid object of 1 Bulk or less, though it can't duplicate moving parts or specialized functions. A mimicking proog gains a +2 Circumstance bonus to Deception checks to conceal its true nature. Touching the proog negates this bonus.
Each enemy in the proog swarm's space takes @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic] save).

details.publicNotes:
While individually cute and relatively harmless, when proogs gather in greater numbers, they become quite dangerous.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

Upgrades: 1

This snub-nosed scattergun is commonly used to blast through doors and walls. Smaller than a reaction breacher, this scattergun is infamous for its mighty recoil.

items.name: Integrated tools appropriate for disguise shell

description.value: The circuitry of an assassin robot self-destructs upon the robot's destruction, making tracing its owner extremely difficult. Add 10 to the DC of a Computers check to recover data from a destroyed assassin robot.

The demolition-class assassin robot attempts to close the distance between it and its target, removing all obstacles in its way. The robot makes a ranged Strike with its breaching gun against the Force Open DC of a door or other obstacle. If successful, the obstacle gains the broken condition. The robot then Strides twice toward (and even through) the obstacle, but if the obstacle still bars the robot's movement, it stops adjacent to the obstacle and attempts an Athletics check to [[/act force-open]] the obstacle with a +4 circumstance bonus.

The demolition-class assassin robot enters or leaves its disguised form. While in its disguised form, it loses the ability to make Strikes or use its Breaching Rush ability, but it becomes indistinguishable from a noncombatant robot or drone model that it's based on; it gets an automatic result of 33 on Deception checks and DCs to pass as a robot of that model. Damage to the robot can prevent this disguise from being effective at the GM's discretion.
The demolition-class assassin robot's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

The demolition-class assassin robot establishes a target lock on a creature it can see within 120 feet. The creature can't become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} against the robot or [[/act create-a-diversion]] against the robot. This ability lasts until the robot loses line of sight with the target or the target otherwise becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}.

details.publicNotes:
Named for their tendency toward extreme violence and aggressiveness, demolition robots nevertheless start their mission with stealth being a priority. Outwardly, these robots conceal their true nature with an outer casing resembling that of industrial, civic, or even domestic robots and drones.

Once the target has been detected, all stealth flies out the window as these killer machines unfurl their weapons and sensory suite from hidden panels and rush toward the mark at full speed. With their encyclopedic data banks on the stress points and emergency release mechanisms of most doors, only the most solid barriers can hold them at bay for long.

In some societies, machines automate many processes. It only makes sense, then, that some would attempt to automate the act of murder. Most manufacturers would never admit to creating such designs, forcing wealthy figures to go through hidden channels to acquire units from secret facilities or unscrupulous manufacturers. Regardless of their origins, these robots can be programmed with a target and set loose, the owner assured that either their target will be dead or will be unable to prove who tried to kill them thanks to the unit's fried circuitry.

description.value:
The target hears ominous sounds containing unspeakable mysteries from beyond the stars that disorient its mind. The target attempts a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The target hears the strange whispers, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The target begins to wander aimlessly, using each action to Step in a random direction. When it moves, the target takes the safest route and doesn't enter hazardous terrain. The spell has a duration of 1 minute or until a creature uses a hostile action against the target.

Critical Failure As failure, except the target babbles incoherently. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of the target must attempt a Will saving throw. On a failure, it must use its first action on its next turn to Step in a random direction and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. This is an incapacitation effect.

You create a shroud of darkness that prevents light from penetrating or emanating within the area. Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a torch or lantern, do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical light of your darkness spell's rank or lower. Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. From outside, it appears as a globe of pure darkness.

Heightened (4th) Even creatures with darkvision (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the darkness as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

You draw on a creature's thoughts of home and the comfort it finds in that concept, overwhelming the creature with wistful memories of whatever home means to it. The target must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the memories.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

Effect The driftdead teleports up to its Speed. If it's in the Drift, the distance a driftdead can teleport is doubled.

While inhabiting a ship in this manner, the crasher imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to all Piloting checks attempted by the ship's pilot. Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round.

details.publicNotes:
While most driftdead are spirits inhabiting their own corpses, some souls get ejected from their shells at death. These wraiths desperately struggle to return to their bodies, following their fragmented memories down aimless paths—woe to the living who cross them.

Crashers don't actually take their name from the Drift Crash that created many of them—they earned this name for their tendency to possess starships and reroute them into collision courses. If passengers trapped aboard a possessed ship don't survive, the victims might themselves twist into crashers instead of departing for the afterlife.

Driftdead are created when a restless soul fuses with planar energies and reanimates its own corpse into a disturbing parody of life. Rarely, disturbed spirits snatched from elsewhere fuse together to inhabit a single dead body. Any death within the Drift might spawn a driftdead, but creatures that die in a frenzy of intense emotion are more likely to make the transition.

If the globe overlaps with an area of magical darkness, sunburst attempts to counteract the darkness effect.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d10, and the vitality damage against undead increases by 1d10.
You send the creature a mental message of 25 words or fewer, and it can respond immediately with its own message of 25 words or fewer.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. divine

description.value: 60 feet. All allied creatures in the aura gain a +5-foot status bonus to Speeds (if the dragon is photon-attuned), or all enemies take a –5-foot status penalty to Speeds (if the dragon is graviton-attuned).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

The cosmic dragon is always attuned to an underlying stellar force: either photons or gravitons. They can swap attunement as a free action once at the start of their turn in combat and is otherwise attuned to whichever force they choose at any time outside of combat. The dragon's melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage while they're photon-attuned and an additional @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage plus Knockdown while they're graviton-attuned. The damage of their cosmic blast Strike is likewise determined by their current attunement, as are the effects of some of their other abilities. The dragon can choose to exclude any creatures from any effect of the aura or action that uses the aura.

The dragon can always sense the distance and direction to the nearest Drift beacon and to the Starstone on Absalom Station.
The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Stellar Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

The dragon unleashes divine power like that from the core of a star or black hole, dealing @Damage[10d10[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:40] (@Check[will|dc:34|basic|options:area-effect] save). The dragon can't use Stellar Breath for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon conjures a mote of stellar energy that shares their current attunement. They direct the mote to a square within 90 feet. While the mote is deployed, the dragon's attunement aura also emanates from that square, with the same emanation radius. The dragon can Sustain to move the mote up to 90 feet or to recall the mote from any distance. The mote is made of cosmic energy, doesn't occupy a space, and can't be targeted or destroyed by any effect.

The dragon imposes an effect on creatures within their attunement aura. The effect is based on the dragon's current attunement. The dragon can't use Stellar Pulse again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Stellar Pulse]]{1d4 rounds}.

  • Graviton Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:34|options:area-effect] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

  • Photon Each creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

details.publicNotes: These majestic dragons embody the stellar forces that underly and balance all that exists in the universe. Cosmic dragons are wise, social, and patient beings, accepting of all whom they come across; they see all creatures as equal components of the universe and are just as likely to be found discussing astrophysics in the company of scholars as they are to be found lamenting the twists and turns of fate in the company of the downtrodden. Most cosmic dragons can change their shape, though they usually only do so to make other creatures comfortable. They detest deception and always introduce themselves as dragons even while in another form.

description.value:
You can ask questions of and receive answers from natural or worked stone. While stone is not intelligent, you speak with the natural spirits of the stone, which have a personality colored by the type of stone, as well as by the type of structure the stone is part of, for worked stone. A stone's perspective, perception, and knowledge give it a worldview different enough from a human's that it doesn't consider the same details important. Stones can mostly answer questions about creatures that touched them in the past and what is concealed beneath or behind them.

Heightened (6th) The duration is 8 hours.

Your target's skin becomes a thick carapace of solid stone, granting resistance 5 to physical damage, except adamantine. The target gains a fist unarmed attack that deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The target uses their highest weapon or unarmed attack proficiency with these attacks, and if they have weapon specialization or greater weapon specialization, they add this damage as well. On a critical hit with one of these unarmed attacks, the creature struck must succeed at a Fortitude save against the target's class DC or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.v724q5Xi7KPPnLQ3]{Spell Effect: Cairn Form}

Heightened (6th) The resistance increases to 10, and the damage dealt by the attacks increases to 3d6.

Heightened (10th) The resistance increases to 20, and the damage dealt by the attacks increases to 4d6.

You can either transform into a stone or merge with stone. While transformed, you can't move or affect anything outside the stone, but you can cast spells as long as they don't require line of effect beyond the stone. You can Dismiss this spell.

  • Merge with Stone The spell's duration is 10 minutes. While casting the spell, you must touch a stone with enough volume to fit you and your possessions or the spell is disrupted. While merged, you can hear, but not see, what's going on outside the stone. If the stone takes damage while you're inside it, you're expelled from the stone and take 10d6 damage. Magic passage expels you without dealing damage. The spell ends if you're ever outside the stone.
  • Turn into a Stone The spell's duration is 8 hours. You become a Large stone. Perception checks don't reveal your true nature, but a successful Nature or Survival check against your spell DC reveals that you appear to be a stone that is strangely new to the area. While in this form, you can observe everything around you, using your normal senses. As a stone, your AC is 23, and only status bonuses, status penalties, circumstance bonuses, and circumstance penalties affect you. Any successes and critical successes you roll on Reflex saves are failures

A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.con2Hzt47JjpuUej]{Spell Effect: Resist Energy}

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

You free the target's limbs from ailments that impede mobility. Attempt to counteract an effect of your choice imposing one of these conditions on the target: @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}. If you didn't counteract the effect, but you would have if its counteract rank were 2 lower, instead suppress the effect until the beginning of your next turn. The effect's duration doesn't elapse while it's suppressed.

This spell can't counteract or suppress curses, diseases, or conditions that are part of the target's normal state.

Heightened (4th) Add @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} to the list of conditions.

Heightened (6th) As 4th rank, plus add @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dTwPJuKgBQCMxixg]{Petrified}

Heightened (8th) As 4th rank, plus add petrified and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned}.

You create an illusion that causes the target to appear more charming and genteel to observers. The spell accentuates the target's appearance, clothing, voice, and mannerisms according to what an observer finds appealing. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

The target gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression} and lets the target add its level to Diplomacy checks if untrained. In addition, the target can attempt a Diplomacy check to Gather Information in only 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hOp5ybOzyJlECU6B]{Spell Effect: Glow Up}

Heightened (+2) The bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th rank.

A spray of heavy rocks flies through the air in front of you. The rubble deals 2d4 bludgeoning damage to each creature in the area. Each creature must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is pushed 5 feet away from you.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage by 2d4.

You analyze the natural environment in the area within 1 mile, learning general information about the atmosphere, biomes, and gravity. You forecast the weather for the next hour, including the direction it will come from; learn which atmospheres, biomes, and gravity are in the area, as well as the direction and distance to those elements; and learn if anything in the area presents an environmental hazard, including cold or heat dangers (at what temperature range), radiation (at what severity), smoke effects, and underwater conditions. This is enough to detect large effects that span spaces over 30 feet in diameter only. You gain a general sense of where and how far away these dangers are.

Heightened (+1) The scanned area increases in radius by 1 mile.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

While on their bonded celestial body, a nymph can sense approaching starships within a day's travel from the celestial body. This doesn't give them any information about the ship, its occupants, or its exact position. The nymph can send a message to the ship as if using a personal comm unit.

A sterieid is mystically bonded to a small asteroid, no more than 1 mile across. If they're away from their bonded asteroid for more than 24 hours, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save each additional 24 hours or increase their drained value by 1. A sterieid can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond themself to a new asteroid.

Trigger The sterieid takes physical damage

Effect Shards of rocky debris fly off the sterieid's body, dealing @Damage[2d12[bludgeoning]] damage to all adjacent creatures (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save) and giving the sterieid the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition until the end of their next turn.

10 feet. A sterieid's body radiates an icy chill. Creatures entering or starting their turns in the aura take @Damage[2d6[cold]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save); a creature that critically fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the start of its next turn as it grows sluggish from the cold.

details.publicNotes:
Sterieids reside on asteroids, relishing the beauty of their rocky, icy surfaces. Due to the ubiquity of asteroids, sterieids are the most common type of space nymph. Since many asteroids exist near one another in asteroid belts, sterieids are more likely to form communities among their own kind than other nymphs. Conversely, they tend to be wary of outsiders, despite visitors being less of a novelty. Those sterieids who desire company, however, build way stations on their asteroids for passing starships, offering food, amenities, and information about the local system.

Mining is a contentious issue for sterieids, who usually react with hostility to such operations. Most mining corporations will survey an asteroid to ensure it isn't protected by a sterieid before setting up shop, though less ethical companies have been known to pay mercenaries to "relocate" sterieid guardians of asteroids that contain valuable resources.

Nymphs are beautiful fey who appear as humanoids with elven features. They generally take female forms, but nymphs of all genders and presentations exist. While many nymphs live on planets and act as protectors of natural ponds, forests, caves, and cliffsides, other nymphs—often called space nymphs—are known for their association with celestial bodies. Like their planet-bound kin, space nymphs guard their bonded territories from exploitation or harm.

As admirers of the arts, nymphs are especially friendly toward creatives, and offering to depict a nymph in story, picture, or song is a sure way to win their favor—unless the nymph doesn't like the final result and lashes out at the artist for insulting them with a mediocre product. Vidcasting and livestreaming are popular pastimes among more sociable nymphs, whether to show off their remote environments or find audiences for performances. Some nymphs entice movie crews to film in their territories, with two provisos: first, that the production not damage the environment, and second, that the nymph receive a prominent role in the film.

Unless their territories are threatened, most space nymphs are friendly or indifferent to mortals, but they have long memories. Anyone visiting a space nymph's territory would be wise to research that nymph's grudges, past encounters with mortals, or any recent events in the area that might color the nymph's perspective of those traveling near their home.

A mortal who demonstrates the right attitude and abilities, such as by protecting a stellar body from destruction or creating an especially beautiful work of art, might draw a space nymph's attention. From time to time, space nymphs will offer their services as mentors to solarians or mystics, though they seldom seek pupils on their own. Space nymphs rarely enter into more than one such arrangement at a time and typically only make commitments that promise to last decades or centuries, so they're extremely selective in their choice of mentee.

description.value:
A powerful globe of searing sunlight explodes in the area, dealing 8d10 fire damage to all creatures in the area, plus an additional @Damage[(@item.level+1)d10[vitality]] damage to undead creatures. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} permanently.

If the globe overlaps with an area of magical darkness, sunburst attempts to counteract the darkness effect.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d10, and the vitality damage against undead increases by 1d10.

A well of desolate darkness scours all life, dealing 10d10 void damage to all living creatures in the area. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save. If void scour overlaps with an area of magical light or affects a creature affected by magical light, void scour attempts to counteract the light effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and @Damage[(@item.rank - 6)d6[persistent,void]] damage, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and @Damage[(@item.rank - 5)d6[persistent,void]] damage, and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the initial void damage by 2d10. The persistent void damage increases by 1d6 on a failure, or by 2d6 on critical failure.

You create a sudden vacuum, forcing creatures in the area to hold their breath or fall @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} and start suffocating. The explosive decompression deals 5d12 bludgeoning damage to each creature in the area when the spell is cast or that enters the area during the duration (unless they were already in a vacuum), with a basic Fortitude save. Creatures who critically fail the save are also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12.

You cast your thoughts through a creature's mind, sifting for information. You access the target's memories and knowledge unless it fends you off with a Will save.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure Each round of the spell's duration, you can Sustain the spell to ask a different question and attempt to uncover the answer. For each question, the target can attempt a Deception check against your spell DC; if the target succeeds, you don't learn the answer, and on a critical success, the target gives you a false answer that you believe is truthful Once you've asked the target a given question, asking it again, even with a separate casting of mind probe, produces the same result.

Critical Failure As failure, and the target takes a –4 circumstance penalty to Deception checks against your questions.

You deter enemies with a wave of hostile intent. The target takes 7d8 nonlethal mental damage, and the target must attempt a Will save. The psychic energy then arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the wave at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome).

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected, and the wave ends with that target.

Success The target takes half damage.

Failure The target takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The target takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d8.

target.value: 1 creature, plus any number of additional creatures

description.value:
Your target's skin becomes a thick carapace of solid stone, granting resistance 5 to physical damage, except adamantine. The target gains a fist unarmed attack that deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The target uses their highest weapon or unarmed attack proficiency with these attacks, and if they have weapon specialization or greater weapon specialization, they add this damage as well. On a critical hit with one of these unarmed attacks, the creature struck must succeed at a Fortitude save against the target's class DC or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.v724q5Xi7KPPnLQ3]{Spell Effect: Cairn Form}

Heightened (6th) The resistance increases to 10, and the damage dealt by the attacks increases to 3d6.

Heightened (10th) The resistance increases to 20, and the damage dealt by the attacks increases to 4d6.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

Applying a combination of vital energy and acupressure, you help the target regain its footing. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition if it has it. The target becomes immune to the off-guard condition until the start of your next turn, even if the condition would be circumstantially applied, such as by being flanked or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The target is then immune to reorient for 10 minutes.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

The moon giant changes gravity in a 20-foot radius burst anywhere within 60 feet. Gravity in the area is treated as one step higher or lower, such as from zero gravity to low gravity, for 1 minute or until they use this ability again, whichever comes first.

60 feet. The moon giant can choose waxing or waning at the start of each of their turns. When the aura is waxing, a living creature that starts its turn in the aura gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} condition and can use this extra action to only to Stride or Strike. When the aura is waning, a creature that isn't a moon giant that enters the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33] save. This is an incapacitation effect. It's then temporarily immune to the effects of the waning aura for 24 hours.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} while in the aura.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} while in the aura.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} but can still wake up due to a successful Perception check. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

details.publicNotes:
Moon giants are among the largest humanoids, standing approximately 24 feet tall. Their weight varies greatly depending on the locality, as their mass increases with the number of lunar bodies orbiting their current location, though this quality never seems to affect their mobility. Moon giants are calm and even-tempered for the most part, but lunar phases and celestial occurrences influence them easily, and anyone approaching them would be wise to consult the stars first. These giants often work as oracles, receiving gifts or offerings from pilgrims in exchange for their services.

Giants are enormous humanoids of many varieties who live all across the galaxy.

description.value: The biotech of a biotech zombie can be disabled for 1 hour by a creature that succeeds at a @Check[medicine|dc:18] of Life Science Lore check. Attempting to do so can typically be done only while within reach of the zombie and is a 2-action activity that has the manipulate trait. A biotech zombie with disabled biotech is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use any reactions.

A biotech zombie must use this reaction unless its biotech is disabled

Trigger The zombie is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The zombie melts into a caustic pool of biological goo, dealing @Damage[2d8[acid]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save) and making that area hazardous terrain for 1 minute (1 acid damage). This ability destroys any organic items on the zombie (including biotech).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The biotech zombie breathes a blast of flame that deals @Damage[4d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save). The zombie can't use Dragon Gland again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Dragon Gland]]{1d4 rounds}.

Upgrades: 1

This kasathan sword breaks down into interlocking pieces for storage and transport. Traditionalists practice the art of ritually assembling puzzleblades, with different pieces symbolizing the kasathan virtues of conviction, dedication, moderation, and tradition. Criminals and smugglers value the puzzleblade for its discreet design.

Upgrades: 1

This pistol automatically ejects used cartridges and loads new ammunition after every shot fired.

A hacking toolkit is required for Computers checks to Hack computers. You can use a hacking toolkit to access a computer without using a user interface, but this requires physical contact with the computer or contact via an infosphere or a similar linked network. An advanced hacking toolkit adds a +1 item bonus to checks made with it and allows you to access computers at a range of 30 feet, unless you're in a radioactive environment.

You need an infiltrator's toolkit to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick Locks} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable Devices} (of some types) using the Thievery skill. A tactical infiltrator's toolkit adds a +1 item bonus to checks to Pick Locks or Disable Devices.

An emissary-class infiltrator robot is covered in a layer of synthetic programmable skin that counts as a disguise kit. The robot can alter the skin over the course of a minute to Impersonate a Medium humanoid ancestry or specific person (if it has information about that person or has previously interacted with them). If the robot makes a retractable spike Strike or uses Morphing Visage, it takes a –4 circumstance penalty to its Deception DCs and skill checks to Impersonate against any who witnessed the action.
An emissary-class infiltrator robot's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to sickened creatures.

By sending ripples through its synthetic skin, the emissary-class infiltration robot creates a nauseating display that affects all creatures within 20 feet that can see it. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to the robot's Morphing Visage for 10 minutes.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

languages.details: Common plus up to three other languages

details.publicNotes:
While many of the earliest infiltrator robots were designed for more direct stealth missions, the emissary-class infiltrator robots were created for more insidious tasks. These include planting evidence, spreading rumors and propaganda, and generally creating unrest or fomenting open rebellion.

Programmed with a sophisticated virtual intelligence and constructed with the ability to disguise itself as a creature of flesh and blood, an emissary-class infiltrator robot can wreak havoc on an organization or settlement without ever having to engage in combat. They are considered the ultimate tool in corporate or governmental espionage and fetch a high price on most illegal markets.

Brought to the forefront during the war against the Veskarium, and further perfected by various corporate interests within the Pact Worlds, infiltrator robots are known for being able to get into places where no one else could. Generally small and agile, infiltrator robots sneak into enemy territory and either download precious data or record incriminating evidence.

description.value:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet that the veolisk can see starts its turn

Effect The target must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save. If it fails, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the end of the veolisk's next turn from distracting thoughts of the emptiness of space.

The veolisk makes a jaws Strike against a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} creature, which gains the deadly d10 trait. If the target attempts the flat check to recover from its confusion when it takes damage from this Strike, it rolls twice and takes the lower result.

The veolisk's eyes exude the dark, claustrophobic aspects of deep space. A creature the veolisk can see within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save. If it fails, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the end of the veolisk's next turn. On a critical failure, or on a failure if the creature was already off-guard from this ability or Void Glance, the creature instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} until the end of the veolisk's next turn.

details.publicNotes:
Veolisks, formidable creatures from remote planets within the Vast, are reminiscent of prehistoric megafauna, despite their relatively smaller size. These lumbering quadrupeds, characterized by their reptilian features and bony exoskeletons, evoke the mystery and fear often associated with ancient colossal creatures. The reason for their seclusion becomes apparent as most xenobiologists posit that the creature's gaze, capable of inducing mental distress in other species, renders habitation near veolisk territory a daunting challenge. Encounters with veolisks near established communities lead to ruthless hunts.

The creatures, with their signature Void Gaze ability, have sparked speculation about their lineage. Some theories propose that veolisks descended from ancestors with more potent gaze attacks, earning them the moniker "void basilisks" among those versed in Lost Golarion's ancient folklore. An intriguing hypothesis suggests that millennia ago, destruction-loving cultists of the Devourer might have selectively bred the first veolisks, a narrative predating the Gap

Despite their fearsome reputation, veolisks find utility as guardians for Devourer cult bases and even as pets to individuals embracing the chaos of the universe. Interestingly, veolisks are docile and harmless when equipped with blinders, veils, or other means to nullify their gaze—a quirk rarely explored in cross-cultural attempts at domestication.

Surviving on minimal sustenance, veolisks consume a single meal daily, feeding on flesh, fruits, fungus, or any available plant life. The physiological systems of veolisks remain a subject of study among scientists. Theories range from hyperefficient metabolisms to feeding on cosmic radiation, akin to trees photosynthesizing sunlight. A fringe notion suggests veolisks might subsist solely on background arcane energies pervasive throughout the galaxy, challenging the boundaries of scientific understanding.

The dragon transforms into an insect swarm. They drop all held, worn, and carried items. While in this form, they can't use attack actions (except Swarming Bites and Swarm Breath) and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their individual insects to fit without having to Squeeze. They can use the same action to coalesce back into their normal form.

details.publicNotes: Innumerable insects follow these lumbering, ravenous dragons wherever they roam, swarming around them and living in the countless orifices along the host dragon's body. The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them. Host dragons despise answering questions and violently defy all attempts others make to understand their nature.

You create an illusion that causes the target to appear as another creature of the same body shape, and with roughly similar height (within 6 inches) and weight (within 50 pounds). The disguise is typically good enough to hide their identity, but not to impersonate a specific individual. The spell changes their appearance and voice, but not mannerisms. You can change the appearance of its clothing and worn items, such as making its armor look like a dress. You can Dismiss this spell.

details.publicNotes:
Many poor souls who encounter a gnackle don't realize until it's too late. These gremlins relish watching other creatures suffer humiliating failure and often spend weeks or longer selecting and grooming a victim into bringing out their own downfall. After reading their target's mind to learn what they want most, gnackles then magically disguise themselves, presenting to their target as a fairy godmother or similar granter of wishes and convincing their victims of some great destiny. They offer a magical "gift" and encourage the target toward acts of greater and greater ambition. However, once gnackles deem their victims in a suitably embarrassing or dangerous situation, they twist the gift into a terrible curse.

Gremlins have plagued the Universe with cruel tricks since long before the Gap, and they've fully adapted to a galaxy filled with technology. Whether meddling with a comm unit, a starship, or an entire space station, gremlins delight in ruination and chaos.

details.publicNotes:
Alloids are interplanetary scavengers sent by the Dominion of the Black to plague the galaxy. These tall, thin, and spindly creatures appear to have metallic sheens to their smooth skin, often in copper or gold tones. An alloid's surprisingly pliable physiology allows them to mold their physical form to fit into tight spaces or stretch paper-thin against surfaces. It's rumored they can, with great effort, even mimic other people.

While few enough people have encountered alloids for the name to truly catch on, they've become colloquially known as "ship stealers" in the Pact Worlds. They frequently travel in groups of three to five, hanging around spaceports and scoping out potential marks. They often look for smaller ships with crews not much larger than their pack or massive ships with many hiding places. They then slip aboard inside containers or backpacks, often during resupply cycles.

Once the ship is out in space and the crew settled in, the pack gets to work, ideally taking out their targets one by one with barbs that make their prey's flesh as malleable as their own, allowing them to easily hide the bodies. After kidnapping a crew, they fly the ship to places where they can link up with other alloids—or their Dominion masters—to strip the starship for parts. What they do with their living prey is unknown.

Before the Gap, the Dominion of the Black had a strong presence on Aucturn and would occasionally invade other planets, such as Lost Golarion, in search of brains to devour and in pursuit of other mysterious agendas. Now, the people of the Pact Worlds understand that the Dominion has secret bases across the galaxy—the Pact Worlds might draw their attention from time to time, but their missions take them to many worlds and many star systems.

The Dominion creatures most commonly seen by others are mere scouts, while more powerful members of the Dominion conglomeration emerge only rarely to terrorize society. Stories of these creatures are as few as the scant number of people who survive such encounters—most spacefarers know to immediately flee any ship or territory with signs of Dominion presence.

description.value: A squox can [[/act disarm skill=acrobatics]] and [[/act trip skill=acrobatics]] creatures with its tail using Acrobatics instead of Athletics, even if it doesn't have a free hand. A squox can attempt to Disarm or Trip @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures up to two sizes larger than it and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Disarm or Trip an off-guard target

details.publicNotes:
The tiny vulkarikis are a common animal in the Pact Worlds, originating from the planet Castrovel. Human explorers from Lost Golarion gave them the name "squox," as they interpreted the creatures as being a mix of fox and squirrel. Squoxes are cunning to the point of being devious and employ scare tactics to routinely abscond with shiny items they uncover on their travels. They exhibit complex social behaviors, including a distinct hierarchy between individuals in a skulk, and have a vast vocal repertoire used to assign signature sounds to different individual squoxes.

Having long since been exported from the Pact Worlds, squoxes are found throughout the galaxy, with many civilizations in Near Space maintaining growing communities of these tiny furry creatures, much to the growing consternation of the Xenowardens who worry that the highly adaptable squox has become an invasive species in several biomes. While some advocate for culling these populations, the popularity of the animal as a pet has made this view an incredibly unpopular stance.

Young squoxes are referred to as kits and can be purchased from the open market on most Pact Worlds. Squoxes tend to live for about 10 years. A typical squox weighs about 10 pounds and is about 3 feet long, with much of its length being its tail. Squoxes come in all colorations, and many even take on unique environmental adaptations to specific climates, such as desert-dwelling squoxes growing longer ears to help disperse heat.

You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

description.value:
You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

Gubbins can spread their glitchiness to others. Creatures damaged by the gubbin's claw or glitchy beam Strikes must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical hit). If the target has the tech trait, it becomes glitching instead of sickened.

details.publicNotes:
Gubbins—also known as data gremlins—are spontaneously born from corrupted data, especially as a result of errors in artificial intelligence programs. While gubbins aren't particularly powerful, they're notoriously hard to hit due to the way their bodies flicker and jolt through space as if slightly out of sync with the rest of the world. Gubbins are most easily set apart from other gremlins by their fingers, which vary in number from moment to moment and sprout from illogical locations on their hands, such as the backs or palms. While lacking in fine mobility, their warped hands are disturbing to observe in motion, a fact they delightfully use to their advantage.

Due to being weaponized in corporate espionage efforts and later multiplying out of control, gubbins are one of the most ubiquitous types of gremlin on heavily computerized planets. While not as malevolent as other gremlins, gubbins delight in irrevocably corrupting computer data, which they do for their own amusement as well as to create more gubbins. Gubbins are often found where large amounts of data are stored, accompanied by corrupted technological constructs such as hardlight life-forms or robots.

Gremlins have plagued the Universe with cruel tricks since long before the Gap, and they've fully adapted to a galaxy filled with technology. Whether meddling with a comm unit, a starship, or an entire space station, gremlins delight in ruination and chaos.

details.publicNotes:
Adult nyssholoras band together in pairs only when mating and raising a newly hatched brood, during which time they consider the borders between their territory communal—they're otherwise extremely territorial.

Incredibly ferocious, nyssholoras are dinosaur-like creatures that number among the most dangerous and feared apex predators on the planet of Castrovel. These walking weapons brandish lethal biology: a massive maw lined with twin rows of razor-sharp teeth, scythe-shaped phasic blade forearms that vibrate at ultrasonic speeds, and a scourge-like tail capable of absorbing electricity from the surrounding atmosphere. Yet, nyssholoras are also cunning and perceptive—a frill of tentacles that crowns their head detect telepathic signals, making it impossible for the local formians and lashuntas to escape their notice. Their phasic blades can cut through walls and vehicles as easily as underbrush, ensuring prey have nowhere safe to hide.

description.value:
You slice through reality, forming a chasm of life-draining entropy in a straight line 20 feet high, 120 feet long, and 2 inches thick. The chasm provides cover. The chasm deals 7d8 void damage to each creature that's in the chasm's space when it's created, that attempts to pass through the chasm, or that ends its turn inside the chasm, with a basic Reflex save. A creature that succeeds at this save when the chasm is created is pushed to the nearest space on the side of its choice. Creatures trying to move through the chasm fail to do so if they critically fail the save, ending their movement next to the chasm.

The psychic energy then arcs to another creature within 30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the wave at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once, and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome).

Critical Success The target creature is unaffected, and the wave ends with that target.

You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You unleash a beam of divine energy. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, the target takes 2d4 spirit damage (double damage on a critical hit).

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

You touch a weapon with no remaining ammunition and recharge it with magical energy. The target gains ammunition or charges equal to its expend until the end of your next turn, allowing anyone holding the target to make one Strike with the weapon. This spell only recharges weapons that use ammunition with a cost of 10 credits or less.

Heightened (3rd) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to double its expend.

Heightened (6th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to triple its expend.

Heightened (9th) The target gains ammunition or charges equal to quadruple its expend.

You harmlessly place your unique magical sigil, which is about 1 square inch in size, on the targeted creature or object. The mark can be visible or invisible, and you can change it from one state to another by using an Interact action to touch the target. The mark can be scrubbed or scraped off with 5 minutes of work. If it's on a creature, it fades naturally over the course of a week. The time before the mark fades increases depending on your heightened rank.

Heightened (3rd) The sigil instead fades after 1 month.

Heightened (5th) The sigil instead fades after 1 year.

Heightened (7th) The sigil never fades.

Upgrades: 0

A mainstay of the Azlanti Star Empire, this sleek laser rifle includes a slot for aeon stones to enhance and focus the intensity of its beams.

Upgrades: 1

These durable gloves fit over a wielder's hands (or other appendages) and feature weighted plates or spikes over the knuckles.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}

These grenades discharge a shocking field that deals 3d8 electricity damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save. If you have access to the critical specialization effect of grenades, tech creatures and unattended tech items that critically fail their saving throw against electromag grenades become glitching.

Upgrades: 0

These decorative suits of medium armor feature exaggerated shoulder guards, stylized dragon wings, and draconic emblems across the breastplate's back and chest. Skyfire armor is often plated with gold or other attractive metals, sacrificing upgrade space for style.

This circuit safely courses electricity through your weapon. It deals an extra 1d6 electricity damage. On a critical hit, electricity arcs out to deal an equal amount of electricity damage to up to two other creatures of your choice within 10 feet of the target.

Staple items within the Azlanti Star Empire, these gemstones are popular status symbols for adventurers and wealthy collectors. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to build the Universe in ancient times.

When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head (or another visible part of your anatomy if you don't have a head) instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an aeon stone with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into specific items, such as weapons with the aeon trait or antique magical devices called wayfinders.

This deep-blue prism with cloudy inclusions is light as a feather. Its resonant power gives you a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks.

Activate—Fly Free 1 (concentrate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You gain a fly Speed for 1 minute equal to your Speed or 20 feet, whichever is greater.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment-effects.Item.xZ5XMY55S42aGyjg]{Effect: Aeon Stone (Uplifting)}
This visor wraps around your visual organs, enhancing your natural senses. You gain darkvision.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} on critical failure

Trigger The Sihedron Guard specialist is reduced to 0 Hit Points and has used their Reward of Obedience reaction in the last year. A Sihedron Guard must use this ability when the trigger is met

Effect The Sihedron Guard specialist is utterly destroyed, leaving behind only equipment. Their soul can't be restored, and no magical means can contact their soul.

Frequency once per year

Trigger The Sihedron Guard specialist is reduced to 0 Hit Points, but not from an effect that leaves no remains (like the promession spell)

Effect The Sihedron Guard specialist regains @Damage[5d8[healing]] Hit Points and doesn't fall @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} or drop their equipment.

The Sihedron Guard specialist makes a ranged Strike with their aeon rifle or a melee strike with their battleglove, then Casts a Spell requiring 2 actions or fewer against the same target. If the spell requires an attack roll, don't count the ranged or melee Strike toward the multiple attack penalty.
A Sihedron Guard specialist who makes a Strike while adjacent to another member of the Sihedron Guard gains a +4 circumstance bonus to damage rolls for that attack.

details.publicNotes: While all Aeon Guards swear an oath of allegiance to the Aeon Throne, Sihedron Guard specialists are conditioned from a young age to be utterly devoted to Lissala—and thus to high priest Ishmerai Ophir. Such is their devotion that, the first time they die each year, a brilliant display of green lightning forms the Sihedron symbol in the air above them, preventing their death and allowing them to continue to fight in Lissala's name. Whether this boon is granted by Ishmerai or a direct gift from Lissala is unknown, but no one dares to question a Sihedron Guard specialist's devotion or deadly skill.

description.value:
You reach into the void and call down a vertical column of dangerous cosmic material that rains destruction upon creatures in the area and shrouds the terrain around it in a swirl of stardust. This matter is made up of bits of burning stars and chunks of frozen comets, and it deals 5d12 cold damage and 5d12 fire damage to every creature in the area when you Cast the Spell. For the spell's duration, the cosmic material reduces visibility and mobility, making the area greater difficult terrain and making everything seen within or through the area @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

Heightened (10th) The cold and fire damage each increase by 2d12.

A globe of freezing darkness explodes in the area, dealing 8d10 cold damage to creatures in the area, plus an additional 8d4 void damage to living creatures. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

If the globe overlaps with an area of magical light or affects a creature affected by magical light, eclipse burst attempts to counteract the light effect.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} by the darkness for an unlimited duration.

Heightened (+1) The cold damage increases by 1d10 and the void damage against the living increases by 1d4.

A powerful globe of searing sunlight explodes in the area, dealing 8d10 fire damage to all creatures in the area, plus an additional @Damage[(@item.level+1)d10[vitality]] damage to undead creatures. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} permanently.

If the globe overlaps with an area of magical darkness, sunburst attempts to counteract the darkness effect.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d10, and the vitality damage against undead increases by 1d10.
You send the creature a mental message of 25 words or fewer, and it can respond immediately with its own message of 25 words or fewer.

The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. divine

description.value: 60 feet. All allied creatures in the aura gain a +5-foot status bonus to Speeds (if the dragon is photon-attuned), or all enemies take a –5-foot status penalty to Speeds (if the dragon is graviton-attuned).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

The cosmic dragon is always attuned to an underlying stellar force: either photons or gravitons. They can swap attunement as a free action once at the start of their turn in combat and is otherwise attuned to whichever force they choose at any time outside of combat. The dragon's melee Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage while they're photon-attuned and an additional @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage plus Knockdown while they're graviton-attuned. The damage of their cosmic blast Strike is likewise determined by their current attunement, as are the effects of some of their other abilities. The dragon can choose to exclude any creatures from any effect of the aura or action that uses the aura.

The dragon can always sense the distance and direction to the nearest Drift beacon and to the Starstone on Absalom Station.
The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.
The dragon recharges their Stellar Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

The dragon unleashes divine power like that from the core of a star or black hole, dealing @Damage[13d10[spirit]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50] (@Check[will|dc:41|basic|options:area-effect] save). The dragon can't use Stellar Breath for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon conjures a mote of stellar energy that shares their current attunement. They direct the mote to a square within 90 feet. While the mote is deployed, the dragon's attunement aura also emanates from that square, with the same emanation radius. The dragon can Sustain to move the mote up to 90 feet or to recall the mote from any distance. The mote is made of cosmic energy, doesn't occupy a space, and can't be targeted or destroyed by any effect.

The dragon imposes an effect on creatures within their attunement aura. The dragon can choose either option for each creature, regardless of their current attunement. The dragon can't use Stellar Pulse again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Stellar Pulse]]{1d4 rounds}.

  • Graviton Each creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:41|options:area-effect] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round.

  • Photon Each creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

details.publicNotes: These majestic dragons embody the stellar forces that underly and balance all that exists in the universe. Cosmic dragons are wise, social, and patient beings, accepting of all whom they come across; they see all creatures as equal components of the universe and are just as likely to be found discussing astrophysics in the company of scholars as they are to be found lamenting the twists and turns of fate in the company of the downtrodden. Most cosmic dragons can change their shape, though they usually only do so to make other creatures comfortable. They detest deception and always introduce themselves as dragons even while in another form.

description.value: 10 feet. At the beginning of their turn, while the biotech giant is in contact with the ground, they can extend fungal tendrils from their body, starting as a 10-foot aura and increasing or decreasing by an additional 5 feet per turn as the giant wishes at the start of their turn. Any creature entering or starting its turn in the aura takes @Damage[5d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the giant Steps or Strides, they end their current aura and leave behind fungal ground cover in those spaces, starting a new aura at the beginning of their next turn.

Any creature that starts its turn in an area of fungal ground cover must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:26|options:area-effect,damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[3d8[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage. This area is also difficult terrain for creatures other than biotech giants and fungus. Any amount of fire damage automatically destroys a square of fungal ground cover.

When the biotech giant Strikes a creature with their mycelial whip or spore cannon, they leave some of the fungus behind, which immediately attempts to graft itself onto the targeted creature. The affected creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28] save; on a failure, its implanted augmentations that count toward its implant limit cease to function for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
For eons, these giants traveled across the stars searching for a home. While exploring a potential new home in the Vast, they were overrun by a strange fungus that consumed everything they touched. The giants' size protected them from immediate annihilation, but most could only watch as the mycelium's blue-green tendrils covered their new home. In desperation, the giants devoted all their resources to science to find some way to eradicate the fungus. Instead, they learned how to adapt. The giants began living symbiotically with the fungus, grafting it onto their skin and incorporating it into their technology. Now, these giants explore the galaxy as scientists and preservers of myriad ecosystems. While they're most often found providing their scientific expertise to other species, a growing movement of daredevil explorers known as sporerunners travel some of the most dangerous locations in the galaxy on a quest to find a planet they can call home.

Giants are enormous humanoids of many varieties who live all across the galaxy.

description.value: A cyanoscum can sense the presence of water within 60 feet as an imprecise sense.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Regeneration]
A cyanoscum can Squeeze through spaces that aren't airtight at its Speed per round.
Any creature whose skin comes in direct contact with the cyanoscum, such as during an unarmed Strike, must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:13] save or take @Damage[1d4[poison]] damage.

details.publicNotes:
While these wood elementals bear the adorable appearance of a rodent, their main bodies are unfortunately composed of toxin-producing algae. Cyanoscums thrive in any warm, wet environment. Cyanoscum blooms are easy to spot; from a distance, they look like blue-green meadows floating atop still waters or clusters of fuzzy, teal moss clinging to plants and rocks. An explorer moved by this majestic sight might venture too close to the water's edge or reach out to touch the soft, resplendent moss—inviting contact with these beautiful but poisonous creatures.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

description.value:
You befuddle your target with strange impulses, causing it to act randomly. The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target babbles incoherently and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.

Critical Failure The target is Confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early.

Heightened (8th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

target.value: 1 creature or object with the tech trait

description.value: You erect protective wards that make the target difficult to detect via magic. Veil of privacy attempts to counteract all detection, revelation, and scrying effects used against the target or the target's gear throughout the duration, counting cantrips as 1st-rank spells for this purpose. Successfully counteracting a spell that targets an area or multiple targets negates the effects for only veil of privacy's target.

You create an illusory image of a Large or smaller creature. It generates the appropriate sounds, smells, and feels believable to the touch. If you and the image are ever farther than 500 feet apart, the spell ends.

The image can't speak, but you can use your actions to speak through the creature, with the spell disguising your voice as appropriate. You might need to attempt a Deception or Performance check to mimic the creature, as determined by the GM.
