When you Cast the Spell and each time you Sustain the spell, your wisp attempts to distract your target, which must succeed at a Will save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the start of your next turn. A creature who critically succeeds becomes immune to wisp ally for 1 hour.

Heightened (+1) You create one additional wisp, allowing you to choose a different target. Each wisp you create must have a different target, and all of them attempt to distract their targets at the same time when you cast or Sustain this spell.

The wyspiria can communicate through items and creatures with the tech trait, projecting sound from and speaking through the item or creature's voice, speakers, or other audio output. In addition, the excuba can use any item or creature with the tech trait within their digital telepathy aura as the point of origin for their spells and abilities when calculating range, distance, line of effect, and area of effect.

An wyspiria has an internal biomechanical Drift engine, allowing them to function as a Drift engine for a starship or vehicle they pilot as a 1-minute activity. An excuba can enter or exit the Drift themself as a 1-minute activity. When an excuba gets a critical failure on a Piloting check to Navigate or Plot a Course through the Drift, they get a failure instead.
The wyspiria senses creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

100 feet. A wyspiria can communicate telepathically with any item or creature with the tech trait within their aura as well as with other technologically telepathic creatures (such as those with shortwave and similar abilities). This ability also allows the spectra to remotely attempt Computers checks within their aura.

Frequency once per minute

Trigger The wyspiria's turn begins

Effect The wyspiria pulls potential energy from their future self to use in the present. The wyspiria becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} this turn and can use the extra action only for Fly, Stride, and Strike actions. At the end of the wyspiria's turn, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of their next turn.

When a wyspiria Grabs a creature with a tentacle Strike, they automatically plug one of their many cord-like appendages into it. The sudden connection feeds the creature a stream of information and fabricated sensory data, making them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} unless they succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] save. If the wyspiria moves, they bring the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} creature along with them. The wyspiria doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the creature grabbed; the creature remains grabbed as long as it's connected. The grabbed creature can attempt to [[/act escape dc=30]] as normal. The wyspiria can connect with up to 10 creatures simultaneously.

Requirements At least one creature is connected to the wyspiria's tentacles

Effect The wyspiria maintains their connection. All connected creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round.

The wyspiria emits a series of synthesized squeals. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] takes @Damage[6d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage and @Damage[6d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save) and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute on a failure (or 10 minutes on a critical failure). While a creature is deafened in this way, it also can't understand telepathic communication or any message conveyed by a tech device—for example, the target perceives text on comm units or other digital displays as scrambled text. The wyspiria can't use Piercing Signal again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Piercing Signal]]{1d4 rounds}.

languages.details: Arc network, Digital telepathy, Truespeech, Can't speak any language

details.publicNotes:
Whether they were created by the god Triune, the arc spectra Facilian, or by the Drift itself, wyspirias are a new evolution of spectra first encountered within the last few decades. Metaphysicians often explain wyspirias in contrast against iridias as spectra emissaries; where iridias travel the galaxy to share gifts of technology and information, wyspirias instead come to mortal worlds on a mission to collect and preserve mortal art and knowledge, storing it within themselves or bringing it back to the Drift for reasons as of yet unknown.

Wyspirias' glass-like bodies include a cascade of tentacle-like cords descending from their waists, which they use to plug themselves directly into both people and machines. Because their tentacles can resemble the outstretched roots of a small, floating tree, the librarian spectra are sometimes known as "trees of knowledge." They use their networks to transfer data and share emotional or sensory experiences between participants.

Though accounts of wyspirias appearing on mortal worlds are extremely rare, they've been known to sometimes involve themselves as mediators between warring civilizations. They work to ease tense negotiations at peace talks by connecting the opposing delegates to one another, enabling various parties to experience each other's perspectives. It's widely believed that spectra only dispatch wyspirias to intervene in mortal conflicts when a war could result in the loss of important information, prevent the discovery of future innovations, or interfere with the Drift itself.

In the Great Beyond, time is measured not in hundreds, thousands, or millions of years, but rather in eons—in iterations of reality itself. Only rising to prominence within the last three centuries alongside the advent and proliferation of Drift technology, spectra have thrived in a burgeoning niche of the otherwise stagnant zeitgeist of the Great Beyond.

Though their origins and objectives remain shrouded in mystery, encounters with spectra revolve around new technological discoveries, sharing the knowledge of faster-than-light travel, and protecting the Drift. No one knows if spectra existed before the Drift, but their connection to the hyperspace plane is indisputable. Prominent metaphysicians believe spectra were created as amalgams of raw quintessence pulled into the Drift, just as the Drift itself grows from pieces of the Multiverse. If this is true, spectra's interest in the proliferation of Drift technology takes a subtle sinister turn, as each new Drift engine serves to harvest the Great Beyond and all its denizens for the growth of the Drift and spectra themselves.

description.value:
With a word of power, you seize control of the target. It gains the minion trait. If you or an ally uses any hostile actions against the target, the spell ends.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.rVkUzqS4rpZp9LS3]{Spell Effect: Bind Undead}

target.value: 1 mindless undead creature with a level no greater than bind undead's spell rank

description.value:
You unleash a ray of entropy that rapidly and indiscriminately decays matter. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 void damage, or it takes 2d6 persistent acid damage and is glitching 1 if it has the tech trait. On a critical hit, double the damage, and if the creature has the tech trait, it's glitching 2 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1 die.

You rapidly destabilize and decay the atoms around you to create a burst of magically enhanced mild radiation. Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save against mild radiation sickness. This magical radiation uses your spell DC in place of the radiation's normal Fortitude DC, has an immediate onset, and the interval between each stage is 1 round. Technology doesn't gain the glitching condition from the radiation created by this spell.

Heightened (5th) The radiation increases to severe.

Heightened (7th) The radiation increases to extreme.

Heightened (9th) The radiation increases to incredible.

Your touch leeches the lifeblood out of a target to empower yourself. You deal 6d6 void damage to the target. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to half the void damage the target takes (after applying resistances and the like). You lose any remaining temporary Hit Points after 1 minute.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

cost.value: black onyx, see Creature Creation Rituals table

description.value:
You transform the target into an undead creature with a level up to that allowed in the Creature Creation Rituals table. There are many versions of this ritual, each specific to a particular type of undead (one ritual for all zombies, one for skeletons, one for ghouls, and so on), and the rituals that create rare undead are also rare. Some forms of undead, such as liches, form using their own unique methods and can't be created with a version of create undead.

Critical Success The target becomes an undead creature of the appropriate type. If it's at least 4 levels lower than you, you can make it a minion. This gives it the minion trait, meaning it can use 2 actions when you command it, and commanding it is a single action that has the auditory and concentrate traits. You can have a maximum of four minions under your control. If it's intelligent and doesn't become a minion, the undead is helpful to you for awakening it, though it's still a horrid and evil creature. If it's unintelligent and doesn't become a minion, you can give it one simple command. It pursues that goal single-mindedly, ignoring any of your subsequent commands.

Success As critical success, except an intelligent undead that doesn't become your minion is only friendly to you, and an unintelligent undead that doesn't become your minion leaves you alone unless you attack it. It marauds the local area rather than following your command.

Failure You fail to create the undead.

Critical Failure You create the undead, but its soul, tortured by your foul necromancy, is full of nothing but hatred for you. It attempts to destroy you.

You create a shroud of darkness that prevents light from penetrating or emanating within the area. Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a torch or lantern, do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical light of your darkness spell's rank or lower. Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. From outside, it appears as a globe of pure darkness.

Heightened (4th) Even creatures with darkvision (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the darkness as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.con2Hzt47JjpuUej]{Spell Effect: Resist Energy}

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

You fling a large glob of acid that immediately detonates, spraying nearby creatures. Creatures in the area take 1d8 acid damage with a basic Reflex save; on a critical failure, the creature also takes @Damage[(1+floor((@item.rank -1)/2))[persistent,acid]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a critical failure increases by 1.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

An arc of lightning leaps from one target to another. Each target takes 2d4 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

Tendrils of darkness curl out from your fingertips and race through the air. You deal 2d4 void damage and @Damage[(@item.level)[bleed]] damage to living creatures in the line. Each living creature in the line must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half the void damage and no persistent bleed damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double void damage and double persistent bleed damage.

Heightened (+1) The void damage increases by 2d4, and the persistent bleed damage increases by 1.

You channel void energy to harm the living or heal the undead. If the target is a living creature, you deal 1d8 void damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. If the target is a willing undead creature, you restore that amount of Hit Points. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing an undead creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse void energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the area.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

You focus on the target object, opening your mind to perceive magical auras. When the casting is complete, you know whether that item is magical. You or anyone you advise about the aura gains a +2 circumstance bonus to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.eReSHVEPCsdkSL4G]{Identify Magic} on the item. If the object is illusory, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your read aura spell.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

20 feet. Mindless undead that begin their turn in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22|options:area-effect] save; on a failure, the Corpse Fleet recruiter seizes control of that undead. It gains the minion trait.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The Corpse Fleet recruiter Sustains each spell they cast to create minions and Commands any number of their minions.

Frequency once per day

Requirements A formerly living creature that died within the last round is within 10 feet

Effect The Corpse Fleet recruiter immediately performs the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.c3b6LdLlQDPngNIb]{Create Undead} ritual, as if it were a spell with a casting time of 2 actions and the Corpse Fleet recruiter rolled a critical success on the ritual.

details.publicNotes:
Corpse Fleet recruiters are powerful necromancers capable of maintaining control of mindless undead, creating new undead, and turning their magic against the living.

The Corpse Fleet is an army made up of undead supremacists who refuse to bow to the will of the living. Founded by defectors from the Eoxian navy, the Corpse Fleet aims to shatter Pact Worlds unity and liberate Eox from the influence of the living and those bone sages who sided with them. Unyielding and utterly ruthless, the Corpse Fleet deals in death and destruction, feeding on the living and exploiting the dead.

description.value:
Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the security robot with an attack

Effect The robot overclocks its processing power to attempt to avoid an attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.
The robot sprays a torrent of water that functions as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.jfVCuOpzC6mUrf6f]{Hydraulic Push} (1st-rank, DC 16). The robot must refill its tanks after using this ability.

details.publicNotes:
On many scrappy starships and remote space stations, it's hard to afford dedicated security robots, especially if you can't manage to secure a license to own them in the first place. Most such operations rely on cheap maintenance bots to step up and repel intruders—they might not get the job done against serious threats, but they might be able to take out common vermin before they can cause too much damage to the starship's circuitry. After all, sometimes the best maintenance is preventive maintenance.

Security robots are ubiquitous throughout industrialized space and have major presences in both the Pact Worlds and Veskarium, employed and customized by corporations and private citizens alike.

While most soldier robot models are built to be as destructive as possible, and elimination robots are designed to kill, security robots usually have fail-safes programmed into their behavior to prevent them from causing any more violence than is necessary to perform their designated security function. This makes them especially useful in cities, where unintended destruction might harm bystanders or cause legal issues, and even on starships, where a stray laser blast might cause serious damage to vital starship systems.

description.value:
The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You instantly transport yourself and any items you're wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space within range you can see. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an extradimensional container—the spell is lost.

Heightened (5th) The range increases to 1 mile. You don't need to be able to see your destination, as long as you have been there in the past and know its relative location and distance from you. You are then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

A wave of magical light washes over the area. You choose the appearance of the light, such as colorful, heatless flames or sparkling motes. A creature affected by revealing light is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled}. If the creature was @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} instead. If the creature was already concealed for any other reason, it is no longer concealed.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The light affects the creature for 2 rounds.

Failure The light affects the creature for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The light affects the creature for 10 minutes.

You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

The next time you make an attack roll before the end of your turn, roll it twice and use the better result. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}. You are then temporarily immune to sure strike for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.fpGDAz2v5PG0zUSl]{Spell Effect: Sure Strike}

Trigger An enemy damages the archon's ally and both are within 15 feet of the archon

Effect The ally gains resistance 15 to all damage against the triggering damage, and the archon can make a Strike against the enemy. This Strike doesn't count toward the archon's multiple attack penalty, and their multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.IsNnfuzanT5teGt4]{Effect: Archon's Protection}

A single creature can wield an adjacent willing arqsheth as a free action as long as it has two free hands. Once wielded by another creature, an arqsheth can't be wielded by a different creature until the start of the arqsheth's next turn. While wielded as a weapon, an arqsheth counts as a martial ranged weapon that deals @Damage[2d10[fire]] damage; is in the laser group; has a range increment of 40 feet; and has the analog, holy, and tracking +2 traits. Strikes made with this weapon deal an extra @Damage[1d10[spirit]] damage. They don't require ammunition and have no Bulk, as they support their own weight.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.c3Y1xZ6IIxbsnPk1]{Effect: Armament of the Archons}

Frequency once per day

Requirements The arqsheth saw another creature teleport within the last round

Effect The arqsheth casts @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]{Translocate}, which is heightened to 5th rank and causes the arqsheth to arrive in an unoccupied space they choose within 30 feet of the creature they're pursuing. If the creature is too far away, the arqsheth arrives as close as possible.
An arqsheth can be the target of spells that can target a ranged weapon, such as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.SbBqa1HStMrV47fb]{Supercharge Weapon}, while wielded by another creature.

allSaves.value: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

details.publicNotes:
Believed to have once taken the shape of ballista-sized arbalests, arqsheths are living cannons who line the walls of Heaven to defend it against fiends. They often make themselves known to pure-hearted mortals who are either in the path of an imminent fiendish incursion or else struggling to vanquish a vile foe. They can be obsessively goal oriented and have been known to abandon a stalwart ally to pursue a fiend rather than delay the fight against their target.

Once an arqsheth destroys the object of their pursuit or determines that their wielder no longer needs their aid, they typically return to Heaven, but rare circumstances sometimes lead an arqsheth to remain in the Universe for an extended time. These arqsheths might find themselves embarking on long journeys with a loyal starship crew, standing guard over a seemingly abandoned planet, or taking up a post near a Drift beacon. They never reveal their motivation for remaining, and some suspect that arqsheths have a sort of sixth sense that warns of potential threats and leads them to take up such posts.

description.value:
Trigger The diatha takes acid, electricity, fire, piercing, slashing, or sonic damage

Requirements The diatha has 5 or fewer Hit Points

Effect The diatha dies, its body combusting in a flash of flame. All creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:5] take @Damage[1d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect] save).

If a diatha dies before it can detonate, its body rapidly decomposes into a slick of highly volatile hydrocarbons. Any effect that deals acid, electricity, or fire damage in an area that includes the square where the diatha died, or targets a creature in that square, causes a small explosion and consumes the slick.

All creatures in the square and in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] take @Damage[1d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect] save). The slick evaporates after 10 minutes if it isn't ignited.

Requirements The diatha has 5 or fewer Hit Points

Effect The diatha Strides twice and makes a melee Strike at the end of that movement. After the Strike, the diatha uses Detonate.

details.publicNotes:
Often called "boom bugs" or "exploding slugs," diathas are invertebrates that store nutrients as hydrocarbon chains rather than fat deposits. Threatened diathas secrete hormones that prime their energy stores to combust, detonating themselves to drive off or kill predators. Even if several diathas sacrifice themselves this way, their hive rarely suffers thanks to their rapid reproductive cycle.

Diathas are hardy scavengers that can convert almost any carbon-based molecules into energy. Originally, diathas consumed biowaste in underground warrens, but their appetite for hydrocarbon-based fuels and industrial runoff drew them into contact with modernized civilizations, where they quickly adapted to urban environments. Though some entrepreneurs try to use these creatures as an organic means of converting plastic waste into petrol products, most see diathas as vermin that do far more harm than good when allowed to propagate.

Diathas can easily stow away in the nooks and crannies of starships, and interstellar travelers often don't notice them until it's too late. Because they can digest almost any carbon-containing matter, diathas that colonize new worlds can adapt to a wide range of biomes and persist as a threat to native species for generations. Efforts to eradicate invasive diathas carry substantial risks to the exterminators and the environment they're trying to protect, as the explosive vermin use literal scorched earth tactics to protect their hives. To make matters worse, diathas are hermaphroditic and can grow new hives from a single organism if they have sufficient food sources to multiply. As a result, the Pact Worlds and Veskarium maintain strict quarantine measures for vessels suspected of carrying diatha infestations.

As diathas age, their stored internal gases allow them to become buoyant. These "elder" diathas instinctively take prominent roles as scouts and guards for other diathas. To create an elder diatha, apply the aerial and elite adjustments to a diatha, or increase its level using the guidelines from GM Core.

description.value:
You can ask questions of and receive answers from the spirits inhabiting a computer. These synthetic spirits manifest within computer code and often have personalities colored by the nature of the device, its condition, and its applications and personalized settings. A computer's perspective, perception, and knowledge give it a worldview different enough from a human's that it doesn't consider the same details important. Computers can mostly answer questions about creatures that interfaced with them in the past and what information is concealed within their files.

Heightened (6th) The duration is 8 hours.

Your touch installs a dangerous self-replicating virus that disrupts a technological creature's programming. The target must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure The target is glitching 1 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2}.

You touch one object and concentrate on a dataset contained within it, such as a story, message, file, infosphere site, audio recording, or video. You understand the gist of the dataset and can quickly locate the most relevant pages or sections to your search. If the dataset is written or otherwise communicated in a language you don't know, or you attempt to gain information from a computer you don't have access to (such as one with a password you don't know), the spell fails.

If you Cast this Spell as part of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.t5nBkpjroaq7QBGK]{Research} activity, you treat a critical failure as a failure instead.

target.value: one dataset contained within a touched object

description.value:
You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

target.value: 1 dataset or tech item with the tracking trait

description.value:
You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

You learn the approximate measurements (length, width, depth, height, and weight) of creatures and objects you can observe in the area. If an object or creature extends out of the area, you learn only the measurements within the area, but nothing about what you can't observe. For example, you can't determine the thickness or weight of a wall you can observe only one side of, or the depth of water that extends beyond the spell's range. Each time you Sustain the spell, you learn the exact measurements of one creature or object in the area, including imprecisions or anomalies that might indicate structural weakness. Magically altered targets give you information that reflects what you observe rather than accurate measurements.

Heightened (+1) Extend the range of measure by 100 feet.

You analyze the natural environment in the area within 1 mile, learning general information about the atmosphere, biomes, and gravity. You forecast the weather for the next hour, including the direction it will come from; learn which atmospheres, biomes, and gravity are in the area, as well as the direction and distance to those elements; and learn if anything in the area presents an environmental hazard, including cold or heat dangers (at what temperature range), radiation (at what severity), smoke effects, and underwater conditions. This is enough to detect large effects that span spaces over 30 feet in diameter only. You gain a general sense of where and how far away these dangers are.

Heightened (+1) The scanned area increases in radius by 1 mile.

100 feet

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Telepathy]

20 feet. Enemies in the aura take a –1 status penalty to AC and all saves against sonic effects or effects that alter written or stored data. A creature within this aura attempting to read a document must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or cannot read the document for the next [[/gmr 1d4+1]]{1d4+1 rounds}.

Typodaemons paradoxically despise having their errors pointed out. If a creature Interacts to correct the grammar or syntax of something the typodaemon has written or is writing, including with a spell such as implant data, the typodaemon becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}. While sickened this way, the daemon must spend an action to justify its actions instead of retching, attempting a @Check[will] save instead to remove the condition.

The typodaemon remotely accesses the code of a visible item with the tech trait within 60 feet and injects erroneous data. The item becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}; if the item is in possession of a creature, the creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18|options:inflicts:glitching] save, negating the effect on a success. The item is then immune to Error Injection for 1 hour.

details.publicNotes:
These child-sized daemons represent the horrors of death through dictation errors, transcription woes, or incorrect copying and pasting of text. Typodaemons make discreet adjustments to text, changing a word, adding a taunting emote, or adjusting punctuation to create entirely new meanings for written words. These changes lead to conflicts, and the typodaemon thrives on the strife and potential for death because of these simple errors.

Hailing from the plane of Abaddon, daemons are utterly devoted to the destruction of all life. Their existence is based around specific means of death, and they seek the death of all things in service to their lords: the Apocalypse Riders. Each daemon represents a different way to die, and they use their specific powers to further that means of death.

Advents in technology have only created new means of death and torment, so new types of daemons enter existence with regularity, each created once a sufficient mass of new deaths occurs to spawn them. These new advent daemons often prefer to spend their time in the galaxy rather than Abaddon, as they can better spread their torments to the mortals of creation.

description.value:
Trigger The irradiated undead plant is reduced to 0 Hit Points by energy damage of any kind

Effect The energy acts as a catalyst for a nuclear reaction, causing the plant to explode and dealing @Damage[3d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] with a @Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]
20 feet. A creature entering or starting its turn inside the aura is exposed to mild radiation.

When a irradiated undead plant Strikes a creature, some of its contaminated seeds stick to the target. The irradiated undead plant gains the Sprout action. An affected creature can remove this seedpod as an Interact action.

Requirements One or more creatures within 60 feet of the irradiated undead plant are currently host to at least one seedpod

Effect The irradiated undead plant pulses with energy, causing the seedpods to sprout vines. Creatures hosting a seedpod must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:16] save.

Critical Success No effect.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage, is slowed 2 for 1 round, and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until it [[/act escape dc=16]]{Escapes}. Until it Escapes, the creature is a new source of the irradiated undead plant's radioactive remains aura with a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5].

details.publicNotes:
Flora are often the first victims of industrialization, dying petal by petal and blade of grass at a time before anybody even notices a loss of habitat. When nuclear fallout corrupts the hardiest species of vegetation, sometimes their irradiated seeds inexplicably bloom into new, unliving specimens.

To survive in the unforgiving expanse of space, creatures must be naturally adapted or take protective measures. While the dead rarely need such precautions against these threats, sometimes radiation can animate a corpse (or several) to a state of undeath!

Scientists and religious scholars alike debate whether a specific type of radiation is to blame for this phenomenon that spontaneously triggers unlife, but all agree on the dangers such irradiated creatures pose. Many types of radiation could be the cause: intense bursts of cosmic radiation emitted by the stars, magically enhanced radioactive isotopes found in a planet's or moon's crust, or even the advent of new energy sources in a society (or its radioactive waste) contaminating the land. Regardless of their specific origin, irradiated undead all pose the same threat—they emit ionizing radiation that ravages everything around them.

description.value:
The target can understand all words regardless of language and also speak the languages of other creatures. When in a mixed group of creatures, each time the target speaks, it can choose a creature and speak in a language that creature understands, even if the target doesn't know what language that is.

Heightened (7th) The duration is 8 hours.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You delete data from a device or object with a flamboyant magical gesture. This data might be handwritten, printed, or digital. You delete up to 1,500 words' worth of text, one page of content, or one display screen worth of text or other visual information. Non-magical writing in or on an unattended or held object is automatically deleted. Attempt a counteract check to delete digital or magical writing. If you fail to delete writing that's part of a hazard, you trigger the hazard. This can't be used to delete the contents of a spellbook or similar means of storing magical formulas or spells.

If you target an attended tech item with the tracking trait, attempt a counteract check. On a success, the item becomes glitching 1. A creature holding the item can Interact to restart the software, reducing the glitching value by 1.

Heightened (7th) You can delete all the data stored locally on a single device or book, or all the data on a device or book related to a specific name, topic, or category.

You reprogram a computer or device to display a chosen piece of data when accessed. You can implant up to one piece of data per spell rank. A piece of data can be a simple fact, such as a creature or object's location or physical description; an image of a creature or object; or a simple statement of no more than 300 characters. When the target computer or device is accessed, this implanted data is the first data the accessing creature gains, regardless of what data the creature is looking for. Implanted data doesn't prevent a creature from accessing other information stored on a device, change how the device functions, or otherwise inhibit the device from functioning. If this spell's duration ends before a creature accesses the implanted data, the information vanishes.

Heightened (3rd) The duration is one year.

Heightened (6th) The duration is unlimited.

You cause the target comm unit to play a cosmically composed personal theme song specific to its owner. The creature who owns the target comm unit regains 1d8+4 Hit Points when you Cast the Spell, a +5-foot status bonus to all Speeds, and a +1 status bonus to saves against fear effects for the duration as long as the comm unit continues to play the ringtone.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.NmhqKZyMgQSHbyHj]{Spell Effect: Motivating Ringtone}

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing increases by 1d8+4.

system.requirements: You have a comm unit, used as a locus, and the contact info of the target.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This imposing pistol fires a jolt of paralyzing electricity from its barrel. Its remaining charges are visible as glowing lines of energy radiating down its body. Pulsecaster pistols are often used by bounty hunters and law enforcement who need to keep their targets alive.

A personal comm unit is a pocket-sized device that combines a minor portable computer and a cellular communications device, allowing instantaneous wireless communication with other comm units in both audio- and text-based formats at planetary range. A personal comm unit also includes a calculator, a flashlight (emits bright light in a 5-foot radius around you and dim light in the next 5 feet), a browser that accesses any local infospheres, and several entertainment options (including games).

Personal comm units integrated into armor have some voice-activated capabilities; you can communicate with other comm units in audio-based formats and receive responses without needing a free hand, but typing a message, browsing the infosphere, or using an app requires the use of at least one hand.

Comm units that function at longer ranges aren't as portable; they include built-in generators that provide the necessary amount of power and rely on active Drift beacons to communicate. Short-term use of these units can be purchased in most major settlements.

An online community moderated by an emvermod never experiences random bugs or downtime. Any checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Yn4jLPVWVE1vtAaF]{Access Infosphere}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.rmwa3OyhTZ2i2AHl]{Craft} virtual items, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.QyzlsLrqM0EEwd7j]{Earn Income}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.RF8xNJ8QsMwogerB]{Hack} using the community as a tool receive a +2 circumstance bonus. If the emvermod is unfriendly, such checks take a –2 circumstance penalty instead. An emvermod must spend a week in an online space to become a moderator.

An emvermod can sense items and creatures with active accounts on any community they moderate as an imprecise sense at the listed range. They recognize community members, even if they use an infosphere alias.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.AtWillSpells]

An emvermod is naturally invisible while holding a comm unit or in a virtual space. They don't appear to have an account in any infosphere space they moderate. They can become visible, or even selectively visible, allowing only specific people to see them.

Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect A creature within 60 feet with a comm unit or other computer connected to an infosphere must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save as the emvermod tries to ban it from communicating.

Critical Success No effect, and the creature is temporarily immune to Shadow Ban for 1 day.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature can't use auditory actions or communicate, even by gesturing, generating text, or using telepathy. This also prevents the target from casting spells due to the magical words involved in casting, with the exception of subtle spells. This effect lasts for 1 minute, or until the moderator fey retracts the Shadow Ban or dies, whichever comes first.

Critical Failure As failure, except this effect lasts 24 hours.

details.publicNotes:
A form of house spirit, the emvermod, or "moderator fey," is a shy resident of one or more online communities who helps keep the online space vibrant, engaged, and orderly. They often take on the appearance of their avatar, choosing one that fits with the other members of their infosphere niche.

When given proper respect, an emvermod is a diligent administrator who helps an online community thrive while working alongside other moderators in the online space. When disrespected or accused of causing harm to the community, they cause all sorts of mischief, such as deleting posts, shadow banning members, and even imitating other users.

description.value:
Trigger A creature takes a hostile action against the kyokor, produces a visible area effect, or flies within 120 feet of the kyokor

Effect The kyokor roars, producing a @Template[type:cone|distance:120] of sound in the direction of the triggering creature. Unattended objects of 5 or less Hardness immediately gain the broken condition, and concrete and metal surfaces warp into difficult terrain. Creatures in the area take @Damage[20d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:44|basic] save). On a failure, a creature also takes @Damage[3d6[persistent,mental]|options:area-damage] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

details.description:
The kyokor unleashes a terrible roar.

When a kyokor passes by or through a settlement—or even attacks!—it leaves a trail of destruction that can be represented by the following hazards.

Terrifying, skyscraper-sized beasts roam the wastelands of Daimalko. Called colossi by the population, these monsters erupted from deep within the earth to stomp cities into dust, decimating the population and destroying every surface civilization on the planet in a terrifying Awakening. Kyokors are among the most dreaded colossi. There's no hope of defeating one without a valko—a war machine created by Daimalko's stubborn survivors to fight the colossi.
details.reset: The kyokor must wait [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} before roaring again.

description.value: A crest-eater can use its scent as a precise sense at the listed range against creatures that have taken damage from crest-eater venom in the past hour.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Max Duration 4 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[acid]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[1d8[acid]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[1d10[acid]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}, and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
Crest-eaters ignore difficult terrain caused by rubble, sand, and uneven ground made of earth and stone.

details.publicNotes:
Crest-eaters are large four-legged beasts with traits of both reptiles and mammals. Originating from the distant world of Kasath, they've spread throughout the cosmos, accompanying numerous kasathas and even having a small enclave aboard the Idari worldship in the Pact Worlds. A crest-eater is a predator known for consuming the bones of their prey. They use a highly corrosive acidic venom that eats through minerals with ease. Their name comes from their history of tracking down kasathas and cracking open their prey's elongated skulls to feed on the "bony crests" within. As crest-eaters consume bones and other substances, they produce large mineral protrusions across their bodies. Crest-eaters are social creatures and often form prides to hunt together or join with other nearby species if they're accepted—it's not uncommon to find a starship crew or local raider gang with one or more crest-eaters they've adopted. Tamers who have hunted with crest-eaters for generations craft a ceremonial cestus from their comrade's body after a crest-eater passes, a tradition still practiced to this day despite the high value and demand for crest-eater bones.

Most crest-eaters can grow as long as 14 feet and about 6 feet tall (not including their noticeable mineral protrusions). Thanks to their mineral-laden composition, they often weigh in excess of 1,000 pounds, somewhat hampering their mobility in environments that can't support their girth. Young and mature crest-eaters tend to stay together in small prides, though some act as solitary hunters in environments where food sources are scarce. Especially large males frequently become increasingly aggressive as they age, and they're often driven away by the rest of the pride for both their hostility and gluttonous appetites. If these solitary beasts survive long enough, they can become unpredictable and dangerous threats that attempt to establish territory and find food by attacking desert settlements. Many kasathan epics feature one or more brave warriors defending their village against an ancient male crest-eater. Whether dispatched by local legends or eventually dying off in secret, these oversized crest-eaters can leave behind a valuable cache of minerals for enterprising adventurers.

special.label: to Track creatures afflicted by crest-eater venom

description.value: Once an animated firearm is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, it becomes permanently @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} until its Hit Points are fully restored (such as by being Repaired).

In the hands of its creator, an animated firearm is a commercial laser pistol. The creator can Interact to release the firearm into an adjacent space to act on its own, or to retrieve it from an adjacent space and use it as a weapon.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Hover]

A creature that takes damage from the animated firearm's stun gun must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:15] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).

On a critical hit, the target creature uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll. On a success, the target is immune to stun for 1 minute.

details.publicNotes:
Animated firearms make for great training aids but can just as easily defend goods and possessions from intruders or serve as hidden sentries.

Objects can be animated in multiple ways for a variety of functions. Since ancient times, weapons and armor have been given life as guardians and sentries through magic or ritual.

description.value:
You ignite a sudden spark of electricity that surges into a technological creature or object. You de-energize an item with the tech trait or disrupt a creature with the tech trait, with effects based on the target being a creature or object.

  • Creature If your target is a creature with the tech trait, it attempts a Will save. On a failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}; on a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.
  • Object If you target an attended object, the creature with the object attempts a Will save. On a success, the object loses half of its maximum charges. On a failure, or if you target an unattended object, the object loses all its remaining charges.

target.value: 1 creature or object with the tech trait

description.value:
You unleash a ray of entropy that rapidly and indiscriminately decays matter. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 void damage, or it takes 2d6 persistent acid damage and is glitching 1 if it has the tech trait. On a critical hit, double the damage, and if the creature has the tech trait, it's glitching 2 and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1 die.

An ephemeral, taloned hand grips the target, burning it with magical acid. The target takes 2d8 acid damage plus @Damage[floor(@item.level/2)d6[persistent,acid]] damage depending on its Reflex save. A creature taking persistent damage from this spell takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds. @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.6TGcfVyzzVHEo7ke]{Spell Effect: Acid Grip}

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and no persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice.

Failure The creature takes full damage and persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and full persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 20 feet in a direction of your choice.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

The target's form appears blurry. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}. As the nature of this effect still leaves the target's location obvious, the target can't use this concealment to Hide or Sneak.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You shout a command that's hard to ignore. You can command the target to approach you, run away (as if it had the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition), release what it's holding, drop @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}, or stand in place. It can't @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.A72nHGUtNXgY5Ey9]{Delay} or take any reactions until it has obeyed your command. The effects depend on the target's Will save.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure For the first action on its next turn, the creature must use a single action to do as you command.

Critical Failure The target must use all its actions on its next turn to obey your command.

Heightened (5th) You can target up to 10 creatures.

duration.value: until the end of the target's next turn

description.value:
You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You conjure grease, choosing an area or target.

  • Area [4 contiguous 5-foot squares] All solid ground in the area is covered with grease. Each creature standing on the greasy surface must succeed at a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check against your spell DC or fall @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. Creatures using an action to move onto the greasy surface during the spell's duration must attempt either a Reflex save or an Acrobatics check to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.M76ycLAqHoAgbcej]{Balance}. A creature that Steps or Crawls doesn't have to attempt a check or save.
  • Target [1 object of Bulk 1 or less] If you Cast the Spell on an unattended object, anyone trying to pick up the object must succeed at an Acrobatics check or Reflex save against your spell DC to do so. If you target an attended object, the creature that has the object must attempt an Acrobatics check or Reflex save. On a failure, the holder or wielder takes a –2 circumstance penalty to all checks that involve using the object; on a critical failure, the holder or wielder releases the item. The object lands in an adjacent square of the GM's choice. If you Cast this Spell on a worn object, the wearer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves against attempts to grapple them.

You vent heat toward a nearby target. You deal 2d6 fire damage to the target. On a failed save, the target's held equipment becomes hot to the touch for the next round. Unless the target drops its currently held equipment at the start of its turn, it takes @Damage[(3*@item.rank)[fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6. Increase the damage of touching affected objects by 3.

A spinning disk or smooth orb of shimmering steel leaps from one target to another. You deal 2d4 bludgeoning or slashing damage (with a basic Reflex save).

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4.

You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.feats.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

If you hit, you deal 2d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage—as appropriate for the object you hurled. This bracket-mounted grenade launcher is most often advertised for use with fragmentation grenades, but can efficiently deploy utility ordinances, such as smoke grenades and flashbangs. Unlike a dedicated grenade launcher, it takes 10 minutes to load an undermounted grenade launcher, reassembling the grenade into ammunition by mounting it on a specialized reusable firing cap. Once a grenade has been loaded, it can only be safely removed by disassembling the entire grenade, destroying it in the process.

These latticed gemstones are suspended in a silicon casing and encoded with the magical script necessary to cast a single, specific spell. Casting a spell from a spell gem requires holding the gem in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell. The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell must be of your spellcasting tradition. The spell in a spell gem can be cast only once, and the spell gem is destroyed as part of the casting. Spell gems can't contain cantrips or rituals. The traits for this item vary based on the spell it contains.

Activate Cast a Spell

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank.

You call down a tendril of lightning that cracks with thunder, dealing 1d12 electricity damage and 1d4 sonic damage to the target with a basic Reflex save. A target wearing metal armor or made of metal takes a –1 circumstance bonus to its save, and if damaged by the spell is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

undermounted frag grenade (@Template[type:burst|distance:10], consumable, range 30 feet, tech, tracking +1), Damage @Damage[2d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save; @Damage[1d4[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage on critical failure)

The aeon guard Flies using their jetpack, tracing arcane sigils through the air with their motion to empower their spells. If the next action the aeon guard uses is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet. In addition, if the spell requires an attack roll or a Reflex save, any targets of the spell that have lesser cover reduce the bonuses they gain from cover by 1 against the spell.

details.publicNotes: Sihedron Guard thunderstrikers, so named for one of their most common forms of attack, serve as air support for units of Aeon and Sihedron Guards. They bombard enemies with magic and explosives to force their targets out of defensive positions and into firing range for the rest of their squad. They also frequently execute extraction missions, whether getting an important asset to safety or capturing an enemy for interrogation.

The garaggakal polymath senses all creatures that are under emotion effects as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

The garaggakal's imprecise senses function through most materials and walls. The senses are blocked by energy fields (such as force fields and force effects) and sufficiently dense material: 10 feet of wood or plastic, 5 feet of stone, 1 foot of common metal, or 6 inches of lead or any starmetal.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NoBreath]

Requirements The garaggakal's last action damaged a living creature with a jaws Strike

Effect The garaggakal uses their extensive knowledge of anatomy to dig into the wound, causing agonizing pain to the target. The garaggakal attempts a jaws Strike against the required creature, using the same multiple attack penalty as the previous Strike. On a hit, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical hit). This counts as two Strikes for the garaggakal's multiple attack penalty.

A creature that takes damage from a garaggakal's spit Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).

Requirements A living creature within 30 feet of the garaggakal has the drained condition

Effect The garaggakal amplifies the drain on the required creature's essential life force, dealing @Damage[10d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:38|basic] save). The garaggakal gains temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt that last 1 minute. Regardless of the result of the save, the garaggakal then Flies up to 15 feet closer to the required creature, and the required creature becomes temporarily immune to Leech Life for 24 hours.

A garaggakal can shift out of phase with reality and thus can't be held by walls or cages. The garaggakal moves through up to 15 feet of solid matter. They must end their turn outside of the solid matter or take @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage. A garaggakal can't phase through force effects.

languages.details: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

details.publicNotes:
These garaggakals have lived for centuries and reached sizes greater than 15 feet tall and 1,000 pounds. Garaggakal polymaths have spent their many years tormenting and tearing apart all the creatures they've encountered, and they have extensive recollection of anatomy of a wide variety of living things. They're even crueler than younger garaggakals, taking great delight in causing excruciating pain to their victims before stealing their life force. They travel much further through the Drift than their younger counterparts, driven by their insatiable desire to find and dissect new creatures.

The Drift is a strange place, and the creatures native to it are stranger still. Garaggakals prove no exception. Shaped vaguely like humanoids, they have arms and legs but also sallow, rubbery skin with long, scaly tails and gauzelike wings. Most horrifyingly, their faces are almost entirely taken up by their enormous, funnel-like mouth, lined with sharp teeth. While they use these mouths for the obvious purpose of biting, they can also use its powerful suction to draw actual life force from their prey.

Also known as "Drift wraiths," garaggakals hunt down any living creatures that linger too long in the Drift. For garaggakals, anything that isn't a fellow garaggakal is prey. However, they're no mere mindless hunters. Garaggakals are surprisingly intelligent and prefer to dissect victims, learning all about them before eating them. Often, garaggakals take their victims apart while they're still alive. Xenobiologists have determined that garaggakals don't even technically need to eat to survive, so their relentless hunger is seemingly psychological. They do require the energy of the Drift to survive in the absence of food, however, and further need the energy of the Drift to reproduce, seeking out areas of intense radiation to absorb and then build a new offspring from their own cells. Their children are fully grown at creation.

Garaggakals surprise their prey. They are quite stealthy and can even phase through solid matter, suddenly appearing on the inside of a starship—bad news for whatever living creatures happen to be inside. Con artists sell garaggakal repelling devices for travelers to use while in the Drift to prevent such visits.

Freebooter armor is named for the pirates and outlaws who often wear these outfits. Freebooter armor typically consists of a breastplate worn under a jacket or flight suit, heavy boots and gloves, a helmet, and hidden straps and holsters for concealing weapons.

This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

The Free Captain telepathically orders their crew to mercilessly slaughter one target. All allied creatures of equal or lower level within 60 feet of the Free Captain gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls against one creature until the end of the Free Captain's next turn.
The stars of the Free Captain's cutlass seem to empower their starfall pistol, which deals an additional @Damage[2d6[void]] damage as long as the Free Captain is holding both weapons.

The Free Captain uses Area Fire with their starfall pistol split by their phase cutlass, causing the starry darkness to spread. The pistol's Area Fire uses a @Template[type:cone|distance:10] that deals @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage. Creatures that fail their save against the Area Fire become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

details.publicNotes:
Captain is the highest rank on board any vessel. They answer only to admirals of the Free Captains and only if their ship isn't engaged in active combat. This captain uses the void mode of her cutlass by default, the hundreds of shimmering stars in the blade representing the lives taken in her countless engagements.

The Free Captains have been a scourge of interstellar shipping across the Pact Worlds for generations, but since the adoption of Drift Lanes, the outlaws have expanded their reach and scale by targeting ships across the galaxy using the new standardized interstellar shipping routes.

description.value: The target's form appears blurry. It becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}. As the nature of this effect still leaves the target's location obvious, the target can't use this concealment to Hide or Sneak.

A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{Create a Diversion} with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check. If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{Spell Effect: Figment}

The hardlight scamp holds perfectly still so it appears as a normal hologram and becomes hidden. A hardlight scamp can Freeze even while being observed and counts as being @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

The hardlight scamp shoots sparks at a creature within 60 feet. The target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure).

details.publicNotes:
These creatures typically resemble the ad or media they were born from, though they often also resemble bat-like elemental scamps, thus earning their name.

Unlike normal holograms, hardlight life-forms are tangible, living entities.

description.value: This is a precise sense against creatures under an emotion effect or in the grips of overwhelming emotions (at the GM's discretion). Against other creatures, this is an imprecise sense. Creatures immune to emotion or mental effects or under the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.b515AZlB0sridKSq]{Calm} spell are undetectable by emotionsense.

Frequency once per day
