Now, scholars on Castrovel desperately search for additional records about elindrians, their origins, and—most importantly—the truth about their connection to the terrifying ooze-like creature that recently descended from their moon, Elindrae. One theory is that elindrians share some primal bond with the ooze-thing called the Terror of Ukulam, and perhaps both originate from Elindrae—other theories claim that elindrians created the ooze in the first place or that they're distant cousins to formians, corrupted by the Terror long ago.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

The fast-firing and continuous beams of this laser cannon converge on the target to create enough heat to melt a steel door into slag.

Upgrades: 1

Valued as both a crafting tool and an effective weapon, this hammer uses specially weighted heads and handles to deliver maximum force with each strike.

Upgrades 1

Activate 2 Area Fire

The rotating barrel of this automatic breechloading launcher holds up to six grenades of any variety with smart loading technology, allowing its wielder to choose a different grenade each time they fire. A grenade launcher is a martial ranged weapon, has a reload of 2, and a range 350 feet.

Activate 1 Area Fire

Critical @Damage[1d6[bleed]]

These fragmentary grenades explode in a cloud of shrapnel and deal 3d8 piercing damage in a @Template[burst|distance:10] with a @Check[reflex|against:class|basic] save.

Also known as "varman" in some parts of the Impossible Lands, this armor is made from fine metal cables woven into latticework patterns with plated segments to protect the head, neck, and shoulders. This armor disperses blows much like rings of chain mail but is much tighter in construction, making it quieter.

The Xenowarden biohulk Strides three times, then makes a melee Strike, dealing an additional @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage on a hit. If the target of this Strike is an object or construct, this Strike ignores 5 Hardness.
Whenever a Xenowarden biohulk makes a melee Strike that would deal bludgeoning damage, that Strike gains the razing trait.

The Xenowarden biohulk bellows with rage, causing plants to sprout from the ground and hinder the biohulk's enemies. Creatures within 30 feet of the biohulk take @Damage[6d8[slashing]] damage from the thrashing plants (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save), and the area becomes difficult terrain for 10 minutes. The Xenowarden biohulk ignores difficult terrain created by Nature's Fury. Then, the Xenowarden biohulk can attempt an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] each creature in the area.

details.publicNotes:
Biohulks are devoted Xenowardens who purposefully fused their body with plants and other flora to gain formidable strength. This transformation is usually accomplished using biotech, magic, or genetic experimentation, and can't be reversed. Regardless of their method, biohulks are incredibly strong and capable of battering down most artificial barriers with ease. Xenowarden biohulks are usually extremists, entirely and passionately devoted to the Xenowardens' cause.

The Xenowardens are a powerful and well-respected organization who aim to safeguard and study the natural world and ensure that technological advances are ecologically responsible. With an incredibly diverse membership, their ranks include, among others, hermits who practice nature magic and eschew technology and scientists who create renewable energy from algae. On the other end of the spectrum are elite combat units like the Wildknights and the Greenguards and feared ecoterrorists like the enigmatic Oleander. The Xenowardens are led by a Greenspeaker, who's elected by acclaim and advised by their peers.

description.value: The war machine can detect all creatures and objects within 100 feet of itself, treating @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}, and unobserved creatures as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Trigger An enemy Strides

Requirements The remote war machine is outside the target's reach

Effect The remote war machine Strides up to its Speed in the same direction as the target until the target stops moving or the war machine has moved its full Speed.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]
The superior war machine's ranged Strikes are launched with such high velocity that they can be used underwater without penalty.
The remote war machine makes a ranged Strike at a target as though its weapon had double its normal range. This Strike ignores all forms of concealment and cover.
The war machine Steps or Strides twice. The war machine gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed during these moves.

details.publicNotes:
The pinnacle of current robotic Pact Worlds military technology, the "aurovak" war machine was developed by a group of elven, formian, and lashunta engineers working together under a new united Castrovelian initiative.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value: Creatures that take mental damage are also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round on a critical failure
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Thoughtsense]

Trigger A non-mindless creature enters a space adjacent to a cerebinite

Requirements Initiative hasn't yet been rolled

Effect The cerebinite automatically notices the creature, makes a crystalline growth Strike against one creature adjacent to itself, then rolls initiative.

When the cerebinite dies, it explodes in a blast fueled by the memories stored in its crystalline body, affecting all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. Each creature in the area (including other cerebinites) must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:22|options:area-effect,inflicts:stunned] save or take @Damage[2d8[mental]|options:area-damage] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (or double damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 2} on a critical failure).

@Damage[(1d8)[bludgeoning],1d6[mental]], @Check[fortitude|dc:22|basic]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]

details.publicNotes:
The mineral cerebinium, also known as memory crystal, naturally appears in the broken region of space known as Kazmurg's Absurdity. Typically, these minerals develop in remote subterranean locations, where they grow based on the presence of thinking minds within a planetary range. The more active minds in a region, the faster these mineral formations grow. A major world of millions can easily create vast underground stores, and in these majestic caverns, cerebinites spontaneously emerge.

Cerebinites are essentially mindless creatures—save for the memories that power them—who have developed as an offshoot of cerebinium growth. Though possessing no intellect of their own, they act off the impulses provided by the memories stored within the crystals that constitute their forms. In many ways, a cerebinite is like the many oozes that lie in wait throughout the cosmos.

Encountering these active mineral deposits often requires a delicate approach, as cerebinites can engage up close or at a short range by hurling cerebinium spikes and blasts of cerebinium shards charged with memories drawn from their surroundings. Even in death, these masses of cerebinium explode in a destructive burst of psychic energy, affecting the minds of those nearby with jumbled visions of countless different lives. When multiple injured cerebinites gather near one another, this proximity can cause an especially destructive chain reaction due to cerebinites' own susceptibility to mental attacks.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This fierce but deceptively elegant polearm rips apart enemies with a motor-powered fangblade. An adjustable pulse trigger allows the wielder to channel extra pain through the weapon.

Upgrades: 1

This single-shot sniper rifle is one of the most popular weapons in the Vast thanks to its stability and reliability with minimal upkeep.

The vanguard-class soldier robot Leaps to a space it can see within 60 feet. All creatures within 5 feet of where the robot lands must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:33] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The robot then makes a painglaive Strike against one creature within reach. The robot can't use Assault Jump again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Assault Jump]]{1d4 rounds}.
The vanguard-class soldier robot shares senses with other allied vanguard-class soldier robots within 60 feet.

Frequency once per day

Effect The vanguard-class soldier robot fires a plasma warhead at an intersection within 200 feet it can see. All creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:15] take @Damage[8d12[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic] save). On a failure, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened} for 1 minute. The area in which the warhead exploded becomes difficult terrain.

The vanguard-class soldier robot can see through most materials and walls within 60 feet. Its sight is blocked by 2 feet of wood or plastic, 1 foot of stone, 2 inches of metal, or any amount of lead or starmetal, and it can see only in black and white.

details.publicNotes:
Vanguard-class soldier robots are hulking machines of battle. They often have four clawed hands, digitigrade legs, a long tail, and a featureless, diamond-shaped head. Their back-mounted plasma launcher and burst-duration jump jets give them a hunched, predatory appearance, augmented by top-of-the-line weapons. Each is 8 feet tall and weighs 700 pounds.

Though their striking image has captured media attention, the vanguard-class soldier robot is too expensive to mass-produce on the scale of an army, and so they are used as special forces, vanguards, bodyguards, and even field commanders for all-robotic forces. A lack of large-scale feasibility is, admittedly, poor comfort to any soul within seeker warhead range.

Living soldiers are a liability. They have families who don't want them to die, need salaries, have morale, require discipline, commit atrocities, and have opinions on the war. They're a necessary, but unfortunate, part of warfare for any society without an abiding love for necromancy.

Cutting-edge soldier robots promise to solve this problem. Soldier robots have no loved ones, opinions, self-preservation instinct, or tendency toward random atrocities. Many companies market their soldier robots as a humane solution to modern combat.

In practice, they're anything but.

Without lives on the line, soldier robots encourage their owners to engage more often and more violently. Standoffs turn into skirmishes, skirmishes into battles, and inevitably the innocent are caught up in the mix. They're particularly popular with corporations and non-governmental organizations that appreciate being able to throw a dozen soldier robots at any naysayer, public critic, or explorer they dislike while being able to deny true responsibility for what happens.

Though there's constant public fear of soldier robots going rogue, it has yet to prove a serious concern. If a soldier robot wants you dead, it's because someone doesn't mind if it kills you.

perception.details: linked senses 60 feet, x-ray vision (precise) 60 feet

description.value:
Trigger The kyokor (or other kaiju) applies any amount of force to the structure

Effect The building trembles. The floors of the building and the streets within 30 feet of the building become difficult terrain; creatures on this difficult terrain take a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, AC, and skill checks. The hazard rolls initiative.

stealth.details: (master) to notice cracks forming in the walls of the structure as the kyokor (or another kaiju) approaches.

details.description:
The kyokor (or other kaiju) grazes a structure, severely compromising the building's stability and possibly causing it to collapse.

When a kyokor passes by or through a settlement—or even attacks!—it leaves a trail of destruction that can be represented by the following hazards.

Terrifying, skyscraper-sized beasts roam the wastelands of Daimalko. Called colossi by the population, these monsters erupted from deep within the earth to stomp cities into dust, decimating the population and destroying every surface civilization on the planet in a terrifying Awakening. Kyokors are among the most dreaded colossi. There's no hope of defeating one without a valko—a war machine created by Daimalko's stubborn survivors to fight the colossi.
details.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:43] (master), @Check[civil-engineering-lore|dc:43] (master), or @Check[crafting|dc:43] (master) check to brace the structure to reduce the risk of collapse until the end of the creature's next turn. The DC of the flat check for this round (see Routine) is increased by 4 on a success, or by 8 on a critical success. Increasing the flat check DC to 21 or higher stabilizes the structure, ending this hazard.
details.reset: The building becomes susceptible to Demolish again [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} after it's stabilized.
details.routine: (1 action) The GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:9] to determine if the building collapses, increasing the DC as listed in Disable. On a success, dust and debris fall within the building and 30 feet around it, providing concealment and dealing @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/gmr 1d4 #Targets]] randomly chosen targets (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save); on a critical failure, a creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. On a critical success on the flat check, the effect is the same, but the debris deals @Damage[12d6[bludgeoning]] damage to [[/gmr 2d4 #Targets]] randomly chosen targets instead (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic] save); on a critical failure, a creature is knocked prone and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by rubble until freed ([[/act force-open dc=38]]; [[/act escape dc=35]]).

description.value:
You sense the presence of thinking creatures around you. You detect the presence or absence of thoughts from conscious creatures that have Intelligence modifiers of –3 or higher. You can choose to ignore the thoughts of creatures you're familiar with so they don't interfere with your detection of other thoughts. The spell can penetrate some barriers, but 3 feet of dirt or wood, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, a thin sheet of lead, or any force barrier blocks it. Creatures who are immune to mental effects are immune to detection by this spell.

Heightened (4th) You detect the number of thinking minds and whether or not the creatures are telepathic. Creatures with telepathy have louder thoughts than creatures who don't have telepathy and might be able to sense your intrusion, at the GM's discretion.

You magically broadcast grim news onto nearby devices, holo projectors, screens, and other displays (including comm units on armor). Creatures in the area who can see one or more displays must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the display.

Failure The creature is fascinated by the display and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (even if the fascination ends).

Critical Failure The creature is fascinated by the display, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (even if the fascination ends).

You share a mood by psychically projecting an intense emotion into a creature's mind. The target attempts a Will save. On a failure, the target experiences your choice of the following emotions, but if you pick anger or joy this spell gains the incapacitation trait.

  • Anger On its next turn, the target spends its first action to attack the nearest creature, even if it's an ally.
  • Joy The target can't take any hostile actions.
  • Fear The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.
  • Sorrow The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and bursts into tears.

Heightened (4th) The spell affects all creatures in a 20-foot burst. Each creature must be affected by the same emotion.

You focus on the target, gathering data as magical holograms display information. When the casting is complete, you learn biometric information about the target, including its fingerprints, medical conditions, and other basic physiological information. You or anyone you advise about the analysis gains a +1 status bonus to checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} the creature, to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds} on the creature, and to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} about it. If the target is illusory or under the effects of illusion magic, you detect this only if the effect's rank is lower than the rank of your analyze target spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.7r6mw8H87SUaj9Tq]{Spell Effect: Analyze Target}

Heightened (3rd) The status bonus increases to +2, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

Heightened (6th) The status bonus increases to +3, and you can target any number of creatures.

You transport yourself to either a celestial plane or a world in the Universe where worshippers of your patron can be found. You must be of no higher level than double angelic messenger's spell rank.

Critical Success As success, but if you've never visited that plane or world before, you appear right in front of the message's intended recipient.

Success You arrive on the target plane or world at the last location you visited on that plane or world, or to a random location within [[/r 10d10 #Miles Off]] miles of your message's intended recipient if you've never visited that plane or world before.

Failure You don't travel.

Critical Failure You accidentally travel to the wrong plane, possibly a dangerous plane.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be an angel)

description.value:
You channel vital energy to heal the living or damage the undead. If the target is a willing living creature, you restore 1d8 Hit Points. If the target is undead, you deal that amount of vitality damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing a living creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse vital energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the burst.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

You cause the target comm unit to play a cosmically composed personal theme song specific to its owner. The creature who owns the target comm unit regains 1d8+4 Hit Points when you Cast the Spell, a +5-foot status bonus to all Speeds, and a +1 status bonus to saves against fear effects for the duration as long as the comm unit continues to play the ringtone.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.NmhqKZyMgQSHbyHj]{Spell Effect: Motivating Ringtone}

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing increases by 1d8+4.

system.requirements: You have a comm unit, used as a locus, and the contact info of the target.

description.value: Applying a combination of vital energy and acupressure, you help the target regain its footing. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition if it has it. The target becomes immune to the off-guard condition until the start of your next turn, even if the condition would be circumstantially applied, such as by being flanked or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The target is then immune to reorient for 10 minutes.

You materialize a handheld @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.oRuWi8EhgBiS3cbh]{Musical Instrument (Handheld, Commercial)} in your grasp. The instrument is typical for its type, but it plays for only you. It vanishes when the spell ends. If you cast summon instrument again, any instrument you previously summoned disappears.

Heightened (3rd) The instrument is instead a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.TwnyjvE7ZX35lCrj]{Musical Instrument (Handheld, Tactical)}.

items.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Mental

description.value: 30 feet. Allies within the angel's aura gain a +1 status bonus to all saves against emotion and mental effects. Enemies in the area take a –1 status penalty to all saves against emotion and mental effects.

The emissar angel urges their allies to hustle for the cause. Until the beginning of the emissar's next turn, the emissar and their allies within 100 feet that can sense the angel gain a +5-foot status bonus to their Speeds.

The emissar commands an ally within 30 feet to ignore a harmful effect and keep fighting. The target gains 2d10 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute and suppresses one condition that's currently affecting it for the next [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}, chosen from the following list: @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied}. The target is then temporarily immune to Spin Doctor for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.ncmWr1EPq7nGErQf]{Effect: Spin Doctor}

allSaves.value: +1 status bonus vs. emotion and mental

details.publicNotes:
Emissars, or propaganda angels, are a divine embodiment of radical hope and the celestial answer to information warfare. Some emissars spring from the sainted souls of revolutionary leaders or celebrities who used their platforms to help people in life, having been transformed into a celestial in their afterlife. Others spontaneously form out of the condensed faith and desires of a movement or ideal.

Emissar angels take on the role of celestial spin doctors and political organizers, inspiring their allies to rally together and keep pushing toward a goal even when defeat seems inevitable. Emissars often support mortal causes that align with their own beliefs and goals, and while they always hope to change hearts with an inspiring speech or an act of radical kindness, emissars aren't afraid to fight for what they believe in—no matter the cost to themselves. As soon as a battle ends, their first priority is caring for their allies and saving any enemies who might be swayed to join the cause. While their allies usually appreciate their unshakable hope and upbeat attitude, their opponents resent what can be construed as the emissars' toxic positivity and comforting lies.

While many angels in modern times ascended from mortal souls across the cosmos, these celestial beings were among the first creatures to exist in all the cosmos. Warriors and messengers of the gods, they're utterly devoted to their cause, whether that be destroying evil or spreading a deity's message across the galaxy.

description.value: Dust worms communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Dust worms sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the dust worm.

Dust worms that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

Requirements The dust worm is in a sandy or junk-ridden environment

Effect The dust worm uses its magnetic field to kick up a swirling shroud of sand and metallic bits in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] around itself. This shroud moves with the worm and lasts for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds}. The shroud grants the worm resistance 6 to physical damage, and any creature that ends its turn within the area takes @Damage[2d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Medium, @Damage[(1d12+9)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 14

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

A creature hit by the dust worm's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 21 that deals at least 14 slashing damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Requirements The dust worm has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

details.publicNotes:
Diaspora worms mutated to navigate sandy or barren asteroid surfaces, and as a result, dust worms trade agility for bulk and can resist radiation. Their electromagnetic manipulation affects small bits of ferrous material, allowing them to telekinetically wield sand and small metallic shards as weapons or cloaks.

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

description.value: A ferrofluid ooze can feel magnetic fields, allowing it to detect large sources of magnetic metal (Bulk 1 or greater) and items with the tech trait within the listed range.

30 feet. A ferrofluid ooze is surrounded by a constant field that interferes with nearby equipment. Items with the tech trait inside the aura take a –1 circumstance penalty to all checks using them. Unattended metal objects in the aura of 1 Bulk or less are pulled adjacent to the ooze.

A ferrofluid ooze can attach itself to any creature its size or larger that has the tech trait or is wearing or wielding items with the tech trait. This is similar to Grab, but the ooze moves with that creature rather than holding it in place.

As long as the ooze is attached, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}, and the creature it's attached to must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] to Interact with items with the tech trait. On a failure, the item becomes attached to the ooze (treat as if being held by the ooze).

A ferrofluid ooze that takes fire damage has its magnetic field deactivated until the end of the ooze's next turn. It releases itself from any creature it was attached to, drops any held items, and can't use Magnetic Leap or Attach until its magnetic field returns.

The ferrofluid ooze instantly Attaches to a creature within its magnetic field that meets the requirements of that ability. A ferrofluid ooze can't use this ability again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Magnetic Leap]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
Since the earliest days of space travel, engineers have used ferromagnetic fluids to help with the construction, operation and maintenance of starships. It was only a matter of time before overeager spellcasters tinkered with this substance and inadvertently imbued a modicum of life into it, creating yet another strain of ooze to plague the cosmos. These strange oozes exert magnetic powers and have an instinctive desire to seek out sources of electromagnetism. Ferrofluid oozes are considered galactic nuisances, and many shipyards across the Pact Worlds have begun retaining the services of expert "ooze control" agents to ensure these fluids don't impede their day-to-day operations.

A common ferrofluid ooze appears as a spiked pool of dark metal liquid that shifts in erratic ways but always reacts to nearby magnetic sources. They're mostly found hiding in the darkest corners of starships and space stations.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

description.value:
You protect the target with a powerful, long-lasting energy barrier. The target gains resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, and void damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.4Lo2qb5PmavSsLNk]{Spell Effect: Energy Aegis}

Heightened (9th) The resistances increase to 10.

An infusion of vital energy grants a creature continuous healing. The target temporarily gains regeneration 15, which restores 15 Hit Points to it at the start of each of its turns. While it has regeneration, the target can't die from taking Hit Point damage and its @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying} condition can't increase to a value that would kill it (this stops most creatures' dying condition at dying 3), though if its @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} value becomes 4 or higher, it stays @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} until its wounds are treated. If the target takes acid or fire damage, its regeneration deactivates until after the end of its next turn.

Each time the creature regains Hit Points from regeneration, it also regrows one damaged or ruined organ (if any). During the spell's duration, the creature can also reattach severed body parts by spending an Interact action to hold the body part to the area it was severed from.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.dXq7z633ve4E0nlX]{Spell Effect: Regenerate}

Heightened (9th) The regeneration increases to 20.

Your finger pushes a tiny root into the target's body that begins to grow, burrowing through the nervous system as it feeds off the electric signals triggered by the agonizing pain of its intrusive spread. The target attempts a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes 2d8 mental damage the first time each round it performs any action other than an action that is purely mental, such as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1OagaWtBpVXExToo]{Recall Knowledge} (or other actions as determined by the GM).

Failure As success, except the target takes 4d8 mental damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for the duration of the spell.

Critical Failure As success, except the target takes 6d8 mental damage and is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for the duration of the spell.

A field of life energy fills the area, exuding warmth and rejuvenating those within. Each living creature that starts its turn in the area regains 1d8 Hit Points, and any undead creature that starts its turn in the area takes 1d8 vitality damage.

Heightened (8th) The healing and damage increase to 1d10.

Heightened (9th) The healing and damage increase to 1d12.

You manipulate the target's genetic code and accelerate the inevitable, pressuring the target to undergo carcinization and become a crab. A willing target fully transforms into a crab, as if it had cast animal form heightened to the same rank as this spell and using the following statistics.

  • Crab

    • Speed 25 feet, swim 15 feet
    • Melee 1 big claw, Damage 2d8 piercing
    • Melee 1 claw (agile), Damage 2d4 piercing.
    • Can breathe air and water.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.16Li37zRYKClxqsv]{Spell Effect: Carcinization}

Unwilling targets must instead attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target's body gains minor features of a crab, its appendages turning into claws and causing it to become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}.

Failure The target transforms into a partial crab-like being, becoming clumsy 1, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is transformed into a partial crab-like being and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

You conjure a poisonous fog. This functions as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.9XHmC2JgTUIQ1CCm]{Mist}, except the area moves 10 feet away from you each round. You deal 6d8 poison damage to each breathing creature that starts its turn in the spell's area. You can Dismiss the spell.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

You shape a wall of solid stone. You create a 1-inch-thick wall of stone up to 120 feet long, and 20 feet high. You can shape the wall's path, placing each 5 feet of the wall on the border between squares. The wall doesn't need to stand vertically, so you can use it to form a bridge or set of stairs, for example. You must conjure the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost.

Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the wall has AC 10, Hardness 14, and 50 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage. A destroyed section of the wall can be moved through, but the rubble created from it is difficult terrain.

Heightened (+2) The Hit Points of each section of the wall increase by 15.

You repel effects that would hinder a creature or slow its movement. While under this spell's effect, the target ignores effects that would give them a circumstance penalty to Speed. When they attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} an effect that has them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, they automatically succeed unless the effect is magical and of a higher rank than the unfettered movement spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.CdAyAiMGESvgNQtz]{Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement}

Using the weight of earth, you hamper a target's flight, with effects based on its Fortitude save. If the creature reaches the ground safely, it doesn't take falling damage.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target falls safely up to 120 feet.

Failure The target falls safely up to 120 feet. If it hits the ground, it can't Fly, levitate, or otherwise leave the ground for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target falls safely up to 120 feet. If it hits the ground, it can't Fly, levitate, or otherwise leave the ground for 1 minute.

Trigger A creature within range enters an environment that would be harmful to it.

A shimmering bubble envelops the target's body, allowing it to breathe normally in a vacuum or other unsafe environment and protecting it from severe temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (7th) You can target up to 5 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts for 8 hours and also protects against extreme temperatures and radiation.

Heightened (10th) You can target up to 10 additional creatures to gain protection against the environment the triggering creature entered. The spell lasts until your next daily preparations and also protects against incredible temperatures and radiation.

You create a barrier of gusting winds that hinders anything moving through it. The wall of swirling winds is 5 feet thick, 60 feet long, and 30 feet high. The wall stands vertically, but you can shape its path. Though the wall of wind distorts the air, it does not hamper sight. The wall has the following effects.

  • Ammunition from physical ranged attacks-such as arrows, bolts, sling bullets, and other objects of similar size-can't pass through the wall. Attacks with bigger ranged weapons, such as javelins, take a -2 circumstance penalty to their attack rolls if their paths pass through the wall. Massive ranged weapons and spell effects that don't create physical objects pass through the wall with no penalty.
  • The wall is difficult terrain to creatures attempting to move overland through it. Gases, including creatures in @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.V8wXOsoejQhe6CyG]{Vapor Form}, can't pass through the wall.
  • A creature that attempts to fly through the wall using a move action must attempt a Fortitude save.

Critical Success The creature can move through the wall normally this turn.

Success The flying creature can move through the wall this turn, but the wall is difficult terrain.

Failure The wall stops the movement of the flying creature, and any remaining movement from its current action is wasted.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature is pushed 10 feet away from the wall.

area.details: all squares in a 20-foot-radius burst that contain plants

description.value:
Plants and fungi burst out or quickly grow, entangling creatures. All surfaces in the area are difficult terrain. Each round that a creature starts its turn in the area, it must attempt a Reflex save. On a failure, it takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it leaves the area, and on a critical failure, it's also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round. Creatures can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} to remove these effects.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.rjM25qfw5BKj9h97]{Spell Effect: Entangling Flora}

You shield the target against dangerous temperatures. Choose severe cold or heat. The target is protected from the temperature you chose (but not extreme cold or heat).

Heightened (3rd) The target is protected from severe cold and severe heat.

Heightened (5th) The target is protected from severe cold, severe heat, extreme cold, and extreme heat.

You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals. The spell doesn't make them more friendly than normal. Cunning animals are likely to be terse and evasive, while less intelligent ones often make inane comments.

The targets can breathe underwater.

Heightened (3rd) The duration increases to 8 hours.

Heightened (4th) The duration increases to until your next daily preparations.

You reinforce the target's musculoskeletal system to bear more weight. The target can carry 3 more Bulk than normal before becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.D5mg6Tc7Jzrj6ro7]{Encumbered} and up to a maximum of 6 more Bulk.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.5yCL7InrJDHpaQjz]{Spell Effect: Ant Haul}

You fling a large glob of acid that immediately detonates, spraying nearby creatures. Creatures in the area take 1d8 acid damage with a basic Reflex save; on a critical failure, the creature also takes @Damage[(1+floor((@item.rank -1)/2))[persistent,acid]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a critical failure increases by 1.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You detect whether a creature is venomous or poisonous, or if an object is poison or has been poisoned. You do not ascertain whether the target is poisonous in multiple ways, nor do you learn the type or types of poison. Certain substances, like lead and alcohol, are poisons and so mask other poisons.

Heightened (2nd) You learn the number and types of poison.

You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

A burst of microgravity causes a tremor around the target, pulling it toward the ground. The target must attempt a Reflex save.

Failure The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 10 feet downward.

Critical Failure The target falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If the target was flying, swimming, or floating, it falls 20 feet downward.

Heightened (+1) If the target falls, it falls an additional 5 feet on a failure and 10 feet on a critical failure.

A vine appears from thin air, flicking from your hand and lashing itself to the target. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.TwtUIEyenrtAbeiX]{Spell Effect: Tangle Vine}

Critical Success The target gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} condition and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. It can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} against your spell DC to remove the penalty and the immobilized condition.

Success The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round. It can attempt to Escape against your spell DC to remove the penalty.

Failure The target is unaffected.

Heightened (2nd) The effects last for 2 rounds.

Heightened (4th) The effects last for 1 minute.

Trigger The dragon critically fails a saving throw

Effect Swarming insects interfere with the effect, sacrificing themselves to protect their host's body and mind. The dragon attempts a @Check[flat|dc:17]. On a success, the dragon fails the saving throw instead of critically failing.

90 feet. @Check[will|dc:37|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

items.name: +2 status to all saves vs. mental

description.value: A creature that takes damage from a host dragon's jaws Strike or is swallowed by the dragon is also bitten by hundreds of tiny insects; the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 round.

Trigger The dragon Grabs a creature

Effect The dragon uses Swallow Whole.
The host dragon is immune to any attack made by a swarm of insects or similar creature (at the GM's discretion).

Large, @Damage[(3d10+7)[bludgeoning]] plus excruciating bites, Rupture 26

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

The dragon disgorges a swarm of biting insects that deals @Damage[12d8[piercing],2d6[persistent,piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50] (@Check[reflex|dc:37|basic] save). They can't use Swarm Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Swarm Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

The dragon transforms into an insect swarm. They drop all held, worn, and carried items. While in this form, they can't use attack actions (except Swarming Bites and Swarm Breath) and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their individual insects to fit without having to Squeeze. They can use the same action to coalesce back into their normal form.

Requirements The dragon is a swarm

Effect Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[12d6[piercing]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:37|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 round.

details.publicNotes: Innumerable insects follow these lumbering, ravenous dragons wherever they roam, swarming around them and living in the countless orifices along the host dragon's body. The dragon is both a part of the swarm itself and a part of the swarm's ecosystem—the swarm feeds on their body, and the dragon feeds on the swarm in a grotesque, centuries-long cycle that somehow results in the host dragon growing larger and more powerful even if they feed on no other substance in their lifetime. Host dragons draw their power from the primal instincts of insects and, despite their intelligence, often behave like the insects they gather to them. Host dragons despise answering questions and violently defy all attempts others make to understand their nature.

description.value:
You shape a wall of crackling plasma that burns creatures passing through it. You create either a 5-foot-thick wall of plasma in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high, or a 5-foot-thick, 10-foot-radius ring of plasma with the same height. The wall stands vertically in either form; if you wish, the wall can be of a shorter length or height. Everything on each side of the wall is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from creatures on the opposite side. Any creature that crosses the wall or is occupying the wall's area at the start of its turn takes 4d6 fire damage and 2d6 persistent electricity damage.

Heightened (+1) The persistent electricity damage increases by 1d6.

You create a hovering shield made of fire. As long as the shield persists, its heat grants you cold resistance 5 and makes you immune to mild and severe environmental cold. You can Raise a Shield with the fire shield as a normal shield to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC. You can use the Shield Block reaction with the fire shield, which has Hardness 10, is immune to fire, and has 40 HP (with no Broken Threshold), and its Hardness is halved against effects that have the water trait. If you Shield Block a melee attack that is either an unarmed attack or made by an adjacent attacker, the attacker takes 2d6 fire damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.1RsScTvNdGD9zGWe]{Spell Effect: Fire Shield}

Heightened (+2) The cold resistance increases by 5, the HP increase by 10, and the fire damage increases by 1d6.

A roaring blast of fire detonates at a spot you designate, dealing 6d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage.

For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

Heightened (+1) The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2-action and 3-action versions.

To the target, your words are honey and your visage seems bathed in a dreamy haze. It must attempt a Will save, with a +4 circumstance bonus if you or your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you use hostile actions against the target, the spell ends. When the spell ends, the target doesn't necessarily realize it was charmed unless its friendship with you or the actions you convinced it to take clash with its expectations, meaning you could potentially convince the target to continue being your friend via mundane means.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and aware you tried to charm it.

Success The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of charm, unless it identifies the spell.

Failure The target's attitude becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v66R7FdOf11l94im]{Friendly} toward you. If it was Friendly, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v44P3WUcU1j0115l]{Helpful}. It can't use hostile actions against you.

Critical Failure The target's attitude becomes Helpful toward you, and it can't use hostile actions against you.

Heightened (4th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations.

Heightened (8th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

You create an illusion that causes the target to appear more charming and genteel to observers. The spell accentuates the target's appearance, clothing, voice, and mannerisms according to what an observer finds appealing. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

The target gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression} and lets the target add its level to Diplomacy checks if untrained. In addition, the target can attempt a Diplomacy check to Gather Information in only 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hOp5ybOzyJlECU6B]{Spell Effect: Glow Up}

Heightened (+2) The bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th rank.

You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.cVVZXNbV0nElVOPZ]{Spell Effect: Light}

Heightened (4th) The orb sheds light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light for the next 60 feet).

You vent heat toward a nearby target. You deal 2d6 fire damage to the target. On a failed save, the target's held equipment becomes hot to the touch for the next round. Unless the target drops its currently held equipment at the start of its turn, it takes @Damage[(3*@item.rank)[fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6. Increase the damage of touching affected objects by 3.

While on their bonded celestial body, a nymph can sense approaching starships within a day's travel from the celestial body. This doesn't give them any information about the ship, its occupants, or its exact position. The nymph can send a message to the ship as if using a personal comm unit.

30 feet. A heliad's eyes blaze like a sun. When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save. The heliad can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the concentrate trait.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the start of its next turn.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} until the start of its next turn.

A heliad is mystically bonded to a single protostar or small dwarf star. If they're away from their bonded star for more than 24 hours, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save each additional 24 hours or increase their drained value by 1. A heliad can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond themself to a new star.

Trigger The heliad dies

Effect The heliad's body explodes into brilliant, luminous flame. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] takes @Damage[6d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 minute.

details.publicNotes:
Denizens of stars throughout the galaxy, heliads tend to be sociable and friendly, but most have mercurial tempers befitting their fiery homes. Heliads reside among still-coalescing protostars or smaller dwarf stars. Though not mortal, they follow a "life cycle" mirroring that of their star, growing in power as their star burns hotter and matures, then gradually weakening as the star burns out.

Many heliads take a keen interest in their star's entire system, eagerly following news, gossip, and activities of its planets' residents, if any, or travelers passing through. They view the arrival of visitors with excitement and frequently broadcast messages offering hospitality to those who venture near their stars. To host such visitors, heliads often build lodgings within special protective bubbles for creatures who couldn't otherwise survive the environment.

Nymphs are beautiful fey who appear as humanoids with elven features. They generally take female forms, but nymphs of all genders and presentations exist. While many nymphs live on planets and act as protectors of natural ponds, forests, caves, and cliffsides, other nymphs—often called space nymphs—are known for their association with celestial bodies. Like their planet-bound kin, space nymphs guard their bonded territories from exploitation or harm.

As admirers of the arts, nymphs are especially friendly toward creatives, and offering to depict a nymph in story, picture, or song is a sure way to win their favor—unless the nymph doesn't like the final result and lashes out at the artist for insulting them with a mediocre product. Vidcasting and livestreaming are popular pastimes among more sociable nymphs, whether to show off their remote environments or find audiences for performances. Some nymphs entice movie crews to film in their territories, with two provisos: first, that the production not damage the environment, and second, that the nymph receive a prominent role in the film.

Unless their territories are threatened, most space nymphs are friendly or indifferent to mortals, but they have long memories. Anyone visiting a space nymph's territory would be wise to research that nymph's grudges, past encounters with mortals, or any recent events in the area that might color the nymph's perspective of those traveling near their home.

A mortal who demonstrates the right attitude and abilities, such as by protecting a stellar body from destruction or creating an especially beautiful work of art, might draw a space nymph's attention. From time to time, space nymphs will offer their services as mentors to solarians or mystics, though they seldom seek pupils on their own. Space nymphs rarely enter into more than one such arrangement at a time and typically only make commitments that promise to last decades or centuries, so they're extremely selective in their choice of mentee.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This reinforced rod is commonly made from metal alloys and designed to strike and parry incoming blows.

Upgrades: 1

This snub-nosed scattergun is commonly used to blast through doors and walls. Smaller than a reaction breacher, this scattergun is infamous for its mighty recoil.

This durable yet lightweight defensive plate comes in a variety of shapes and sizes, and it's often customized with LED displays that broadcast personal insignia or an organization's colors and symbols.

Upgrades: 1

Freebooter armor is named for the pirates and outlaws who often wear these outfits. Freebooter armor typically consists of a breastplate worn under a jacket or flight suit, heavy boots and gloves, a helmet, and hidden straps and holsters for concealing weapons.

You have four arms, which allows you to wield and hold up to four hands' worth of weapons and equipment. At any time, one pair of hands is designated as your active hands. You can change this designation from one pair of hands to another by taking the Switch Hands action, which is a single action. You can wield items only with your active hands.

Requirements You are wielding a shield.

You position your shield to protect yourself. When you have Raised a Shield, you gain its listed circumstance bonus to AC. Your shield remains raised until the start of your next turn.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ShieldBlock] In addition to the normal use of Shield Block, a Free Captain gunner can use Shield Block to prevent damage from an area attack and also prevent damage that would be dealt to an adjacent ally instead of themself.

A special payload gives the breaching gun the area (@Template[type:cone|distance:15]) and unwieldy traits until the end of the gunner's turn. The gunner makes an attack using Area Fire (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic] save) that uses 3 projectiles. Enemies in the affected area who fail their save against the attack become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed} until the start of the gunner's next turn.
The Free Captain gunner Switches Hands, then Raises a Shield or Reloads, in any order.
A creature damaged by the gunner's breaching gun can't reduce its frightened condition until the gunner's next turn.

details.publicNotes:
Leading the assault with an explosion of cannon fire, the gunner's role in a boarding party is to incite a rout while protecting their fellow crew by suppressing a ship's defenses.

The Free Captains have been a scourge of interstellar shipping across the Pact Worlds for generations, but since the adoption of Drift Lanes, the outlaws have expanded their reach and scale by targeting ships across the galaxy using the new standardized interstellar shipping routes.

description.value:
You call in a favor from one demon of level 2 or lower, two demons of level 0 or lower, or three demons of level –1 or lower.

Critical Success You conjure the demon or demons. They are eager to pursue the task, so they don't ask for a favor.

Success You conjure the demon or demons. They are not eager to pursue the task, so they require a favor in return.

Failure You don't conjure any demons.

Critical Failure The demon or demons are angry that you disturbed them. They appear before you, but they immediately attack you.

Heightened (+1) Increase the level of demon you call by 2 for each option.

primary.check: Religion (expert; you must be a demon)

description.value: A daipex's powerful arms can be fused into one or more cannons. These arm cannons also can't be Disarmed or Released. The daipex takes a –4 circumstance penalty to any action that requires fine motor control.

5 feet. The heat from a daipex's arm cannons burns the intestines fed into the grafted weapons as ammunition. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:23|options:area-effect,inflicts:sickened] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

A daipex is full of unceasing rage and despises it when they can't deal damage. When a daipex critically fails a Strike or when a creature critically succeeds at its save against Hail of Bullets, the daipex takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage.

The daipex fires a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] of bullets, dealing @Damage[6d10[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save) to creatures in the area. The daipex is pushed 10 feet in the opposite direction and can't use Hail of Bullets again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Hail of Bullets]]{1d4 rounds}.

Frequency once per round

Effect The daipex makes two arm cannon Strikes against a single target. The daipex's multiple attack penalty is applied normally. If both Strikes hit, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

A daipex's relatively small legs can't withstand their arm cannon's kickback. When a daipex makes an arm cannon Strike, they're pushed 5 feet in the opposite direction of the Strike's target. The daipex can take a –2 circumstance penalty to the Strike to avoid this forced movement.

details.publicNotes:
Daipexes are formed from souls of sinful mortals whose lives were consumed by rage and indiscriminate violence. Their hulking figures consist primarily of a pair of chain guns merged into the flesh of their large arms. By comparison, their legs are disproportionately small and incapable of bracing completely against the force of their attacks. Daipexes only use their legs when necessary and otherwise rely on their arms to move about to reduce the strain on their leg muscles.

Daipexes carry about the entrails of their prey, sometimes dangling loops of intestines from their necks or simply dragging it along the ground. They feed these organs into their arms and, by flexing their muscles there, condense the putrid flesh into ammunition that can then be fired directly from their bodies. While they often have enough ammunition to fire off their arm weapons for hours, in the rare instances that they run out of ammunition mid-combat, they either retrieve more from corpses on the battlefield or sacrifice their own flesh in the name of victory.

All mortal creatures have souls that are usually judged in the Boneyard after their demise. The souls of individuals who lived sinful lives—usually by hurting others or acting selfishly—are sent to the Outer Rifts, where they eventually coalesce into demons. These fiends care for nothing but the corruption of mortal life to ensure that the ranks of their fellow demons never wane.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

Frequency once per day

Requirements The tergaiator has a creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}

Effect The tergaiator launches themself and their victim high up in the air before crashing down. The creature takes @Damage[5d8[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save. On a failure, the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} in an unoccupied adjacent square of the tergaiator's choice; on a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. After using Meteoric Death Drop, reduce the tergaiator's size by one category, their reach by 5 feet, and their Strike damage by 2 for 1 day.

Frequency once per minute

Effect The tergaiator hurtles toward their foes, using the momentum to launch a spinning flurry of attacks with outstretched arms. They Stride and make a melee Strike against any number of creatures within reach at the end of their movement. Each Strike applies to the tergaiator's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after they make all of their attacks. After making these attacks, the tergaiator is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the start of their next turn.

details.publicNotes:
Amid space's asteroid belts and atop their rocky exteriors rest the homes of these massive, competition-loving elementals. Tergaiators augment their bodies with pieces of asteroid as they wrestle and jump around. They thrive off the nutrients within asteroids and fight to protect their homes from being excessively farmed by greedy mining ventures.

Elementals are energetic intrusions from the primal planes of existence. They take many forms, from mindless eddies in the wind or water to complex entities capable of wielding primal magic. The advent of space travel and the progress of technology have allowed even more living embodiments of the elements to manifest in the Universe.

Natural events like wildfires, solar storms, and floods can unleash hordes of unruly elementals on a planet or into a region of space. Sometimes, a gathering of powerful elementals causes a natural disaster on an epic scale, like an off-the-charts solar flare that fries a planet's power grid or a ceaseless storm swirling in a gas giant's atmosphere.

description.value:
You gain a vague glimpse of the future. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveal the GM's best guess among the following outcomes: good, bad, mixed (the results will be a mix of good and bad), and nothing (there won't be particularly good or bad results).

The GM rolls a secret @Check[flat|dc:6|showDC:all|traits:secret]. On a failure, the result is always "nothing." This makes it impossible to tell whether a "nothing" result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of augury during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, though, it's possible to get a different result.

You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

An orocoran's stomach fluids are laced with hallucinogens due to their consumption of the ichor-like birthing fluids expelled by Aucturn during the hatching of the Newborn.

A creature hit with the projectile vomit must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:24] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round ([[/r 1d4]] rounds on a critical failure).

Frequency once per round

Requirements A creature within reach of the orocoran's proboscis is taking persistent bleed damage

Effect The orocoran flicks out their proboscis and sups on the flowing blood of their enemy. The orocoran regains Hit Points equal to the target's persistent bleed value.

The orocoran spews vomit in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15], dealing @Damage[3d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[will|dc:24|basic] save). Creatures that fail their save are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round ([[/r 1d4]] rounds on a critical failure). This confusion is a mental effect.

languages.details: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 60 feet page 210

details.publicNotes:
Most orocorans latch onto the shards of Aucturn that were cast through space or scattered throughout the Gelid Edge, consuming what fragments of the Newborn's womb they can and attacking anyone who happens upon them in a wild feeding frenzy. Orocorans act with especially intense and unrelenting violence toward anyone trying to harvest or relocate aucturnite.

Native to the now-destroyed planet of Aucturn, orocorans are foul and desperate creatures, cast adrift in space as they yearn to consume once more the essence of the Newborn god or fragments of the womb it gestated within. They're irritable, violent, and utterly anguished.

While some orocorans have broken free of their addiction and regained their clarity of mind and sense of self, the orocorans presented here are those who still rely on Aucturn's eldritch fluids.

description.value:
Requirements A @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} living creature is within the supenga titan's reach

Effect The supenga titan absorbs the creature's blood with its spines. This requires an @Check[athletics|against:fortitude] check against the creature's Fortitude DC if the creature is grabbed but is otherwise automatic. The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}, and the supenga titan regains @Damage[21[healing]] Hit Points.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:36]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round)

Stage 2 –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (2 rounds)

Stage 3 –20-foot status penalty to all Speeds and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} (1 round)
The supenga titan makes up to four spine Strikes, each against a different target. The supenga titan's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it makes all the attacks.

Large, @Damage[(3d6+9)[bludgeoning],4d8[acid]] plus slowing enzyme, Rupture 35

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
These toxic titans can drain a living creature's blood in moments.

When rains grow sparse in this succulent's native habitat, it turns to another source for liquid: blood. The supenga has adapted to a world of scarcity and takes advantage of any chance to hunt using its sharp leaves and paralyzing toxin.

description.value:
100 feet

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Telepathy]
The garaggakal senses all creatures that are under emotion effects as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

The garaggakal's imprecise senses function through most materials and walls. The senses are blocked by energy fields (such as force fields and force effects) and sufficiently dense material: 10 feet of wood or plastic, 5 feet of stone, 1 foot of common metal, or 6 inches of lead or any starmetal.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NoBreath]

A creature that takes damage from a garaggakal's spit Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure).

Requirements A living creature within 30 feet of the garaggakal has the drained condition

Effect The garaggakal amplifies the drain on the required creature's essential life force, dealing @Damage[5d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save). The garaggakal gains temporary Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt that last 1 minute. Regardless of the result of the save, the garaggakal then Flies up to 15 feet closer to the required creature, and the required creature becomes temporarily immune to Leech Life for 24 hours.

A garaggakal can shift out of phase with reality and thus can't be held by walls or cages. The garaggakal moves through up to 15 feet of solid matter. They must end their turn outside of the solid matter or take @Damage[5d6[bludgeoning]] damage. A garaggakal can't phase through force effects.

languages.details: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

details.publicNotes:
Most garaggakals are around 8 feet tall, weighing 400 pounds, with a 20-foot wingspan. Always on the move, they might find a temporary lair on a broken starship or floating chunk of terrain but are soon off again, in search of a new creature to be their next meal. While one rarely encounters garaggakals in the Universe, it can happen, particularly if their temporary lair exits in the Drift.

The Drift is a strange place, and the creatures native to it are stranger still. Garaggakals prove no exception. Shaped vaguely like humanoids, they have arms and legs but also sallow, rubbery skin with long, scaly tails and gauzelike wings. Most horrifyingly, their faces are almost entirely taken up by their enormous, funnel-like mouth, lined with sharp teeth. While they use these mouths for the obvious purpose of biting, they can also use its powerful suction to draw actual life force from their prey.

Also known as "Drift wraiths," garaggakals hunt down any living creatures that linger too long in the Drift. For garaggakals, anything that isn't a fellow garaggakal is prey. However, they're no mere mindless hunters. Garaggakals are surprisingly intelligent and prefer to dissect victims, learning all about them before eating them. Often, garaggakals take their victims apart while they're still alive. Xenobiologists have determined that garaggakals don't even technically need to eat to survive, so their relentless hunger is seemingly psychological. They do require the energy of the Drift to survive in the absence of food, however, and further need the energy of the Drift to reproduce, seeking out areas of intense radiation to absorb and then build a new offspring from their own cells. Their children are fully grown at creation.

Garaggakals surprise their prey. They are quite stealthy and can even phase through solid matter, suddenly appearing on the inside of a starship—bad news for whatever living creatures happen to be inside. Con artists sell garaggakal repelling devices for travelers to use while in the Drift to prevent such visits.

description.value: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Trigger The war machine would be reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The war machine self-destructs, dealing @Damage[12d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60-foot radius}. For 1 minute after this ability is used, any creature that touches the war machine's remains is exposed to incredible radiation sickness.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

10 feet. The war machine collects and reflects thermal energy to mask its presence. Any creatures targeting the war machine from outside the aura treats it as if it were @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} unless they possess scent or a non-visual–based sense.
The war machine makes a tentacle Strike against up to four creatures in reach, rolling the attack and damage roll only once for all targets.

A creature touched by one of the war machine's tentacles must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30|options:inflicts:slowed] save, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} on a failure (or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). Each time an affected creature ends its turn, its slowed valued decreases by 1.

details.publicNotes:
Devised by engineers based on Kalo-Mahoi, the robotic war machine, code name "atlapak," was built for aquatic warfare. Just like its amphibious creators, the atlapak is equally proficient on land. Code name "ksarik" was first observed in a terrifying attack on a Diasporan mining outpost operated by Ulrikka Clanholdings. When unfurled for battle, the ksarik becomes a mass of spiked tentacles and glaring red sensors that can tear through platoons of troopers in minutes.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct.
