Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value: A khefak chews on an unattended object to feed, dealing @Damage[4[piercing]] damage every 10 minutes to the object. This damage ignores 10 points of Hardness. If a khefak doesn't spend at least 1 hour Gnawing Scrap per day, it begins to starve until it spends 10 minutes Gnawing Scrap.

10 feet. A thasteron khefak gives off an aura of mild radiation. A creature who starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save or take @Damage[1d6[poison]] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

details.publicNotes:
When khefaks consume thasteron, they take on a crystalline appearance and grow into territorial, ravenous threats.

Khefak Biogenetics bioengineered khefaks to consume scrap thasteron so the waste could be refined into starship fuel. These creatures eventually became a prime example of a solution becoming a problem of its own. After the discovery of Drift travel, Akiton's thasteron market plummeted, removing the need for mining infrastructure, including khefaks. Khefak Biogenetics destroyed the production facilities, thinking they'd completely eradicated their creations. Khefaks are hardy, however, and the few survivors left the facilities. Beyond thasteron, they subsisted on inorganic materials, and their rapid reproduction rate allowed them to quickly spread all over Akiton. Some stowed away on starships, allowing them to spread throughout the Pact Worlds.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This engraved club of solid bone is tempered using material from the void that can drain life energy.
A draiclipsed can detect infrared radiation (such as that given off by warm-blooded creatures) as an imprecise sense at the listed range.
15 feet. A draiclipsed absorbs light and is perpetually surrounded by shifting shadows and lengthening darkness. Light in the area decreases by one step (for example, bright light becomes dim light).
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The draiclipsed opens their mouth and sucks in a magical light, consuming it to fuel their own powers. The draiclipsed attempts a counteract check against one magical light effect within 60 feet ([[/r 1d20+15 #Counteract]]{+15} counteract modifier). If successful, the draiclipsed becomes ringed in diffuse light, becoming photonic for the next 24 hours. While a draiclipsed is photonic, they're @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.
Whenever a draiclipsed would deal void damage to an undead or other creature with void healing, that void damage becomes cold damage instead.

Requirements The draiclipsed is photonic

Effect The draiclipsed releases the light they've consumed in a blast of corrupted electromagnetic energy, dealing @Damage[3d8[electricity],3d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic|options:area-effect] save). The draiclipsed is no longer photonic.
If at least one other draiclipsed is within the draiclipsed's eclipse shroud, the draiclipsed's Strikes deal an additional 1d8 void damage.

details.publicNotes:
Draiclipsed are the undead heralds of the Insatiable Eclipse, an ancient and terrifying tzitzimitl warlord buried when the Blackmoon fell on Eox and recently awakened by the Newborn. The Insatiable Eclipse amasses its forces, including legions of draiclipsed, to retake Eox, a world he feels has fallen to decadence and frivolity, whose disgraceful leaders have made the blasphemous decision to ally with the living.

Draiclipsed are canny skeletal undead whose pitch-black bones absorb light, allowing them to cast deep shadows. They can also consume magical light, using the absorbed photons to obscure their form before unleashing it in a blast of electromagnetic energy. Utterly loyal to their creator and liege, draiclipsed are trained for open warfare and even more dangerous when fighting alongside one another.

description.value:
You channel your life force into a spear of energy. You lose 6 Hit Points or, if you're in a bond with a vitality network that has at least 12 Hit Points, you can reduce the vitality bond's Hit Points by 12 instead. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d6 spirit damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage dealt by 2d6 and the Hit Points lost by 2 (or by 4 if you spend Hit Points from a vitality network).

You cause flowering vines or other plant matter to grow from an unattended tech object. Plant matter explodes from the target object, filling all squares within a 20-foot burst. Whenever a creature starts its turn in the area, it takes 2d6 piercing damage (basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} for 1 round or until it Escapes against your spell DC. The growth covers the object, requiring a creature to succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] to physically access the object for the spell's duration.

target.value: 1 unattended object with the tech trait

description.value:
You vitalize and stimulate the target at a cellular level, giving it a temporary burst of energy that unfortunately takes an exhausting toll. The target gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} condition and can use the extra action to Stride or Strike. After that round ends, the target becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} until it can rest for 10 minutes.

Heightened (6th) You can target up to six creatures by using 2 actions to Cast this Spell.

target.value: Up to 6 willing living creatures that aren't fatigued or quickened
1 willing living creature that isn't fatigued or quickened

description.value:
You condense a beam of mind-assaulting eldritch energy from the dark spaces between the stars and fire it at a target. Make a ranged spell attack roll against the target's AC. If you hit, you deal 2d6 mental damage. On a critical success, you deal double damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d6.

You channel vital energy to heal the living or damage the undead. If the target is a willing living creature, you restore 1d8 Hit Points. If the target is undead, you deal that amount of vitality damage to it, and it gets a basic Fortitude save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters.

1 The spell has a range of touch.

2 (concentrate) The spell has a range of 30 feet. If you're healing a living creature, increase the Hit Points restored by 8.

3 (concentrate) You disperse vital energy in a @Template[emanation|distance:30]. This targets all living and undead creatures in the burst.

Heightened (+1) The amount of healing or damage increases by 1d8, and the extra healing for the 2-action version increases by 8.

Applying a combination of vital energy and acupressure, you help the target regain its footing. The target loses the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} condition if it has it. The target becomes immune to the off-guard condition until the start of your next turn, even if the condition would be circumstantially applied, such as by being flanked or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The target is then immune to reorient for 10 minutes.

100 feet. Presulea can sense the presence of any living creature inside their nourishing aura. This functions as an imprecise sense. Any spells with the healing or vitality trait are automatically heightened one rank (up to rank 4) if the caster is within the aura. Presulea treats a creature that casts such a spell as though they can always see it for 1 hour following the casting, ignoring the effects of any cover, concealment, invisibility, or similar effects.

Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature within their nourishing aura uses an effect with the healing trait

Effect Presulea siphons half the amount that would be healed (rounded down) to heal themself or an ally within their aura instead of the original target.

Presulea teleports to the location of any aucturnite fragment within a planetary range. Presulea automatically uses this ability as a free action when reduced to 0 Hit Points as long as a piece of aucturnite is within range, reforming their body and recovering completely over the next 24 hours.

details.publicNotes:
Presulea's mortal existence ended in self-sacrifice to feed the Newborn. Presulea was remade as a quivering mound of rampant growths and pumping organs that shimmer with vital energy.

Thirteen mortal Midwives attended the Newborn's birth and were transformed into eldritch beings by the nascent outer god's awakening. A Midwife always regenerates an identical new form so long as even a single speck of aucturnite—the name for the frozen chunks of planetcenta that once surrounded the Newborn—exists on this plane.

There are 13 types of Midwives, but their true numbers are unknown. Whether their names come from the mortal servants whose souls were the kindling for the Midwives' creation or have some other significance to the Newborn, also remains a mystery.

Each Midwife identifies themself by how they cared for the Newborn: if they swaddled, fed, carried, held, watched, warded, blessed, or performed other acts of nurturing for the outer god. The Midwives are powerful beings capable of transdimensional travel. They might be encountered in liminal spaces, such as the Drift or the Ghost Levels of Absalom Station, or they might be found among the clouds of aucturnite debris in the Gelid Edge. Their incomprehensible actions always advance the Newborn's inscrutable agenda—or their own.

The most well-known encounter the Pact Worlds have had with the Midwives took place in the immediate aftermath of the Newborn's birth. Amnieka, who swaddled the Newborn and has a hood of writhing tentacles around their visage, attacked Absalom Station directly, causing a massive power surge that threatened the lives of everyone on board. Chaos ensued, but a group of adventurers—who happened to be in the right elevator at the right time—found themselves in the station's Ghost Levels, where they thwarted Amnieka and prevented the Midwife from draining the station's reactors. The Midwives haven't acted so directly since that incident, but many suspect it's only a matter of time, and no one knows just how powerful the others might be.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FastHealing]

Trigger The moonflower has swallowed a creature

Effect The swallowed creature is wrapped in a tight cocoon and extruded from the moonflower's body into an adjacent square. The creature continues to be Swallowed Whole. It can't use Acrobatics to Escape a pod, but other creatures can attempt to Rupture the pod. The cocooned creature takes half damage from any damage dealt to the cocoon. Once the cocoon is Ruptured, it deflates and decays.

If a Small or larger creature dies within a Pod Prison, the pod transforms into an adult moonflower with full Hit Points after [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours} of growth. The newly formed moonflower has its own consciousness, but some aspect of its trunk or blossoms typically resemble the creature that died within. The dead creature's equipment remains inside the new moonflower and can be retrieved if the moonflower is slain.

The moonflower releases a pulse of bright light. Each non-moonflower creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:50] must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save. The moonflower can't use Light Pulse again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Light Pulse]]{1d4 rounds}.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Large, @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning],(2d6)[acid]], Rupture 21

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
These enormous plants have thick, knotted trunks festooned with fanged blossoms. A gaping mouth opens from the top of the trunk, capable of swallowing most creatures smaller than the moonflower. Moonflowers can move slowly and attack with their powerful roots, but they usually dig deeply into the surrounding ground and remain stationary to ambush prey.

Though moonflowers can't speak in the conventional sense, they communicate telepathically with other moonflowers. Those who manage to intercept a moonflower's telepathic communications are assaulted by visions of primeval jungles ruled over by titanic plant life. Whether this vision is a genetic memory or a dream of the future shared by all moonflowers is unknown.

Moonflowers first sprouted on a long-dead planet, but their proliferation throughout the galaxy has been perpetuated by both random dispersal and sinister intent. Moonflower groves thrive on Castrovel and other worlds with undisturbed wilderness.

description.value: A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Trigger The war machine would be reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The war machine self-destructs, dealing @Damage[8d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all creatures in a 60-foot radius. For 1 minute after this ability is used, any creature that touches the war machine's remains is exposed to incredible radiation sickness.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

10 feet. The war machine collects and reflects thermal energy to mask its presence. Any creatures targeting the war machine from outside the aura treats it as if it were @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} unless they possess scent or a non-visual–based sense.
The war machine Steps or Strides twice. The war machine gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed during these moves.

When a war machine makes an aurora beam Strike against a creature, it absorbs one of the target's memories and remotely transmits the information to its pilot. The target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:25|options:inflicts:stupefied] saving throw or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}.

details.publicNotes:
Strong and ferocious, code name "thermatrod" is designed to maximize carnage and destruction on a battlefield. In contrast, the "vracinea" is among the weakest of the unidentified biotech war machines observed in the Pact Worlds. Sleek and stealthy, it darts in and out of enemy defenses while transmitting data to its pilot before departing. If discovered, the vracinea consumes itself to destroy records of the data it was carrying.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value: A koruvi endling can sense the presence of creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense at the listed range.

When an endling is destroyed, it reforms after [[/gmr 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the area where it first emerged, fully healed. An endling can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting, such as an act of decolonization, ecological restoration, or curing the disease responsible for its extinction.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

details.publicNotes:
Koruvis were diminutive ungulates inhabiting Calen-5, a wet arboreal world. When a Veskarium mining company discovered rare starmetals on Calen-5, a mining boom destroyed the planet's equatorial rainforests and drove koruvis to extinction. Years after, settlers reported mysterious property damage, along with sightings of ghostly beasts that furiously dismantle any technology they encounter.

Every species follows a natural arc as it emerges and evolves: rising, thriving, declining, and falling into extinction. When extremely unnatural ends to these paths occur—when unexpected predation, industrialization, or alien pathogens wipe out a population—endlings are born. These spectral members of extinct species typically develop as "answers" to their eradication. Xenobiologists differ on what creates endlings, unable to confirm whether the creatures are a species' last collective sorrow made manifest or just vengeful individual spirits—the last lights to go out.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This rifle's oversized body contains a larger version of an arc pistol capacitor, with a short barrel and polycarbon stock designed to insulate the wielder from high voltage. The arc rifle's larger capacitor allows it to produce a charge with enough power to subdue a rampaging renkroda.

Upgrades: 1

This long-barreled weapon shoots charges of ionized gas that explode in a blast of superheated plasma.
An assembly ooze can detect creatures and objects with the tech trait as a precise sense within the listed range.

A scavenger slime builds a shell for itself from discarded pieces of technology and a secretion of natural resin. This grants the ooze Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to its Hardness but temporarily removes its immunity to critical hits. Once the scavenger slime is reduced to half its Hit Points or immediately after being damaged by a critical hit, the scavenger shell breaks, removing its Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 22.
Scavenger slimes can repair any item with the tech trait without tools.

A scavenger slime can incorporate up to three weapons into its protoplasmic body, gaining proficiency with each. These weapons are treated as integrated weapons and are powered by the scavenger slime's own technomagical nature without needing to reload.

details.publicNotes:
For longer than anyone can remember, scavenger slimes have been found all over the galaxy. They hitch rides on starships, drift among wrecks and meteors, and lay hidden in junkyards. Their only goal: to repair everything they can get their protoplasm on. Scavenger slimes are believed to be an accidental marriage of technology and magic, built by an extremely advanced civilization as an all-purpose repair gel, only to develop rudimentary sentience and outlive their creators.

These creatures have an instinctive drive to repair and rebuild nearly any technological item they encounter. Using their extremely malleable bodies, which excrete a highly specialized resin, scavenger slimes can repair even exceedingly complex pieces of technology. They intuitively understand how to shield themselves from harm and how to operate any weapon they manage to find, aggressively using such armaments to drive any and all creatures away from their never-ending repair work.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

description.value:
Hundreds of wirelike nerve endings erupt from the corzador's head in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Each non-corzadai in the area must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:18|options:area-effect] save or be instantly observed by all corzadais within 100 feet. Corzadais in the area gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls against the detected creatures as long as the targets remain in the emanation. The corzador's emanation remains in effect until it takes a move action to leave its current space.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Bw1u0YIbG0ZV1CIR]{Effect: Follicle Germination}

Trigger A non-corzadai creature moves within the corzador's active Follicle Germination

Effect The corzador emits a high-pitched shriek. All non-corzadai creatures within the corzador's Follicle Emanation take @Damage[2d8[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic|options:area-effect] save.

details.publicNotes:
Vaguely humanoid corzadors herd corzad packs as they carry out their grim tasks.

Corzadais spawned from the broken remains of Aucturn. Explosively launched into space, these fungal colonies are held together by mysterious forces. Their strange fruiting bodies have taken on numerous shapes, of which two have been more commonly seen in the Pact Worlds than others: corzads and corzadors.

description.value:
You protect the target with a powerful, long-lasting energy barrier. The target gains resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, and void damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.4Lo2qb5PmavSsLNk]{Spell Effect: Energy Aegis}

Heightened (9th) The resistances increase to 10.

A powerful globe of searing sunlight explodes in the area, dealing 8d10 fire damage to all creatures in the area, plus an additional @Damage[(@item.level+1)d10[vitality]] damage to undead creatures. Each creature in the area must attempt a Reflex save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} permanently.

If the globe overlaps with an area of magical darkness, sunburst attempts to counteract the darkness effect.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d10, and the vitality damage against undead increases by 1d10.

A field of life energy fills the area, exuding warmth and rejuvenating those within. Each living creature that starts its turn in the area regains 1d8 Hit Points, and any undead creature that starts its turn in the area takes 1d8 vitality damage.

Heightened (8th) The healing and damage increase to 1d10.

Heightened (9th) The healing and damage increase to 1d12.

You shape a wall of crackling plasma that burns creatures passing through it. You create either a 5-foot-thick wall of plasma in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high, or a 5-foot-thick, 10-foot-radius ring of plasma with the same height. The wall stands vertically in either form; if you wish, the wall can be of a shorter length or height. Everything on each side of the wall is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from creatures on the opposite side. Any creature that crosses the wall or is occupying the wall's area at the start of its turn takes 4d6 fire damage and 2d6 persistent electricity damage.

Heightened (+1) The persistent electricity damage increases by 1d6.

You call upon the power of the planes to transform into a Medium elemental battle form. When you Cast this Spell, choose a listed element. While in this form, you gain the corresponding trait and the elemental trait. You have hands in this battle form and can take manipulate actions. You can Dismiss the spell.

You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which battle form you choose:

  • AC = 19 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 10 temporary Hit Points.
  • Darkvision.
  • One or more unarmed melee attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +18, and your damage bonus is +9. These are Dexterity based (air, fire, or metal) or Strength based (earth, water, or wood). If your corresponding unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • Acrobatics (air, fire, or metal) or Athletics (earth, water, or wood) modifier of +20; ignore this change if your own modifier is higher.

You also gain specific abilities based on the type of elemental you choose:

  • Air
    • fly 80 feet, movement doesn't trigger reactions;
    • Melee 1 gust, Damage 1d4 bludgeoning.
  • Earth
    • Speed 20 feet, burrow 20 feet;
    • Melee 1 boulder, Damage 2d10 bludgeoning.
  • Fire
    • Speed 50 feet; fire resistance 10, weakness 5 to cold and 5 to water;
    • Melee 1 tendril, Damage 1d8 fire plus 1d4 persistent fire.
  • Metal
    • Speed 40 feet, fly 20 feet;
    • Melee 1 blade (versatile piercing), Damage 1d8 slashing plus 1d4 electricity.
  • Water
    • Speed 20 feet, swim 60 feet; fire resistance 5;
    • Melee 1 wave, Damage 1d12 bludgeoning, and you can spend an action immediately after a hit to push the target 5 feet with the effects of a successful @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove}.
  • Wood
    • Speed 20 feet, climb 30 feet;
    • Melee 1 branch, Damage 2d10 bludgeoning.

Heightened (6th) Your battle form is Large and your attacks have 10-foot reach. You must have space to expand or the spell is lost. You instead gain AC = 22 + your level, 15 temporary HP, an attack modifier of +23, a damage bonus of +13, and Acrobatics or Athletics +23.

Heightened (7th) Your battle form is Huge and your attacks have 15-foot reach. You must have space to expand or the spell is lost. You instead gain AC = 22 + your level, 20 temporary HP, an attack modifier of +25, a damage bonus of +11, double the number of damage dice (including persistent damage), and Acrobatics or Athletics +25.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.Qp0dlhJaCzXIx73r]{Spell Effect: Elemental Form}

You conjure a temporary object from magical energy. It must consist of earthen or plant-derived matter (such as wood, paper, brick, or stone) and be 5 cubic feet or smaller. It can't rely on intricate artistry or complex moving parts, never fulfills a cost or the like, and can't be made of precious materials or materials with a rarity of uncommon or higher. It is obviously temporarily conjured, and thus can't be sold or passed off as a genuine item. The spell gains the appropriate trait for the item created, typically earth, plant, or wood.

Heightened (5th) The item is metal and can include common minerals, like feldspar or quartz. The spell gains the metal trait if used to create a metal object

You create a hovering shield made of fire. As long as the shield persists, its heat grants you cold resistance 5 and makes you immune to mild and severe environmental cold. You can Raise a Shield with the fire shield as a normal shield to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC. You can use the Shield Block reaction with the fire shield, which has Hardness 10, is immune to fire, and has 40 HP (with no Broken Threshold), and its Hardness is halved against effects that have the water trait. If you Shield Block a melee attack that is either an unarmed attack or made by an adjacent attacker, the attacker takes 2d6 fire damage.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.1RsScTvNdGD9zGWe]{Spell Effect: Fire Shield}

Heightened (+2) The cold resistance increases by 5, the HP increase by 10, and the fire damage increases by 1d6.

You raise a blazing wall that burns creatures passing through it. You create either a 5-foot-thick wall of flame in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high, or a 5-foot-thick, 10-foot-radius ring of flame with the same height. The wall stands vertically in either form; if you wish, the wall can be of a shorter length or height. Everything on each side of the wall is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from creatures on the opposite side. Any creature that crosses the wall or is occupying the wall's area at the start of its turn takes 4d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d6.

You raise a blazing wall that burns creatures passing through it. You create either a 5-foot-thick wall of flame in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high, or a 5-foot-thick, 10-foot-radius ring of flame with the same height. The wall stands vertically in either form; if you wish, the wall can be of a shorter length or height. Everything on each side of the wall is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from creatures on the opposite side. Any creature that crosses the wall or is occupying the wall's area at the start of its turn takes 4d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The fire damage increases by 1d6.

You supercharge the targets' electromagnetism and pull them with a tether of lightning. Make a ranged spell attack against each target. If you attack two targets, both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after both attacks. The targets each take 4d8 electricity damage and are pulled 5 feet closer to you, or 10 feet closer on a critical hit. If both attacks hit, you can choose to move the creatures closer to one another instead of closer to you. Moving a target into a barrier, creature, or obstacle causes the target to stop before entering that space.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d8.

Your words fascinate your targets. You speak or sing without interruption throughout the casting and duration. Targets who notice your speech or song might give their undivided attention; each target must attempt a Will save. The GM might grant a circumstance bonus (to a maximum of +4) if the target is of an opposing religion, ancestry, or political leaning, or is otherwise unlikely to agree with what you're saying.

Each creature that comes within range has to attempt a save when you Sustain the spell. If you're speaking, enthrall gains the linguistic trait.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and notices that you tried to use magic.

Success The target needn't pay attention but doesn't notice you tried to use magic (it might notice others are enthralled).

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} with you. It can attempt another Will save if it witnesses actions or speech with which it disagrees. If it succeeds, it's no longer fascinated and is temporarily immune for 1 hour. If the target is subject to a hostile act, or if another creature succeeds at a Diplomacy or Intimidation check against it, the fascination ends immediately.

Critical Failure As failure, but the target can't attempt a save to end the fascination if it disagrees with you.

A roaring blast of fire detonates at a spot you designate, dealing 6d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

A roaring blast of fire detonates at a spot you designate, dealing 6d6 fire damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

You rapidly destabilize and decay the atoms around you to create a burst of magically enhanced mild radiation. Creatures in the area must attempt a Fortitude save against mild radiation sickness. This magical radiation uses your spell DC in place of the radiation's normal Fortitude DC, has an immediate onset, and the interval between each stage is 1 round. Technology doesn't gain the glitching condition from the radiation created by this spell.

Heightened (5th) The radiation increases to severe.

Heightened (7th) The radiation increases to extreme.

Heightened (9th) The radiation increases to incredible.

You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage.

For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

Heightened (+1) The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2-action and 3-action versions.

The gemstone you touch glows, spreading bright light with a color of your choice in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet). The spell ends immediately if the gemstone is broken.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hpbCDbDOoVyhOmck]{Spell Effect: Everlight}

description.value:
You channel your life force into a spear of energy. You lose 6 Hit Points or, if you're in a bond with a vitality network that has at least 12 Hit Points, you can reduce the vitality bond's Hit Points by 12 instead. Make a ranged spell attack against the target's AC. On a hit, you deal 6d6 spirit damage. On a critical hit, the target takes double damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage dealt by 2d6 and the Hit Points lost by 2 (or by 4 if you spend Hit Points from a vitality network).

A gout of flame sprays from your mouth. You deal 2d6 fire damage to creatures in the area with a basic Reflex save.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

To the target, your words are honey and your visage seems bathed in a dreamy haze. It must attempt a Will save, with a +4 circumstance bonus if you or your allies recently threatened it or used hostile actions against it.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you use hostile actions against the target, the spell ends. When the spell ends, the target doesn't necessarily realize it was charmed unless its friendship with you or the actions you convinced it to take clash with its expectations, meaning you could potentially convince the target to continue being your friend via mundane means.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and aware you tried to charm it.

Success The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of charm, unless it identifies the spell.

Failure The target's attitude becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v66R7FdOf11l94im]{Friendly} toward you. If it was Friendly, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.v44P3WUcU1j0115l]{Helpful}. It can't use hostile actions against you.

Critical Failure The target's attitude becomes Helpful toward you, and it can't use hostile actions against you.

Heightened (4th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations.

Heightened (8th) The duration lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations, and you can target up to 10 creatures.

You create an illusion that causes the target to appear more charming and genteel to observers. The spell accentuates the target's appearance, clothing, voice, and mannerisms according to what an observer finds appealing. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

The target gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.OX4fy22hQgUHDr0q]{Make an Impression} and lets the target add its level to Diplomacy checks if untrained. In addition, the target can attempt a Diplomacy check to Gather Information in only 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.hOp5ybOzyJlECU6B]{Spell Effect: Glow Up}

Heightened (+2) The bonus increases by 1, to a maximum of +4 at 7th rank.

You ask for the guidance of supernatural entities, granting the target a +1 status bonus to one attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check the target attempts before the duration ends. The target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling. If the target uses the bonus, the spell ends. Either way, the target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.3qHKBDF7lrHw8jFK]{Spell Effect: Guidance}

You snap your fingers and point at a target, which begins to smolder. Make a spell attack roll against the target's AC, dealing 2d4 fire damage on a hit. If the target is within your melee reach, you can choose to make a melee spell attack with the flame instead of a ranged spell attack, which increases all the spell's damage dice to d6s.

You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.cVVZXNbV0nElVOPZ]{Spell Effect: Light}

You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.cVVZXNbV0nElVOPZ]{Spell Effect: Light}

You vent heat toward a nearby target. You deal 2d6 fire damage to the target. On a failed save, the target's held equipment becomes hot to the touch for the next round. Unless the target drops its currently held equipment at the start of its turn, it takes @Damage[(3*@item.rank)[fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6. Increase the damage of touching affected objects by 3.

The simplest magic does your bidding. You can perform simple magical effects for as long as you Sustain the spell. Each time you Sustain the spell, you can choose one of four options.

  • Cook Cool, warm, or flavor 1 pound of nonliving material.
  • Lift Slowly lift an unattended object of light Bulk or less 1 foot off the ground.
  • Make Create a temporary object of negligible Bulk, made of congealed magical substance. The object looks crude and artificial and is extremely fragile-it can't be used as a tool, weapon, or locus or cost for a spell.
  • Tidy Color, clean, or soil an object of light Bulk or less. You can affect an object of 1 Bulk with 10 rounds of concentration, and a larger object at 1 minute per Bulk.

Prestidigitation can't deal damage or cause adverse conditions. Any actual change to an object (beyond what is noted above) persists only as long as you Sustain the spell.

While on their bonded celestial body, a nymph can sense approaching starships within a day's travel from the celestial body. This doesn't give them any information about the ship, its occupants, or its exact position. The nymph can send a message to the ship as if using a personal comm unit.

A nymph queen is intrinsically tied to a specific celestial body, such as a supergiant star for a heliad queen.

As long as the queen is healthy, her territory thrives, and she can attempt to [[/r 1d20+26 #Counteract]] counteract spells and rituals that alter its physical properties with a counteract rank of 7.

If a nymph queen is injured or unhealthy, however, her celestial body also weakens: asteroids wobble out of orbit, stars dim, and the gravity of black holes diminishes. Restoring the nymph queen returns her celestial body to its equilibrium.

60 feet. A heliad's eyes blaze like a sun. When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:34] save. The heliad can deactivate or activate this aura by using a single action, which has the concentrate trait.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} until the start of its next turn.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} until the start of its next turn.

Trigger The heliad queen dies

Effect The heliad's body explodes into brilliant, luminous flame. Each creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] takes @Damage[12d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:34|basic] save). Creatures that critically fail are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 minute.

The nymph queen magically superimposes her image on all technological items capable of displaying it, such as datapads or comm units, within planetary range. Alternatively, she can send this image to any comm address or addresses that she knows.

Each creature that can see the image must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:34] or have their attitude toward her improve by one step (two steps on a critical failure).

Affected creatures' attitudes revert to their previous level if the nymph queen uses a hostile action against them or their allies.

A nymph queen can traverse her celestial body near-instantaneously. A nymph queen can use Celestial Step to teleport to any area on or within her territory, vanishing into its surface and reemerging at her intended destination.

Nymph queens can transform between their original form, which looks much like a typical nymph of their kind, and any Small or Medium humanoid form, typically choosing a version of their natural form with the appearance of the chosen species.

A nymph queen can inspire a single intelligent creature by sending them a motivational video or text message. As long as the creature keeps the message saved and remains in good standing with the nymph queen, they gain a +1 status bonus to all Crafting, Performance, and Lore checks related to art or performance.

As a 1-action Interact action, the creature can play or view the message to bolster their spirits. Doing so allows the creature to attempt a [[/r 1d20+26 #Counteract]] counteract check against a single emotion effect affecting them.

When the nymph queen's message is used in this way, it becomes a mundane video or text afterward.

details.publicNotes:
Heliad queens live on the largest dwarf stars, mature stars, and supergiants. They take great pride in the luminosity of their stars, often bedecking themselves in clothing specially designed to refract light to show off their home's brilliance.

Nymphs are beautiful fey who appear as humanoids with elven features. They generally take female forms, but nymphs of all genders and presentations exist. While many nymphs live on planets and act as protectors of natural ponds, forests, caves, and cliffsides, other nymphs—often called space nymphs—are known for their association with celestial bodies. Like their planet-bound kin, space nymphs guard their bonded territories from exploitation or harm.

As admirers of the arts, nymphs are especially friendly toward creatives, and offering to depict a nymph in story, picture, or song is a sure way to win their favor—unless the nymph doesn't like the final result and lashes out at the artist for insulting them with a mediocre product. Vidcasting and livestreaming are popular pastimes among more sociable nymphs, whether to show off their remote environments or find audiences for performances. Some nymphs entice movie crews to film in their territories, with two provisos: first, that the production not damage the environment, and second, that the nymph receive a prominent role in the film.

Unless their territories are threatened, most space nymphs are friendly or indifferent to mortals, but they have long memories. Anyone visiting a space nymph's territory would be wise to research that nymph's grudges, past encounters with mortals, or any recent events in the area that might color the nymph's perspective of those traveling near their home.

A mortal who demonstrates the right attitude and abilities, such as by protecting a stellar body from destruction or creating an especially beautiful work of art, might draw a space nymph's attention. From time to time, space nymphs will offer their services as mentors to solarians or mystics, though they seldom seek pupils on their own. Space nymphs rarely enter into more than one such arrangement at a time and typically only make commitments that promise to last decades or centuries, so they're extremely selective in their choice of mentee.

description.value: A nyssholora senses all creatures that have telepathy, limited telepathy, or a similar ability, such as a mystic's bond, as an imprecise sense at the listed range. This sense works through walls and other obstacles.

Trigger A creature fails or critically fails a melee Strike against the nyssholora

Effect The nyssholora snarls at the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(3d6+6)[sonic]] damage.

Frequency once per round

Requirements A creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} in the nyssholora's jaws

Effect The nyssholora chomps down on the creature, dealing @Damage[(2d12+16)[piercing]] damage.

The nyssholora unleashes a blast of discordant sound that deal @Damage[18d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] (@Check[reflex|dc:38|basic] save). Objects and constructs in the area are treated as if they had half as much Hardness as usual. Creatures that critically fail their saving throw are also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round. The nyssholora can't use Discordant Bellow again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Discordant Bellow]]{1d4 rounds}.

The nyssholora Strides up to its Speed then makes a phasic blade Strike against each creature within reach. Each attack counts toward the nyssholora's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.
A nyssholora's phasic blade Strike treats any object or construct it hits as if the target had half as much Hardness as usual.

Medium, @Damage[(4d8+8)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 35

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

details.publicNotes:
The largest nyssholora ever recorded was Ulgaaza the Ageless, a mythical creature so old and so massive it was said to dwarf mountains. Today, any nyssholora that reaches epic proportions is called "Ulgaazan," though even these mighty beasts pale in comparison to their namesake.

Incredibly ferocious, nyssholoras are dinosaur-like creatures that number among the most dangerous and feared apex predators on the planet of Castrovel. These walking weapons brandish lethal biology: a massive maw lined with twin rows of razor-sharp teeth, scythe-shaped phasic blade forearms that vibrate at ultrasonic speeds, and a scourge-like tail capable of absorbing electricity from the surrounding atmosphere. Yet, nyssholoras are also cunning and perceptive—a frill of tentacles that crowns their head detect telepathic signals, making it impossible for the local formians and lashuntas to escape their notice. Their phasic blades can cut through walls and vehicles as easily as underbrush, ensuring prey have nowhere safe to hide.

description.value: With a rush of telekinetic power, you move a foe or something they carry. You can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove}, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.lOE4yjUnETTdaf2T]{Reposition}, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Trip} the target using a spell attack roll instead of an Athletics check.

The target can understand the meaning of a single language it is hearing or reading when you cast the spell. This doesn't let it understand codes, language couched in metaphor, and the like (subject to GM discretion). If the target can hear multiple languages and knows that, it can choose which language to understand; otherwise, choose one of the languages randomly.

Heightened (3rd) The target can also speak the language.

Heightened (4th) You can target up to 10 creatures, and targets can also speak the language.

You push into the target's mind and daze it with a mental jolt. The jolt deals 1d6 mental damage, with a basic Will save. If the target critically fails the save, it is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Heightened (+2) The damage increases by 1d6.

You create a simple illusory sound or vision. A sound adds the auditory trait to the spell and the sound can't include intelligible words or elaborate music. A vision adds the visual trait, can be no larger than a 5-foot cube, and is clearly crude and undetailed if viewed from within 15 feet. When you Cast or Sustain the Spell, you can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.GkmbTGfg8KcgynOA]{Create a Diversion} with the illusion, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to your Deception check. If the attempt fails against a creature, that creature disbelieves the figment.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.2Hg3a06gZCSnUgSA]{Spell Effect: Figment}

You overload a creature's mind with a glut of psychic information or divine judgments. The mental onslaught deals 2d6 mental damage. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 2d6.

In your mind's eye, you chart a path through the cosmos. If you're in Pact Worlds space, you immediately know which direction to travel in order to reach the Starstone on Absalom Station. If you're outside the Pact System, you know the direction to the nearest Drift beacon or the Starstone.

Heightened (7th) You can instead know the direction to a familiar location, such as a world or way station you've visited.

You grace the target's mind, boosting its mental defenses and healing its wounds. The target regains 1d10+4 Hit Points when you Cast the Spell and gains a +2 status bonus to saves against mental effects for the duration.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spell-effects.Item.nkk4O5fyzrC0057i]{Spell Effect: Soothe}

You create a floating, magical hand, either invisible or ghostlike, that grasps the target object and levitates it slowly up to 20 feet in any direction. When you Sustain the spell, you can move the object an additional 20 feet. If the object is in the air when the spell ends, the object falls.

Heightened (3rd) You can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.

Heightened (5th) The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.

Heightened (7th) The range increases to 60 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less.

If you hit, you deal 2d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage—as appropriate for the object you hurled. No specific traits or magic properties of the hurled item affect the attack or the damage.

Success You deal full damage.
