A sapient purple orb always ignores @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} caused by fog, smoke, and mist.

Frequency once per minute

Effect The sapient purple orb emits a red mist (as the spell) centered on themself. Sapient orbs can choose to emit transparent mist, granting no concealment. Allies and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} creatures in the mist gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws. Additionally, any enemy that's in the mist when it appears, that enters the mist, or that ends its turn in the mist must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save unless it's fascinated.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature becomes fascinated by the mist.

Critical Failure The creature becomes fascinated by the mist and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Frequency once per minute

Effect The sapient purple orb momentarily increases their luminance to an aggressively bright spectrum. Each other creature within 20 feet that can see the sapient purple orb must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:21] saving throw or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. On a critical failure, the target instead becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round, then dazzled for [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
Purple orbs are curious, social beings who unfailingly attempt to make contact with any life-form they encounter, though these attempts are frequently inept. Few other creatures understand their ancient language, so purple orbs instead use magic, telepathy, minor illusions, and flashes of light and flowing mist from their 3-foot-diameter luminous bodies.

Purple orbs are eager for cooperation and solidarity with their chosen friends and family, whether those are other sapient orbs or completely different beings, making it relatively easy to befriend them—or deceive them. Purple orbs are devout supporters and defenders of their companions, flinging themselves bodily into the fray if a friend is in danger. They become a deep dark purple as they grow more emotional in the heat of conflict (violent or otherwise), though their emotions rarely grow so intense that they're willing to take another being's life. That purple orbs seem to value their own lives less than those of their friends might be among the reasons that the number of sapient purple orbs in the universe seems to be dwindling.

These enigmatic, orb-shaped beings seem to have no home world, organized society, traditions, or religion. Sightings of the orbs are sporadic—they briefly appear in large numbers, then vanish for a year or more, only to reappear in an entirely different region of space for a few months. Scholars and friends of the orbs have noticed that each time the orbs appear, their numbers have diminished.

Sapient orbs occasionally form permanent bonds, merging three to six orbs (always of the same type, as far as anyone knows) into a cluster called a luminant. Only sapient orbs at advanced stages of their life cycle can form such clusters, and the merge is permanent and irrevocable. Luminants are highly intelligent but far more reclusive than other sapient orbs, making it difficult to discern their motivations or purpose in sapient orb society.

description.value:
Requirements The spined renkroda's spines are bristled

Trigger The spined renkroda would be hit by a ranged Strike, but the damage hasn't been rolled yet

Effect The spined renkroda twists its body, deflecting part of the attack with its spines, and gains resistance 10 to the triggering damage. Its spines are no longer bristled.

The spined renkroda bristles its spines and adopts a defensive posture, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until it uses a move action, uses Spine Deflection, or makes any Strike other than a spine Strike. Any creature that attempts a melee attack against the spined renkroda takes @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage, regardless of whether the attack hits.
The spined renkroda makes three ranged spine Strikes.

The spined renkroda bellows ferociously. Each non-renkroda creature within 60 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:32] save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

details.publicNotes:
Thickly built and burly, spined renkrodas live on open plains, mesas, and plateaus. Unlike most renkrodas, their frill is a deadly weapon, lined with hundreds of foot-long spines that serve as an excellent defense—and a deadly weapon.

Renkrodas are a family of large dinosaur-like predators native to the planet Castrovel. They've spread beyond the jungle planet's boundaries thanks to smugglers, poachers, and ultra-wealthy big-game hunters. Bipedal and strong with long necks and decorative spines, renkrodas are best known for their terrifying roars, which they bellow frequently as a form of long-range communication and to scare away other predators and scavengers. Over a dozen species of renkrodas exist, each specially adapted to their environment or ecological niche.

description.value: The plasma ooze can sense all non-mindless creatures within the listed range.

100 feet. When an item with the tech trait is activated inside the aura, it must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save or become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} on a critical failure).

A tech creature that starts its turn within the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:35] save. If it fails, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} on a critical failure).

An item or creature that succeeds at this save becomes immune to this aura for 1 hour.
Large and smaller creatures and all unattended objects within 100 feet must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:36] save or be pulled 15 feet toward the plasma ooze (30 feet on a critical failure).

Frequency once per day

Effect each creature and wielded object with the tech trait within 100 feet of the plasma ooze must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save. For each target that fails this save, the ooze regains @Damage[15[healing]] Hit Points and the object loses half of its remaining charges (if battery-powered).

A creature damaged by the ooze's pseudopod must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:36] save or be @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} on a critical failure). A creature with the tech trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

details.publicNotes:
Plasma oozes pose a significant menace to seasoned spacefarers within the Pact Worlds. These entities materialize as expansive networks of crackling electromagnetic energy, assuming a captivating spherical form pulsating with luminous hues of purple and blue, with searing white-hot bursts scattered throughout.

These oozes primarily lurk near the surface of stars but are also drawn to potent electromagnetic phenomena elsewhere in the Universe. The electromagnetic fields exuded by the ooze present dangers in space, potentially even incapacitating shielded starships. Armed scions connected to the Plane of Fire, known as the Sunkillers, claim to actively hunt these enigmatic beings, armed with specialized gear and sonic weaponry.

Oozes of many types lurk in dark and abandoned places across the galaxy, emerging only to satiate their appetites and threaten adventurers, explorers, and hapless workers alike.

description.value: An electrovore can sense electricity, including the electrical impulses found in most living creatures' nervous systems, as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Effect The electrovore unleashes a shock wave that deals @Damage[2d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:15|options:inflicts:glitching]). It can't use Electrical Discharge again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Electrical Discharge]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target takes half damage. Items in its possession with the tech trait becomes glitching 1. If the target has the tech trait, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1}.

Failure As success, but full damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2}.

Critical Failure As success, but double damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3}.

When an electrovore scores a critical hit with a tail Strike against a living creature wielding or wearing tech gear or a creature with the tech trait, it siphons off a portion of the target's electrical energy. This restores @Damage[5[healing]] Hit Points to the electrovore immediately, plus 1 Hit Point per minute for the next 10 minutes; the additional healing doesn't increase if the electrovore siphons multiple times.

details.publicNotes:
Electrovores consume electricity as their sole source of sustenance. On their native planet, they fed on a species of three-headed electric eels, but volcanic activity wiped out their food source, sending electrovores to the brink of extinction. Electrovores found their salvation when explorers landed on their planet. The explorer's equipment and vessels had ample amounts of the electricity that the electrovores require. They stowed away on the vessels and soon spread throughout the Pact Worlds, causing damage and mayhem wherever they appeared.

Nothing terrifies a team of engineers more than finding an electrovore chewing on wires in a remote part of their starship. Where there's one electrovore, a dozen more likely await discovery. These 3-foot-tall reptilian creatures, sometimes called "conduit rats," breed rapidly and create such a disturbance in power sources that, over the course of days or weeks, a starship or factory might completely shut down from lack of power. There are many lifeless star vessels adrift in space because an electrovore infestation wasn't stopped in time.

Removing an infestation is no easy task, as electrovores are single-minded and cunning. The creatures are adept at finding places to hide, stalking their preferred energy sources and finding the correct moment to strike. Packs of electrovores even coordinate feedings in different areas at the same time to make it harder to stop them.

When confronted, electrovores rarely fight to the death, preferring to eat some electricity and retreat, hide, and heal, then return for another feast.

description.value: @Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]
A creature critically hit by a noquasar spark's graviton flare is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

The noquasar spark unleashes plasma in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] or @Template[type:line|distance:30]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[2d4[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic] save). A creature that critically fails its save also takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,electricity]|options:area-damage] damage and is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. The noquasar spark can't use Ionized Plasma Blast again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ionized Plasma Blast]]{1d4 rounds}.

details.publicNotes:
A noquasar spark appears as a glimmer of intensely bright light with an agonized face.

When a solarian perishes while out of sync with the cycle of stars, their soul might become entangled with their solar mote. Extreme cosmic forces act on the soul as it attempts to reach the River of Souls, stretching it infinitely thin for all of eternity.

description.value:
Before the hardlight havoc begins its turn in combat, it gains one of the following error states at random, losing any previous error state.

Change Character The hardlight havoc regains @Damage[2d8[healing]|options:area-damage] Hit Points.

Pixelation The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from all creatures.

Power Leak The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. At the end of its turn, it deals @Damage[4d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage to creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save).

Surge The hardlight havoc is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} until the end of its turn and can use the extra action to Stride or Strike.

A hardlight life-form can't travel more than the indicated number of feet from its projector. A hardlight life-form reduced to 0 HP vanishes, though its projector reconstructs it in [[/gmr 1d4 #hours]]{1d4 hours}. The only way to permanently destroy a hardlight life-form is to find its projector and either repair or destroy it.

The hardlight havoc teleports up to 40 feet. If the destination is within melee reach of an enemy, it can make a melee Strike against that enemy. If it teleports to a square without a surface to stand on, it falls after attempting a Strike and takes no damage from falling.

details.publicNotes:
This hardlight life-form is unstable, displaying stuck frames and erratic movements.

Unlike normal holograms, hardlight life-forms are tangible, living entities.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

This high-tech crossbow uses a series of pistons to propel bolts along its barrel. These quiet-firing semiautomatic weapons are popular in high-density settlements where gunfire might cause a panic.

A hacking toolkit is required for Computers checks to Hack computers. You can use a hacking toolkit to access a computer without using a user interface, but this requires physical contact with the computer or contact via an infosphere or a similar linked network.

You need an infiltrator's toolkit to @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick Locks} or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.cYdz2grcOcRt4jk6]{Disable Devices} (of some types) using the Thievery skill.
The robot comes packaged with photosensitive reflectors on its exterior panels. It can [[/act hide]] even without cover or concealment.

The infiltrator robot fires a bomb within 30 feet that disperses sleeping gas in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.

Failure The creature falls @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If it's still unconscious after 1 minute, it wakes up automatically.

Critical Failure The creature falls unconscious. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it wakes up automatically.

details.publicNotes:
Not a front-line combatant, an infiltrator robot's primary tactic is to quietly enter a location, use its sleeping gas bombs to disable those guarding its target, and collect its payload. The leadership of the Pact Worlds found that the snoop-class infiltrator robot could have other, darker tasks during the Silent War: its reactive camouflage could help it transport deadly explosives to sabotage ships, shipyards, and other military installations.

Brought to the forefront during the war against the Veskarium, and further perfected by various corporate interests within the Pact Worlds, infiltrator robots are known for being able to get into places where no one else could. Generally small and agile, infiltrator robots sneak into enemy territory and either download precious data or record incriminating evidence.

description.value: This is a precise sense against creatures under an emotion effect or in the grips of overwhelming emotions (at the GM's discretion). Against other creatures, this is an imprecise sense. Creatures immune to emotion or mental effects or under the effects of a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.b515AZlB0sridKSq]{Calm} spell are undetectable by emotionsense.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[1d6[mental]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 As stage 2, plus @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (1 round)

A non-flying creature damaged by the dread empathnid's psychic web Strike or psychic burst is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, stuck to the ground or nearest adjacent surface until it [[/act escape dc=18]]{Escapes}. A flying creature must instead succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:18] save or fall 20 feet (or 40 feet on a critical failure).

details.publicNotes:
Dread empathnids are deadly hunters native to jungle planets. They thrive on populated worlds, and their preferred prey are intelligent humanoids. Drawn to negative emotions, dread empathnids absorb the terror of their dying victims and infuse their webbing with these horrific experiences.

Empathnids have tremendous empathy for other creatures. The fact that the 8-inch spidery creatures come in a variety of colors and have a lifesaving venom likely explains why they're frequently used as companions or pets across the Pact Worlds.

description.value: An akata can secrete resin that encases it safely in a noqual cocoon. The cocoon has Hardness 9, 40 HP, and a BT of 18. It has resistance 5 to damage from magical sources. While ensconced within this cocoon, the akata can't be harmed, doesn't need to eat or drink, and gains @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.sebk9XseMCRkDqRg]{Lifesense} 30 feet. During its hibernation, the akata remains dormant until exposed to extreme heat or it senses a living creature. It can then break free of its cocoon within [[/gmr 1d4 #minutes]]{1d4 minutes}.

Akatas are immune to auditory effects, automatically critically fail Perception checks that require it to hear, and take a –2 status penalty to Perception checks (but not initiative rolls) that involve sound but also rely on its other senses.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NoBreath]

Salt water attacks an akata's cells and acts as a potent acid. If the akata is fully immersed in salt water, it takes @Damage[4d6[acid]] damage from the water immediately and at the end of each of its turns as long as it remains immersed. Its weakness to salt water applies to this damage.

An akata implants microscopic larvae into any creature it bites, but only Small or Medium humanoids make suitable hosts; all other creatures are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} at Stage 2 and further instead.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect 1 (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2 (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} (1 day)

Stage 5 as stage 4 (1 day)

Stage 6 dead and corpse rises as a void zombie in [[/gmr 2d4 #hours]]{2d4 hours}

details.publicNotes:
Few creatures inspire more dread in isolated explorers and lonely space stations than the breathless, leonine akatas, which infest creatures with parasites to transform them into ravenous void zombies.

An akata's toothy maw, glowing yellow eyes, and lashing twin tails mark it as a silent predator. Their slinking, hairless blue flesh spreads out into a mane of rubbery tentacles. Despite lacking ears, these quadrupeds stalk prey with an enhanced sight and sense of smell. Akatas typically stand at about 3-1/2 feet tall and weigh 400 pounds.

In times of scarcity, akatas ooze noqual from their pores and spin pale green crystalline cocoons capable of holding them in stasis for centuries. They can even use their cocoons to traverse space and find new hosts for their larvae. When a host dies from this infestation, known as void death, the strongest offspring animates the cadaver as a void zombie. Fiercely territorial, they hunt in colonies that are capable of bringing a settlement to its knees within weeks.

description.value: A whiskered renkroda can sense nearby creatures through vibration and air or water movement as an imprecise sense at the listed range.

Trigger A creature ends its movement within the whiskered renkroda's reach

Effect The whiskered renkroda circles its prey, Striding up to its Speed. The renkroda must move to a location where the triggering creature is within its reach.
The whiskered renkroda ignores any difficult terrain caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.

The whiskered renkroda bellows ferociously. Each non-renkroda creature within 60 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

The whiskered renkroda charges forward and slams into its prey. It Strides twice. If the whiskered renkroda ends its movement with an enemy within its reach, it makes a body Strike against that enemy. On a hit, the enemy is knocked @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

details.publicNotes:
The smallest of all renkrodas, whiskered renkrodas have adapted to move easily through dense jungles and forests and blend in amid the vibrant green flora. Whiskered renkrodas have whisker-like antennae growing from their chins that detect vibrations, enabling them to detect prey hiding amid the dense foliage. When they spot their prey, renkrodas charge into the fray and slam into their prey with enough force to topple trees or vehicles. Occasionally, whiskered renkrodas live and hunt in packs, to better protect themselves from larger predators.

Renkrodas are a family of large dinosaur-like predators native to the planet Castrovel. They've spread beyond the jungle planet's boundaries thanks to smugglers, poachers, and ultra-wealthy big-game hunters. Bipedal and strong with long necks and decorative spines, renkrodas are best known for their terrifying roars, which they bellow frequently as a form of long-range communication and to scare away other predators and scavengers. Over a dozen species of renkrodas exist, each specially adapted to their environment or ecological niche.

description.value: A painsaw functions in all ways like a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.eUZcCSon8XlQzOqR]{Painglaive} but doesn't function with normal batteries.

A single celebrant heart functions as a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.equipment.Item.5PS5ESGpnQfJNOdg]{tactical battery} when used with a painsaw; it recharges if the celebrant spends 10 minutes in the presence of an @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.alien-core-bestiary.Actor.vEA1FAanv0l7Yz4Z]{Elindrian Queen}.

120 feet

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Telepathy]

Elindrians can communicate nearly instantaneously via a primal bond similar to a formian hive mind. While within telepathic range of at least one other elindrian with husk mind, an elindrian celebrant gains a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls, Perception checks, and saving throws against mental effects. If one elindrian is aware of a combatant, all members of the husk mind within range are aware of it.

The elindrian's husk mind is integrated with their scent; for purposes of detecting other elindrians, formians, undead, or corpses, their scent is a precise sense, rather than imprecise.

An elindrian always knows the precise distance and direction to the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.alien-core-bestiary.Actor.BFtNnevWKUCnocyn]{Terror of Ukulam}, so long as they're on the same plane of existence.

A celebrant's painsaw is powered by the heart of another celebrant. If a celebrant is within 30 feet of a painsaw that contains their heart when the painsaw Strikes a creature, the celebrant regains a number of Hit Points equal to their level (typically 4). It's taboo for a celebrant to wield a painsaw made from their own heart or a heart from any celebrant whose death they didn't witness (or cause) themself. A painsaw otherwise functions in all ways like a painglaive but doesn't function with normal batteries. A single celebrant heart functions as a tactical battery when used with a painsaw; it recharges if the celebrant spends 10 minutes in the presence of an elindrian queen.

details.publicNotes:
These elindrian warriors relish violence. Their only motivation seems to be spreading pain, which they themselves are immune to; their nerve endings developed in such a way that they can't feel pain at all, experiencing it in some way that causes them little to no suffering. As long as there's an elindrian queen nearby to revive them, celebrants spend most of their time battling one another, honing their capability to do violence until a better target comes along.

The people known as elindrians vanished eons ago into desolate husk-hives beneath the surface of the planet Castrovel. Until recently, they were creatures of legend, existing only in the most obscure formian histories. Now, scholars on Castrovel desperately search for additional records about elindrians, their origins, and—most importantly—the truth about their connection to the terrifying ooze-like creature that recently descended from their moon, Elindrae. One theory is that elindrians share some primal bond with the ooze-thing called the Terror of Ukulam, and perhaps both originate from Elindrae—other theories claim that elindrians created the ooze in the first place or that they're distant cousins to formians, corrupted by the Terror long ago.

description.value: Diaspora worms communicate by shaping and reading electromagnetic fields. This functions like telepathy with creatures that share this ability.

Diaspora worms sense their surroundings by reading electromagnetic fields, including disruptions caused by creatures or objects. This is a precise sense within the listed range. Intense fields, such as areas emitting radiation or auras of electricity, make creatures within them @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} to the diaspora worm.

Diaspora worms that take electricity damage must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The worm is unaffected.

Success The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The worm is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The worm is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.

Requirements The diaspora worm is in water

Effect The worm alters the structure of water within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20], forming light-absorbing nanotubes. This field moves with the worm and lasts for 1 minute. The emanation deactivates non-magical light sources and automatically attempts to counteract magical light (counteract rank 9, counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+38 #Counteract]]{+38}). This field also provides cover for creatures inside of it from those outside, and vice versa.

The diaspora worm sage stirs up a 40-foot-tall cylinder of water with a @Template[burst|distance:20]{20-foot radius} within 300 feet. If the area isn't already filled with water, this ability fails. The vortex buffets all creatures, dealing @Damage[5d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that fail the save are pulled 5 feet toward the center of the vortex (or 10 feet on a critical failure).

The diaspora worm sage emits a concentrated wave of electromagnetism in a @Template[type:cone|distance:50]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:35|options:area-effect,inflicts:glitching,inflicts:stunned,inflicts:paralyzed] save. A creature with the tech trait uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes immune to Electromagnetic Surge for 1 hour.

Success The creature's items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}, and its items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 2} for 1 minute. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

Critical Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round, and its items with the tech trait become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching 3} for 10 minutes. A creature with the tech trait also gains this glitching condition.

Large, @Damage[(6d10+6)[bludgeoning]], Rupture 32

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.SwallowWhole]

A creature hit by the diaspora worm's tongue becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 37 that deals at least 32 slashing damage. This prevents the worm from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 day.

Requirements The diaspora worm has @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature with its tongue

Effect The diaspora worm attempts to [[/act reposition]] the grabbed creature toward the worm. If the grabbed creature is brought to within 10 feet or less of the worm, the worm can attempt to Swallow Whole the creature.

details.publicNotes:
Diaspora worms that survive for centuries grow significantly in size, power, and intelligence. They mostly protect their ancestral home, preserving bioelectric coral records and directing the actions of younger worms.

"Space worm" is a nonscientific term for a xenobiologically complex clade of invertebrate animals and beasts that make their home in the vastness of space. Some, like the diaspora and dust worms, show signs of intelligence and are directly related to each other. Others, like the surnoch, evolved separately, with their origins completely unknown to science.

description.value:
Upgrades: 1

These dependable, mass-produced rifles shoot concentrated beams of energy out of square-shaped barrels. The universal standard Pact Worlds model is highly modifiable and designed to function with a variety of accessories and upgrades, with wielders adapting the laser rifle to their preferences and even kit-bashing it into a nearly unrecognizable weapon.

Trigger A creature within 30 feet that the nihili can see starts its turn

Effect The target must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save. If it fails, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} for 1 minute as it struggles to catch its breath.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

The nihili stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet and imparts vivid visions of their prior death by decompression. A creature affected by this gaze must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. If it fails and hasn't already been @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} by Decompressing Glance or this ability, it becomes fatigued. If the creature is already fatigued, a failed save causes the creature to begin suffocating until the end of the nihili's next turn. The creature remains conscious and can take actions on its turn, such as casting a spell or searching for a source of air. The creature can hold its breath as normal.

A creature can attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save at the end of its turn to break free from the experience being projected into its mind; on a success, the effect of Decompressing Gaze ends. The nihili can Sustain Decompressing Gaze on their turn or end this effect as a free action. The targeted creature's access to air is restored when the nihili targets a new creature with their Decompressing Gaze or the creature successfully saves against the effects. This otherwise follows the rules for suffocation.

The nihili generates a field of gravity that pulls all creatures and unsecured objects toward it. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is pulled 5 feet toward the nihili.

Failure The creature is pulled 15 feet toward the nihili.

Critical Failure The creature is pulled 30 feet toward the nihili.

details.publicNotes:
Dying from exposure in space is horrific, and many starship crews obsessively monitor their hull and armor integrity to avoid such a fate. It can also lead to the creation of the foul undead known as nihilis. A nihili arises after an individual in vacuum dies of asphyxiation while all gases exit their body and their lungs collapse from attempts to hold their breath. Legends say the first person to die in such a terrible manner had a vision of the goddess of undeath, Urgathoa, who bestowed upon them a single kiss, transforming them into the very first nihili. The same legends go on to claim that this nihili still floats aimlessly through space, waiting to cling to an unsuspecting starship.

The physical appearance of a given nihili varies. Most appear as zombie-like versions of their respective ancestries, but with gruesome stretch marks and pockets of bloat caused by escaping gases during their death. A nihili maintains their prior intellect, subverted entirely to inflicting the same suffering on the living that they endured. By focusing their gaze on a target, a nihili can impart to that creature the memories of their own demise, potentially suffocating them.

description.value: The war machine can detect all creatures and objects within 100 feet of itself, treating hidden, invisible, and unobserved creatures as @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

A remote war machine maintains a constant data link with its pilot, using its pilot's languages and skill modifiers for any unlisted skill. In addition, the pilot can speak through the data link using the remote war machine as a mouthpiece.

Trigger The war machine would be reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The war machine self-destructs, dealing @Damage[16d6[electricity]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:36|basic|options:area-effect] save) to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:60]{60-foot radius}. For 1 minute after this ability is used, any creature that touches the war machine's remains is exposed to incredible radiation sickness.

Trigger A creature within 60 feet of the war machine Casts a Spell

Effect The war machine fires a focused lightning beam targeting only the triggering creature. If the creature fails its save, the spell becomes weakened. If the weakened spell requires a saving throw, the target uses the outcome one degree of success better than the result of its roll. If the weakened spell requires a spell attack, that attack roll takes a –4 circumstance penalty.

60 feet. Non-allied comm units and other computers don't function within the remote war machine's aura unless their operator succeeds at a @Check[computers|against:stealth|rollerRole:target] check against the war machine's Stealth DC as an Interact action.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

1 to 3

The remote war machine's tentacles surround it in defensive patterns. For 1 minute, it gains a number of reactions equal to the number of actions spent on Swirling Shield. These extra reactions can be used only for Psionic Flare or Reactive Strike.
The war machine makes a tentacle Strike against up to four creatures in reach, rolling the attack and damage roll only once for all targets.
The war machine becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} for 1 minute or until it attempts to make a Strike.

details.publicNotes:
Only one member of the Stewards has ever encountered the biotechnical horror known as code name "moonflower" and lived to tell the tale—and only after they emerged from a months-long coma. According to her story, a single moonflower war machine was responsible for the massacre of Liavaran moon S-117, which used to house an entire Stewards deep space telescope array and planetary defense battery.

The incursion began with a series of probes detected by anacites on Aballon. Strange machines with blazing sensors and articulated tentacles appeared out of nowhere, ripping through infrastructure seemingly at random and collecting intelligence all around Pact Worlds space. Panic spread fast, but since the machines always vanished without a trace, or exploded when captured, the sightings couldn't be confirmed or identified. Rumors began to circulate that the machines were a form of biotech never encountered before. Soon the Stewards became formally involved, shuttering the incidents behind the adamantine partition of top security clearance.

In Sarenith of 325 ag, after dozens of military operations failed to engage the elusive probes, a wing of fighters dispatched from the Idari finally got lucky and disabled one of the machines before it could self-destruct. Coaxed into giving up some of its secrets by top Pact Worlds scientists, the biotech vessel became the prototype for the "home grown" remote war machines that are currently being tested for use in combat. The replicas aren't alive but are built using robotic construction templates since the core of the original biotech design has proven too complicated to reverse engineer with Pact technology.

Remote war machines are proxies that can be controlled from a great distance. The original biotech war machines connect to the pilot's mind through a psychic link or unknown radio frequency. Robotic remote war machines connect to a computer via data link, allowing the pilot to control their proxy using a keyboard, virtual reality rig, or even a vidgame controller.

description.value: Psychic fungi are connected to a network of psychic filaments bonding all psychic fungi from the same colony. This network grants the elder the ability to detect creatures capable of thought within 30 feet of a bonded psychic fungus up to 1,000 feet away as an imprecise sense.

Trigger The elder is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The elder releases a cloud of spores in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] centered on a corner of its own space that lasts for 1 round. Each creature that's in the cloud or enters it is exposed to befuddling spores. If this reaction was triggered by damage from a melee attack, the triggering creature uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its roll for its Fortitude save (two degrees worse if it was a jaws Strike or other Strike with the creature's mouth).

Plants and fungi are immune

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:21]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} (1 round)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4} (1 round)

The elder psychic fungus consumes the fluids and psychic essences from the brain of a creature within 10 feet that has been dead for no more than 1 minute. The elder psychic fungus regains the use of their highest-ranked spent innate spell. The creature is considered to have died due to a death effect.

items.name: Lore (all subcategories of all creatures trained in Lore skills within 1 mile)

description.value: Salt water quickly attacks a larval akata's cells as a strong acid. If the void zombie is fully immersed in salt water, it takes @Damage[4d6[acid]] damage from the water immediately and at the end of each of its turns as long as it remains immersed. Its weakness to salt water applies to this damage. On any round in which the void zombie takes damage due to its salt water weakness, the larval akata withdraws to hide within the host's body and renders the void zombie @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}.
@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.NegativeHealing]

Requirements The void zombie's previous action was a successful feeding tendril Strike against a living creature

Effect The void zombie drains blood from the required creature using its feeding tendril. The void zombie regains @Damage[4[healing]] Hit Points, and the creature is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} until it receives healing of any kind or amount.

details.publicNotes:
When an akata bites a humanoid, it infests them with microscopic, parasitic larvae. Those who perish from this parasitization, a condition known as void death, rise again as void zombies. While they resemble a typical rotting corpse, an elongated bluish-gray feeding tendril extends from their shattered lower jaw. When the immature akata within the body senses potential prey, this tendril often undulates and writhes with nauseating eagerness.

Once the host body dies, the larvae feed on each other until the strongest makes its way to the victim's brain. After several hours of rapid gestation, it attaches to the brain and infuses it with a mote of necromantic energy. This process leaves the larva ravenous, driving it to consume the blood of living creatures. Void zombies tend to last several weeks as the larva grows, but eventually its host compels it to seek out a safe, secluded place where it can disgorge the larva for one last surge of growth before the host body goes inert. Several hours later, a fully formed akata emerges from the frothing puddle, typically consuming the corpse as its first meal.

In general, akatas leave void zombies alone, and the larval parasites attempt to spread out to avoid competing for food. In a confined and undisturbed space, void zombies stand completely still to conserve energy. The juvenile akatas within become dormant and feed on the host body's tissue until they either reach maturity or sense a viable food source. Given the void zombie's relatively short existence, those who stumble upon a previous attack might find the void zombies already devoured or discarded and an eerie colony of silent, glassy cocoons full of ravenous hibernating akatas instead.

Void zombies tend to ignore other undead, but they become inexplicably aggressive when confronted with other creatures that are infested by parasites or otherwise controlled or influenced by external forces. Many have tried experimenting with void zombie behavior by forcing them to confront, for example, a rendworm host or a creature under the thrall of a spellcaster. Most such experiments end inconclusively at best, and many have ended in disaster.

description.value: The pachinyam shakes it thick fur and releases a cloud of dust and dander in a @Template[type:burst|distance:5] radius centered on a corner of one of its squares that functions as a mist spell. This dust cloud blocks electronic communication to and from anyone inside the cloud. Any item with the tech trait becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{glitching 1} if it's inside the dust cloud. The pachinyam can't use this ability again until they take a dust bath as a 3-action activity with the move trait.

details.publicNotes:
A single pachinyam isn't much of a threat but can be a nuisance around those who rely on technology. Some pachinyam owners take extra time to play with their pets after their daily dust baths, especially before entering a starship or other facility where an unexpected cloud of dust could have catastrophic consequences.

These dusty, long-eared fluffy burrowing rodents are native to Daimalko and emerged alongside the colossi during the Awakening 200 years ago. They were initially heralded as potential pets and affectionately referred to as "dust bunnies," but the survivors of the Awakening soon found the species were as dangerous as the colossi rampaging across the planet.

While one or two pachinyams behave as calm herbivores, they reproduce rapidly. When the pachinyam population reaches a tipping point, they become vicious predators. Their dust's ability to disrupt both visual and telecommunications makes it challenging for settlements to warn or organize against them. Outside civilization, the pachinyam population is kept in check by their swarms' instinctually drive to be near colossi despite the obvious danger. Some also hypothesize that the scent of a pachinyam swarm's dust cloud attracts hungry colossi.

description.value:
Requirements The skreesire is in rocky terrain

Effect Until the next time they act, the skreesire resembles rocky terrain. They have an automatic result of 29 on @Check[deception] checks and DCs to pass as rocky terrain.

Effect A target within 30 feet hears a telepathic manifestation of the skreesire's will and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save.

Failure The creature must use the first action of its next turn to Stride toward the skreesire.

Critical Failure The creature must use all its actions on its next turn to Stride toward the skreesire; when it's adjacent to the skreesire, it drops @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} and ends its turn.

languages.details: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

details.publicNotes:
Skreesires are reptilian monstrosities with a formidable will, razor-sharp teeth, and nonfunctional wings atop a mass of ambulatory tentacles.

Skreesires, and their offspring skreelings, are vicious predators who skulk and prowl in the crags and caverns of the cosmos.
